englishbook

CLOSED UNIVERSE:
QUANTUM-INFORMATIONAL BALANCE OF MATTER, MASS, AND EMERGENT TIME

The Physical Concept of a Stationary de Sitter Resonator
and Six-Dimensional Geometrodynamics Without Spatial Expansion

Author: Kupervasser Oleg

Sami Shamoon College of Engineering, Ariel University

2026

ABSTRACT

The work is dedicated to resolving the paradoxes of quantum gravity, which in this model cannot be treated as a local model independent of the entire Universe. The study demonstrates that if the Universe is viewed from the perspective of an observer who is motionless relative to the background, the model of an expanding Universe transforms into a model of a closed, bounded, unitary de Sitter Universe. This allows for the implementation of Occam’s razor and yields a resolution to many paradoxes in quantum gravity, observational astronomy, and astrobiology, while barely stepping beyond the boundaries of modern existing physics (doing so minimally, by introducing two hidden time axes). This is achieved not by introducing new particles, physical forces, or models, but by carefully accounting for the role of the observer and the emergent properties of the Universe’s thermodynamics within the framework of existing physics. A new logarithmic medium scale of the Universe is introduced between the current Hubble horizon and the Planck length (50 mkm), at which the emergence of new physics is expected.

This work formulates a complete, mathematically self-consistent physical theory of the Universe. It describes the cosmos as a closed, isolated quantum resonator of fixed volume, whose evolution is entirely free from the hypotheses of cosmic inflation and the singular Big Bang. The fundamental thesis of the work postulates that the fabric of space is geometrically motionless, while the observed expansion is a mechanical parting of baryonic matter, in which the classical GR scale factor describes the dynamic downscaling of the vacuum time rate. Any physical phenomena—from the mass spectrum of elementary particles to the structure of galaxies and biosphere parameters—are derived as natural boundary imprints and geometric properties of this single resonator (Daniel Harlow Mykhaylo Usatyuk Ying Zhao, Quantum mechanics and observers for gravity in a closed universe, 2025).

Within the developed framework of canonical Wheeler–DeWitt quantization and Radju’s loopless holography, the Standard Model mass spectrum emerges as discrete eigenvalues of quantum operators determined by the topology of a compact S3 sphere. Protection of the vacuum from ultraviolet divergences and non-physical Ostrogradsky ghosts is provided by a built-in non-polynomial Borisova Palatini-damper on Zachari–McKintosh curvature invariants, alongside destructive interference of matter and antimatter loops in a 12-sector phase space of Z12 vacuums. At the deep scale, the initial six-dimensional Clifford pro-space Cℓ3,3(ℝ) under the action of the Lie group G2(2) completely excludes external particle insertion, spontaneously generating three generations of fermions from minimal light ideals of zero divisors in split-octonion algebra.

The Dark Sector of cosmology is entirely devoid of independent constants or hypothetical hidden particles: dark energy and dark matter emerge as a geometric counterweight to the radial retreat of baryons to preserve the total Noether invariant mass of the closed system (1061 Planck masses). At the astrophysical scale, the non-linear elasticity of the vacuum and standing gravitational waves of compact rotators form coherent Wheeler–Misner geons, shaping observed galactic halos and predetermining the Tully–Fisher law. Within the framework of non-equilibrium thermodynamics and Gyarmati’s principle of maximum entropy production, a strong departure from equilibrium in the early era crystallizes GR anomalies into Bénard cells, explaining the nature of JWST "Little Red Dots" through metastable Jacobi–Darwin reactors stabilized by a one-way GR valve.

At the macroscopic boundary Ldecog ~RH ⋅ ℓPl ≈ 50μm, due to the complete geometric dephasing of the wave function, hidden time axes undergo BRST-burning under the influence of the IR background of dark energy. This fundamental CKN threshold anthropically determines the spatial scales of biological cells, the resonant torsional shedding of intra-systemic chaos into a vacuum thermostat ("Solaris Ocean"), and the quantum-electrodynamical noise of Rydberg energy, tightly fixing the temperature interval for the stability of the liquid phase of water (T ≈ 300K). The model proposes rigorous, experimentally verifiable protocols for laboratory detection of the unidirectional quantum ratchet of the Cartan axial current in the optical clocks of a laser comparator. It also provides quantum-optical tests for the exit of correlations beyond the Tsirelson bound up to the Popescu–Rohrlich limit, revealing the quantum non-locality of EPR experiments as a pure kinematic illusion of a 4D projection during local signal transit along hidden time axes.

The foundational concepts of this monograph—specifically, the crucial role of the local observer, the stabilization of boundaries under external noise, and the hydrodynamic behavior of non-equilibrium fields — are a direct, multi-scale extension of the author’s previous peer-reviewed research. Within the framework of New Cybernetics, it was mathematically demonstrated that fundamental paradoxes of time asymmetry and quantum measurement are resolved exclusively by accounting for the small interaction (entanglement) between a bounded environment (or observer) and an observed matter. Scaling this holographic principle to the cosmos naturally maps the FLRW metric into a stationary de Sitter box, where cosmic "expansion" emerges as a kinematic illusion of the matter moving (Kupervasser O., Olami Z., and Procaccia I., "Stability analysis of flame fronts: Dynamical systems approach in the complex plane", 1999; Oleg Kupervasser, Hrvoje Nikolić, Vinko Zlatić, "The Universal Arrow of Time", 2012; Kupervasser O., "Application of New Cybernetics in Physics", 2017).

‘

Anticipated Criticisms and Analytical Resolution of Paradoxical Impasses

The radical departure of this monograph from the Wilsonian effective field theory (EFT) paradigm will inevitably trigger standard counter-arguments from the conservative QFT and string-theoretic mainstream. In this section, we explicitly formalize these anticipated criticisms and provide their comprehensive, mathematically rigorous resolutions.

1. The Ultrahyperbolic Signature Impasse and Hadamard Instability

Anticipated Criticism: Spacetimes with multiple time coordinates (here, signature (3+, 3-)) are historically prohibited due to the Hadamard catastrophe. The presence of additional time axes (∂t22,∂ t32) introduces non-causal hyperbolic loops, leading to an uncontrolled ultraviolet (UV) explosion of ghost modes that destroys the predictability of the Cauchy evolutionary problem.

Resolution: The Cauchy evolutionary problem is a flawed artifact of the flat Minkowski vacuum approximation. In this model, quantization is executed on an isotropic, dynamic de Sitter background, which fundamentally alters the topological boundary conditions:

  • According to the Banks–Fischler holographic bounds (T. Banks,What does it mean to have a quantum gravitational theory of de Sitter Space?, 2026), de Sitter quantum gravity is strictly described by a finite-dimensional Hilbert space with a finite number of states N = eSdS. Consequently, infinite UV momentum tails fueling the Hadamard instability are mathematically absent from the spectrum.

  • The stationary Painlevé–Gullstrand flows map (Gaur R., Visser M., "Cosmology in Painlev’e–Gullstrand coordinates", 2022) the ultrahyperbolic differential problem into an elliptic Sturm–Liouville eigenvalue problem on compact phase slices. The time evolution coordinate is substituted by the macroscopic area increment of the event horizon Δrc.

2. The Non-Locality of the Mass Spectrum and UV/IR Mixing

Anticipated Criticism: The derivation of the Standard Model mass spectrum from the infrared (IR) causal event horizon boundary I+ violates the decouple core of Wilsonian EFT, which dictates that low-energy parameters must be determined exclusively by local UV counterterms.

Resolution: This is precisely the lesson of holographic UV/IR mixing. The mass spectrum is inherently non-local: each mass gap is an equilibrium pointer state emerging from the balance between the UV dynamics of the internal Cℓ3,3 algebra and the IR pressure of the global cosmological horizon. The Palatini non-polynomial gravity by Borissova and Carballo-Rubio (J.Borissova and R.Carballo-Rubio,"Regular black holes from pure gravity in four dimensions", arXiv:2602.16773 (gr-qc), 2026) functions as a built-in geometric UV damper modifying the gravitational coupling constant:

Geff(P) = G0 1 + ℓPl2P 		(1)

As the curvature invariant diverges (P →∞), the effective response elastically drops to zero (Geff → 0), suppressing the loop divergences of heavy quarks (the top quark) and stabilizing the iterative quantum subtraction.

3. The Violation of the Tsirelson Bound and Macroscopic Causality

Anticipated Criticism: The prediction of super-quantum Bell-type violations (SBell > 22) up to the Popescu–Rohrlich bound (S → 4) at the 50μm scale implies a gross violation of Einsteinian causality and the immediate emergence of closed timelike curves (CTCs).

Resolution: Macroscopic causality is strictly preserved. In the full 6D continuum, signals propagate strictly locally at the speed of light c. The spatial distance Δx is honest-to-God crossed by the wave process, but the propagation vector is directed along the hidden time axes t2,t3, forming a temporal bridge (Mohammad Furquan, Tejinder P. Singh, P Samuel Wesley,"Time-like Extra Dimensions: Quantum Nonlocality, Spin, and Tsirelson Bound", 2025):

Δx2 -Δt 12 -Δt 22 -Δt 32 = 0 →Δt 1 = 0Δx2 = Δt 22 + Δt 32 		(2)

The Rembieliński causal filter (J. Rembielinski, Tachyons and the preferred frame, 1997) in the preferred frame induced by Dark Energy enforces absolute simultaneity synchronization, mathematically blocking the loop closure of causality curves. At the macroscopic scale (r ≥ 50μm), environment-induced quantum decoherence (einselection) washes away the phase coherence between sheets, driving the lock parameter to infinity (ξ →∞) and recovering standard 4D general relativity:

Φanomal(r) ~ Φ0 ⋅ exp ⁡ (- r Ldecog ) 		(3)

The departure from the Tsirelson bound is localized strictly at the self-duality equator, posing no threat to macroscopic causal structures.

4. Resolving the Tegmark Paradox via Cosmological Quantum Scars

Anticipated Criticism: The long-standing veto on quantum biology, formulated by Max Tegmark (2000). Tegmark calculated the very small decoherence time for a "free" particle interacting with a hot, open, and expanding Friedmannian background (ΛCDM), where quantum phase information irreversibly dissipates into an infinite thermal reservoir.

Resolution: However, his argument relies on a fundamental geometric error. Indeed, in a stationary de Sitter resonator locked within Anti-Painlevé–Gullstrand coordinates, the chaotic quantum dynamics of a closed volume operate at full capacity. The very geometry of the fixed cosmological "box" inherently generates invariant, non-ergodic periodic orbits. A living cell within the 50-μm macroscopic threshold does not need to engineer hyper-complex thermal isolation; it simply occupies these pre-existing vacuum corridors—quantum many-body scars. Inside these topological scars (Christopher J. Turner, A. A. Michailidis, D. A. Abanin, M. Serbyn and Z. Papi´c, "Weak ergodicity breaking from quantum many-body scars",2018), the quantum phase is geometrically shielded, rendering the surrounding warm, wet environment completely powerless to destroy it. Life, therefore, emerges not as a chemical anomaly fighting thermal death, but as a self-sustaining quantum processor embedded directly into the structural architecture of the Universe.

Contents

Part I
Part 1. The Initial State of the Universe and the Elimination of the Inflationary Paradigm

Abstract. An alternative cosmological model is proposed, describing the Universe from the perspective of a local static observer within the framework of the fixed de Sitter cosmological event horizon concept. Within the developed approach, the initial state of the system is interpreted as a closed, isolated quantum-informational domain. The entire physical mass of the Universe is initially concentrated in a central ultra-dense core in the form of a Planck star, while the total volume of information is fixed on an external holographic screen. It is shown that the discrete quantum nature of space and the initial non-local entanglement of the spatial degrees of freedom of the macro-volume automatically eliminate the classical problems of singularity, flatness, and causality horizon at the level of the initial configuration. The model mathematically substantiates the ideal homogeneity and isotropy of the early Universe due to a quantum-non-local informational matrix. The obtained results prove the redundancy of the inflationary paradigm, the phase of superluminal expansion of space, as well as the hypothesis of preliminary cyclic contraction.

0.1 Physical parameters of the initial state (t = 1)

Experimentally confirmed is not the fact of space expansion, but the fact of the existence of a growing cosmological event horizon with de Sitter asymptotics . Within the framework of this concept of the Universe, rather than a growing infinite Universe, we will construct our model [3] Physics is an experimental science, and we will build the model from the point of view of a real observer of the Universe, motionless relative to its background, and not of the observed infinite Universe. And we will build the model precisely for this single unique de Sitter Universe with its single background, rather than a backgroundless or multi-background gravity.This methodology strictly extends the principles of New Cybernetics [1], where physical paradoxes and the arrow of time are resolved exclusively through the formal inclusion of a bounded local observer interacting with an open environmental background [2].

The initial state of the Universe at the Planck moment of the expansion start is described as a closed, isolated quantum-informational system with a fixed mass M = 1061 in Planck units. The physical topology of this system directly relies on the concept of black hole quantum remnants developed by Carlo Rovelli and Francesca Vidotto within loop quantum gravity [4]. The geometry and informational structure of the initial state are determined by three rigid parameters:

  • External static event horizon: Space is bounded by an external sphere of radius R = M = 1061 Planck lengths. By its fundamental definition, the event horizon represents the boundary of a spacetime region from which no physical signal or information can escape to a remote observer [7], therefore its radius for a static configuration is a strict constant and does not change with time. Within the framework of the Wheeler–DeWitt formalism [8], the initial state of the Universe is considered as a single macroscopic wave function on a volume of radius 1061, where the homogeneity of space and the localization of mass in the Planck core are given by the initial non-local quantum entanglement of the spatial degrees of freedom of this macro-volume. The total proper time during which the flying matter will fill the system and reach the fixed horizon of 1061 is strictly τ ~ M = 1061 Planck times (17 billion years), which is derived by Rovelli and Vidotto [4] through the calculation of the proper time of free fall and quantum bounce of the internal Planck star.

  • Informational invariant (Unitarity): The total volume of information locked inside this holographic domain, according to Erik Verlinde’s formula for a holographic screen on the event horizon, is strictly equal to the Bekenstein–Hawking entropy [5]:

    S0 = A 4lPl2 = 4πM2 ≈10122 bits

    This number of pure quantum states is constant, which completely preserves the law of conservation of information and the principle of unitarity within the considered volume.

  • Central Planck core (Planck star): The entire physical mass of the Universe (M = 1061) is initially concentrated at the very geometric center of the system in the form of a quantum ultra-dense core — a Planck star [4]. At the maximum Planck density (ρ = 1), the physical radius of this core is calculated as:

    rcore ~ M1∕3 = (1061)1∕3 ≈1.34 × 1020 Planck lengths

    In the metric SI system, this value corresponds exactly to the physical size of a single proton ( ≈ 2.16 × 10-15 meters).

  • Entropy deficit from mass: The central core of mass M creates an elastic deficit of degrees of freedom on the external holographic event horizon of radius R, which is calculated according to Verlinde’s basic formula [6]:

    SM = -M ⋅ R = -1061 ⋅ 1061 = -10122 bits

    The total free entropy on the screen at the initial moment is strictly equal to zero (S0 + SM = 0), which mathematically proves the start of the Universe from an absolutely pure quantum state.

0.2 Why is cosmic inflation completely eliminated?

The standard cosmological model uses cosmic inflation to artificially solve three fundamental problems: singularity, flatness, and causality horizon (homogeneity). In this model, the inflationary paradigm is completely eliminated due to redundancy, and to substantiate the initial homogeneous state, we also do not need to postulate a phase of preliminary cyclic contraction of the previous Universe, which is standard for classical models of loop cosmology [9]. All problems are removed automatically at the level of the initial configuration:

1.
The singularity problem disappears due to the discrete nature of space in loop quantum gravity [9]. The density physically cannot exceed the limit ρ = 1, which replaces the GR singularity with an elastic quantum bounce of the core (Planck star explosion) in the further dynamics [4].
2.
The flatness problem disappears because the expansion of matter occurs inside a fixed static macroscopic volume. Space is initially flat according to Euclidean geometry, and matter simply flows along this rigid, ready-made coordinate grid.
3.
The causality horizon problem disappears due to Verlinde’s holographic principle [5]. The full information of the system (10122 bits) is recorded on the external fixed event horizon. This screen is a single quantum system where all pixels are initially quantum entangled (linked) with each other. Quantum non-locality distributes information through the system instantaneously. The core matter in the center begins its expansion over an already ideally homogeneous and isotropic informational matrix. Mechanical mixing of particles at the speed of light, superluminal inflation of space, or a phase of preliminary contraction of the Universe to ensure homogeneity are no longer required.

Part II
Part 2. Further Dynamics: Quantum-Gravitational Expansion and the Emergent Nature of the Cosmic Microwave Background

Abstract. The dynamics of a cosmological model based on the mechanical parting of quantum matter from the geometric center to a stationary event horizon without expansion of the space fabric itself is investigated. The kinematics of the process is described as an elastic flow of matter within a time-reversible metric, passing through a stage of a cold coherent state with zero free entropy. A holographic mechanism for the generation of the cosmic microwave background via a semiclassical expansion of the wave equation of quantum geometrodynamics is considered. It is shown that primary density inhomogeneities are formed by projecting quantum correlations from an external macroscopic screen into the expanding volume. This approach substantively explains the observed suppression of cosmic microwave background power at large angular scales and the formation of a scale-invariant spectrum. An emergent transition of the system into a classical thermodynamic regime due to quantum decoherence of the wave function of the Universe during the interaction of matter fields with metric fluctuations is described. It is shown that this phase transition leads to the dissipation of the elastic tension energy of the vacuum, the generation of effective entropy, and the homogeneous birth of a hot plasma, in which quantum fluctuations of geometry are transformed into classical acoustic density oscillations.

0.1 Kinematics of the elastic matter flow in the Novikov–Rovelli T-region

The dynamical evolution of the system at times τ > 0 is described as an internal mechanical expansion of the quantum matter of the core from the geometric center to a eternal external boundary fixed at the static event horizon distance R = M = 1061 Planck lengths. This process excludes the expansion of the space fabric itself and completely obeys the laws of quantum gravity in the T-region of spacetime, theoretically described by I. D. Novikov [1]. The mathematical matching of the internal geometry of the quantum bounce with the external stationary Schwarzschild region is performed in Anti-Painlevé–Gullstrand (A-PG) coordinates, which represent a time-reversible map of the original metric, according to the methodology of H. Haggard and C. Rovelli [3]. Within this geometry, the elastic Planck core of mass M, having passed the point of extreme quantum pressure at density ρ = 1, undergoes a symmetric outward parting. The radial motion velocity of the matter front of the elastic core r(τ) along flat Euclidean spatial slices of the metric is determined as a mirror reflection of free-fall trajectories with a "plus" sign (outward motion) and is given in Planck units by the differential equation:

dr dτ = +2M r 		(1)

As long as the spatial scale of the expanding matter front r(τ) remains in the microscopic range from the initial proton volume (1020 Planck lengths) to the size of a hydrogen atom (1024 Planck lengths), Friedmann cosmological effects are absorbed, and the dynamics is dictated by the strong local gravity of the point mass M. The entire Universe inside this flying front represents a cold, structureless coherent state with zero free entropy, where the classical laws of thermodynamics are not yet applicable.

0.2 Holographic mechanism of microwave background generation via the Wheeler–DeWitt equation

The cosmic microwave background (CMB) anisotropy and dynamics observed today are derived from the proposed configuration without involving an inflationary scenario. Primary density fluctuations are formed not by the mechanical stretching of local vacuum oscillations, but as a result of the holographic projection of pre-specified quantum correlations from the external macroscopic Verlinde screen into the expanding volume.

The mathematical basis of this process is described within canonical quantum gravity on a stationary spatial canvas using a semiclassical expansion of the Wheeler–DeWitt wave equation ĤΨ = 0. As shown in the fundamental works of C. Kiefer and M. Krämer [4], as well as D. Brizuela [5], quantum-gravitational contributions to the global wave function of the Universe Ψ naturally modulate primary matter perturbations due to the non-local quantum entanglement of modes in the superspace of geometrodynamics.

In this context, we consider the elastically expanding baryonic Rovelli–Vidotto core [3] to be equivalent to an expanding Friedmann cosmological bubble, the initial radius of which is defined within the boundaries of the initial event horizon of the observation point located at the geometric center of the holographic screen, which corresponds to the event horizon in the infinite time limit (1061 Planck lengths). Since the region outside this initial event horizon is causally isolated from the internal observer, the hypothetical question of the global homogeneity or emptiness of the external infinite space is unobservable and does not affect the local physics of the system.

Inside this volume, the elastic quantum UV limit of loop quantum cosmology (LQC), developed by A. Ashtekar, B. Gupta, and V. Sreenath [8] (Rmax ≈ 62 Planck curvature units), determines the physical limit of contraction and the minimum curvature radius of the core at the moment of bounce (rcurv ≈ 0.31 Planck lengths). In the semiclassical expansion of the Born–Oppenheimer method (higher-order effects in 1∕mP 2 described by Kiefer [4] and Brizuela [5]), long-wavelength infrared modes (angular scale ℓ ≤ 30) comparable to the core size encounter this internal Planckian structure, elastically scattering their energy. This "cosmic tango" between micro- and macro-scales [8] selectively suppresses the power of the primary spectrum at large angles. Short waves, on the other hand, freely pass through the quantum grid of the vacuum, forming a strictly scale-invariant dimensionless Harrison–Zel’dovich power spectrum at macro-scales, fully corresponding to the holographic cosmological scenario of P. McFadden and K. Skenderis [6,7] and the data from the Planck observatory:

Loop quantum cosmology [9,10] predicts the properties of the cosmic microwave background anomalies better than inflation. While classical inflation in general relativity predicts an ideally smooth, homogeneous spectrum at all angles, strict mirror symmetry, and a dimensionless lensing amplitude parameter AL = 1, the cosmic observatories WMAP and Planck found that these predictions are violated [9]. Standard inflation is powerless against these power deficits, asymmetries, and over-smoothing. The value of the works of Ashtekar (2015) and Agullo (2023) [9,10] lies in proving that these effects are generated by loop gravity at the initial moment, completely eliminating the need for an inflationary phase to carry perturbations to the macro-level.

P(k) ~ k3⟨|δ k|2⟩~ k0 = const 		(2)

The expanding Hubble flow of Novikov–Rovelli matter [1,3] sequentially "reads" these spatial quantum correlations, predetermining the future structure of the cosmic microwave background acoustic peaks at the quantum level, which is described in detail via cosmological perturbations in holography [7].

0.3 Decoherence and phase transition into the macroscopic regime

The transition from pure quantum gravity to a classical thermodynamic Universe occurs emergently when the radius of the expanding front of the equivalent core r(τ) reaches macroscopic scales (the range of 1024 - 1028 Planck lengths). The static holographic screen of radius 1061 itself does not entangle with anything, remaining a pure data source. Objective quantum decoherence of the wave function of the Universe is realized strictly inside the emergent volume between the local quantum fields of the flying matter (System) and the inhomogeneous quantum fluctuations of the metric of higher orders, which are unresolvable at the macro-scale (Environment), according to the mathematical models of Kiefer [4].

The coordinate grid of flat Euclidean spatial slices of the Anti-Painlevé–Gullstrand metric [3] acts as a stable, non-expanding background reservoir for this process. In the process of radial expansion of the Hubble flow, matter physically moves along this grid, as a consequence of which local matter fields begin to intensively quantum-entangle with the modes of the surrounding space. Due to this interaction, the local density matrix of the expanding matter automatically transitions from a pure state to a mixed state via the procedure of taking a partial trace over inconsequential macroscopic degrees of freedom of the geometry.This cybernetic transition from quantum phase relations to coarse-grained thermodynamic entropy follows from the generalized environment (or observer)-matter entanglement described in [2].

This process leads to a sharp phase transition:

  • The local quantum phase relations of the matter of the Rovelli core [3] are destroyed, and hidden quantum information is converted into effective (coarse-grained) thermodynamic entropy.

  • Quantum decay of the coherent state of the vacuum occurs, along with the dissipation of the macroscopic elastic tension energy into local material degrees of freedom, homogeneously generating a hot quark-gluon plasma throughout the entire volume of the expanded front.

  • The primary quantum fluctuations of the Wheeler–DeWitt geometry [4] instantly materialize as classical acoustic density oscillations of this plasma, forming the cosmic microwave background spectrum observed today in strict accordance with the astrophysical data of the Planck observatory.

Part III
Part 3. Macroscopic Dynamics: Friedmann Equations in Anti-Painlevé–Gullstrand Coordinates and the Nature of the Dark Sector

Abstract. The macroscopic dynamics of a cosmological model is developed within the framework of the stationary geometric volume concept, which remains unchanged inside a fixed event horizon. In the proposed description, the cosmological scale factor determines the dynamic change in the rate of vacuum time, and the observed expansion is interpreted as a mechanical flow of elastic matter from the center to the periphery along a flat Euclidean coordinate grid. A compensatory mechanism is substantiated, revealing the physical nature of the dark sector as a consequence of the strict conservation of the total mass of a closed system. It is established that dark energy and dark matter arise emergently in a stationary volume to compensate for the radial escape of baryonic matter. Dark energy represents a compensating vacuum mass that gradually replaces the escaping matter, while dark matter is formed as a result of cross-gravitational interaction between the distributed baryon flow and the background vacuum tension. The proposed approach resolves the dark energy density paradox by defining its value as the quotient of dividing the total unchanged mass of the system by the stationary volume, which at the final point of evolution will be completely occupied by the vacuum component.

0.1 Mapping macroscopic dynamics into the Friedmann Anti-PG basis

After passing the point of global quantum decoherence and the emergent birth of a hot quark-gluon plasma, the system enters the macroscopic regime, where the evolution of matter begins to obey the classical equations of GR. However, following the mathematical apparatus developed in [2], [3], we completely discard the standard comoving interpretation of the radial coordinate of the Friedmann–Lemaître–Robertson–Walker (FLRW) metric as breaking the holographic context. We adopt De Vicente’s non-expanding interpretation of the FLRW model, where the three-dimensional spatial volume of the Universe inside the event horizon is geometrically stationary and motionless, and the scale factor a(t) determines not the inflation of vacuum cells, but a dynamic scale of the vacuum time rate (time downscaling).

For an explicit mathematical description of the physical movement of the baryonic bubble inside this stationary box, we perform a strict coordinate transformation from the non-expanding FLRW metric to the cosmological form of Painlevé–Gullstrand coordinates, documented in the works of R. Gaur and M. Visser [3]. According to Gaur and Visser, Einstein’s equations for a flat Universe (k = 0) officially allow choosing a time-reversible Anti-PG metric map (a plus sign before the cross term), in which the GR interval takes the form:

ds2 = - (1 - H2(t)R2) dt2 + 2H(t)RdtdR + dR2 + R2dΩ2 		(1)

In this geometry, spatial slices always remain strictly flat and Euclidean (gRR = 1), and the "expansion of the Universe" is interpreted not as inflation of the space fabric, but as a mechanical Hubble flow of the elastic matter of the bubble (Rovelli core) moving from the center to the periphery. The cosmological Hubble parameter H(t) = ȧ∕a here plays the mathematical role of the shift velocity vector of this flow (v = +HR).

The observation point is rigidly fixed at the geometric center of the holographic screen (the event horizon of the Universe in the infinite time limit). At the same time, the Friedmann cosmological bubble itself is strictly defined within the boundaries of the initial event horizon of this observation point. The external screen is motionlessly fixed at the macroscopic mark R0 = 1061 Planck lengths, and its geometric volume inside the closed system is strictly invariant in time and equals:

V stat = 4π 3 R03 = 4.996173 × 10183 		(2)

0.2 Interpretation of the Friedmann equations and the emergent nature of the Dark Sector as a compensatory mechanism

Since the Friedmann equations are coordinate invariants of GR, when transforming into the Gaur–Visser Anti-PG metric [3], they fully retain their standard mathematical form, ensuring the dynamic stability of the non-expanding world:

H2(t) = 8πG 3 ρtotal(t) 		(3)

ä a = -4πG 3 (ρtotal(t) + 3ptotal(t)) 		(4)

The principal logical breakthrough of this model lies in the strict differentiation of the physical volumes in which energy densities are calculated. In the original, canonical Friedmann Universe, density is by definition calculated exclusively in the inflating comoving volume V comoving ~ a3(t). In this comoving volume, there physically is and never has been any Dark Energy or Dark Matter — there are strictly baryons (Mb) of the elastic Rovelli core. Let us introduce the current baryon density of the Universe as ρb,0 ≡ ρb(a = 1). By the law of baryon number conservation in the comoving volume, the baryon density at any moment of time t is expressed through today’s value as:

ρb(t) = ρb,0 a3(t) 		(5)

Loop quantum cosmology [1,4] predicts the properties of the cosmic microwave background anisotropy anomalies better than inflation. While classical inflation in general relativity predicts an ideally smooth, homogeneous spectrum at all angles, strict mirror symmetry, and a dimensionless lensing amplitude parameter AL = 1, the cosmic observatories WMAP and Planck found that these predictions are violated [1]. Standard inflation is powerless against these power deficits, asymmetries, and over-smoothing. The value of the works of Ashtekar (2015) and Agullo (2023) [1,4] lies in proving that these effects are generated by loop gravity at the initial moment, completely eliminating the need for an inflationary phase to carry perturbations to the macro-level.

We consider the current event horizon as a description of the current volume of baryonic matter. All baryonic matter escaping beyond its limits irreversibly becomes unobservable and is replaced by Dark Matter and Dark Energy. From a quantum perspective, Dark Energy is a Bose-condensate of gravity, and Dark Matter is responsible for correlations between Dark Energy and the baryonic matter remaining in the causal bubble. The holographic horizon (corresponding to the limiting position of the causal horizon and the Hubble horizon as time approaches infinity) is a constant boundary of this volume. Inside it, evolution is unitary from the point of view of quantum mechanics, therefore baryonic matter cannot disappear, but transitions into Dark Energy and Dark Matter (where Dark Matter preserves the correlations between the remaining baryons and Dark Energy, thus Dark Energy replaces the departed baryons, and Dark Matter replaces the correlations of the departed baryons with the remaining ones).

However, since this Friedmann baryonic bubble expands and escapes inside the closed, rigid stationary volume of the Anti-PG metric V stat, the invariance of the system’s Lagrangian under time translations (homogeneity of time by Noether’s theorem) requires strict and absolute conservation of the total internal mass of the Universe at the eternal mark Mtotal = 1061 = const. The dark sector is absent in the Friedmann and Ashtekar Universe — it is born emergently exclusively inside the closed Anti-PG volume to compensate for the physical escape of baryons outward along the De Vicente vacuum coordinate grid [2].

The stationary Gaur–Visser vacuum itself elastically reacts to this emptying, transforming the total mass-energy into a strict algebraic mass balance equation. In this case, the cross term of Dark Matter is given by E. Verlinde’s original formula [5]:

Mdm ∝Mde Mb 		(6)

which within our Anti-PG configuration leads to the final mass relationship:

Mtotal = Mde(t) + Mb(t) + 3 2Mde (t)Mb (t) ≡ 1061 		(7)

  • Dark Energy Mass (Mde(t)): The emergent compensating mass of the vacuum, which dynamically manifests over time, replacing the escaping matter. At the start (t → 1), there is no Dark Energy in the volume (Mde = 0).

  • Dark Matter Mass (Mdm): Arises as a cross term of purely internal gravitational interaction between the distributed baryon flow (Mb) and the background vacuum tension (Mde). The factor 3 2 is a strict rational geometric coefficient for converting forces, reflecting the ratio of the dimensionality of the three-dimensional physical volume of the baryonic bubble to the two-dimensional surface of the radial vacuum tension (3D∕2D = 3∕2). When substituting the current fractions of baryons (Ωb = 0.049) and Dark Energy (Ωde = 0.685) measured by the Planck observatory, this cross term yields the value:

    Ωdm = 1.5 ⋅0.685 ⋅ 0.049 ≈ 0.2748     		(8)

    which ideally closes the total density of the Universe to the value Ωtotal = 1.0088 with an accuracy of 99.1%. In the early era and in the asymptotic end (t →∞), Dark Matter in this balance equation equals zero, reaching its peak exclusively in the intermediate (our) epoch.

0.3 Solution to the Dark Energy density paradox

The proposed compensatory mechanism completely resolves the fundamental cosmological paradox: why, when multiplying the current average Dark Energy density ρde by the full stationary volume of the Universe V stat, does the internal observer obtain the exact total mass of the Universe now (1061), despite the dynamic escape of baryons? As the Hubble flow disperses, the baryon density in the Friedmann comoving volume falls, and the compensatory Dark Energy density in the stationary volume grows. At the final point of the Universe’s evolution (t →∞), absolutely all baryons will leave the closed system (Mb → 0), the Dark Matter induced by them will disappear (Mdm → 0), and the Dark Energy of the vacuum will completely replace the departed matter, occupying exactly 100Consequently, the Dark Energy density by its definition in the closed Anti-PG volume is an emergent imprint of the total Noether constant of the entire system, smeared over the box of the stationary volume:

ρde ≡Mtotal V stat = 1061 4π 3 R03 = 3 4π × 10-122 ≈ 10-122 		(9)

Multiplying the Dark Energy density by the volume V stat now, we obtain the total mass of the Universe because Dark Energy is the exact amount of vacuum elasticity that will ultimately completely replace the Friedmann baryonic bubble, keeping the total mass invariant constant throughout cosmic time under the protection of time translations of Noether’s theorem.

Bibliography

[1]   Ashtekar A., and Barrau A. Loop quantum cosmology: From pre-inflationary dynamics to observations // arXiv preprint arXiv:1504.07559. — 2015. — P. 1–21.

[2]   DeVicente J. Universe Expanding vs Non-Expanding Interpretation of Friedmann-Lemaitre-Robertson-Walker (FLRW) Metric // Centro de Investigaciones Energ’eticas, Medioambientales y Tecnol’ogicas (CIEMAT). — Madrid, 2024.

[3]   Gaur R., and Visser M. Cosmology in Painlev’e–Gullstrand coordinates // Journal of Cosmology and Astroparticle Physics, Volume 2022

[4]   Agullo, I., Wang, A., and Wilson-Ewing, E. (2023). Loop Quantum Cosmology: Relation Between Theory and Observations. In: Bambi, C., Modesto, L., Shapiro, I. (eds) Handbook of Quantum Gravity. Springer, Singapore.

[5]   Verlinde E. Emergent Gravity and the Dark Universe // SciPost Physics. — 2017. — Vol. 2, No. 2. — P. 016.

Part IV
Part 4. Kinematics of the Baryonic Front and the Phase Moment of Maximum Convergence of Cosmological Metrics

Abstract. An analytical model of the baryonic front kinematics is constructed, describing the mechanical expansion of matter from the geometric center to an external stationary screen. Based on the integration of the visibility front trajectory, a dynamic equation is derived for the mass of matter located inside the particle horizon at an arbitrary moment in time. It is discovered that due to the mutual cancellation of dynamic scale factors, the resulting mass functional is expressed directly through the fundamental mass conservation invariant of the closed system and the structural ratio of the component density fractions. Numerical verification of the equation based on modern cosmological data revealed the mathematical convergence of the standard general relativity calculation to the true quantum weight of the central core. It is shown that the discovered coincidence is due to the uniqueness of the current macroscopic epoch, which represents the only phase moment of evolution in which the expanding material bubble is distributed most quasi-homogeneously, and the physical state of the quantum-gravitational cavity is maximally close to the description of a classical homogeneous metric.

0.1 Kinematics of the Baryonic Front

Within the framework of the developed quantum-gravitational model, the Universe is considered not as a homogeneously expanding GR fabric, but as a stationary cavity (cavern), in the center of which an ultra-dense quantum-compressed baryonic core (Planck star) is localized. The mass of this central core is strictly conserved in time and is given by the fundamental Noether invariant of the vacuum:

Mcore ≡ M0 = 1 2H∞

By virtue of the laws of quantum gravity, the expansion of the Universe is interpreted as a mechanical expansion of the baryonic bubble from the center of the cavity to the external holographic screen. As the baryons escape, they are replaced by Dark Matter and Dark Energy. Below is a step-by-step derivation of the total calculated mass of baryons that a local observer fixes inside the particle horizon at an arbitrary moment in time t, followed by an analysis for the current epoch.

Step 1. Setting up the integral for the comoving radius

The dynamics of the visibility front

movement in comoving coordinates is determined by the classical scale factor a(t). In flat space, the expansion trajectory given by the total gravitating matter flow (including the elastic contribution of dark matter) has the form [1]:

a(t) = (Ωm,0 ΩΛ,0 ) 1∕3 sinh ⁡ 2∕3 (3 2H∞t)

The physical radius of the particle horizon RPH(t) at an arbitrary moment in time is calculated by multiplying the current scale factor by the comoving distance integral:

RPH(t) = a(t) ⋅∫ 0t dt′ a(t′) = a(t) ⋅∫ 0t dt′ (Ωm,0 ΩΛ,0 ) 1∕3 sinh ⁡ 2∕3 (3 2H∞t′)

We pull the constant spatial factor out from under the integral sign (the fraction flips):

RPH(t) = a(t) ⋅ (ΩΛ,0 Ωm,0 ) 1∕3 ∫ 0t dt′ sinh ⁡ 2∕3 (3 2H∞t′)

To eliminate the time parameter H∞ from under the integration sign, we make a change of variable: x = 3 2H∞t′. Then the time differential is equal to dt′ = 2 3H∞dx, and the upper limit transforms into the dimensionless quantity 3 2H∞t. Pulling the constant outside, we obtain:

RPH(t) = a(t) ⋅ (ΩΛ,0 Ωm,0 ) 1∕3 2 3H∞∫ 03 2H∞t dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x)
Step 2. Calculating the physical volume of the particle horizon

The volume of the particle horizon sphere V PH(t) at time t is calculated using the standard geometric formula:

V PH(t) = 4π 3 RPH3(t) = 4π 3 a3(t) ⋅ (ΩΛ,0 Ωm,0 ) ⋅ ( 2 3H∞)3 ⋅ (∫ 03 2H∞t dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x) ) 3

Step 3. Integral equation for the mass of baryonic matter

The local baryon density ρb(t) in comoving space drops inversely proportional to the cube of the scale factor:

ρb(t) = ρb,0 a3(t)

To find the calculated mass of baryonic matter MPH(t) inside the visibility horizon, we multiply the physical volume of the particle horizon sphere by the baryon density at the same moment in time:

MPH(t) = V PH(t) ⋅ ρb(t) = [4π 3 a3(t) ⋅ (ΩΛ,0 Ωm,0 ) ⋅ 8 27H∞3 ⋅ (∫ 03 2H∞t dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x) ) 3] ⋅ [ ρb,0 a3(t) ]

The dynamic scale factors a3(t) in the numerator and denominator completely and strictly cancel each other out:

MPH(t) = 32π 81 ⋅ ρb,0 H∞3 ⋅ (ΩΛ,0 Ωm,0 ) ⋅ (∫ 03 2H∞t dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x) ) 3

Step 4. Transition to the fundamental invariant M0

The current baryon density ρb,0 is expressed through the relative fraction Ωb,0 and the critical density of the Universe. In the Planck basis of quantum gravity, the critical density is written through the asymptotic Hubble value as ρcrit,0 = 3 8π H∞2 ΩΛ,0 . From here:

ρb,0 = Ωb,0 ⋅ 3H∞2 8πΩΛ,0

Let us substitute this expression into the obtained coefficient before the integral:

MPH(t) = 32π 81H∞3 ⋅ (Ωb,0 ⋅ 3H∞2 8πΩΛ,0 ) ⋅ (ΩΛ,0 Ωm,0 ) ⋅ (∫ 03 2H∞t dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x) ) 3

We perform algebraic cancellations:

  • The Dark Energy parameters ΩΛ,0 cancel out completely.

  • The dimensionless densities of baryons and total matter form a pure structural ratio of fractions: Ωb,0 Ωm,0.

  • The numerical coefficients reduce to a rigid fraction: 32π⋅3 81⋅8π = 4 27.

  • The powers of the Hubble parameter transform to the form: H∞2 H∞3 = 1 H∞.

The equation takes a crystal clear form:

MPH(t) = 4 27H∞ ⋅ (Ωb,0 Ωm,0 ) ⋅ (∫ 03 2H∞t dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x) ) 3

Using the relationship for the vacuum invariant of the model 1 H∞ = 2M0, we finally transfer the mass dynamics into the M0 basis:

MPH(t) = 8 27M0 ⋅ (Ωb,0 Ωm,0 ) ⋅ (∫ 03 2H∞t dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x) ) 3

Step 5. Numerical verification for the "Today" epoch

For the current moment in time (t = t0), the analytical expression for the upper integration limit x0 is derived through the connection of the Hubble parameters, where x0 = 3 2H∞t0. By the cosmological identity H∞ = H0Ω Λ,0, the upper limit is strictly determined through the observed expansion rate H0 and time t0:

x0 = 3 2H0Ω Λ,0 ⋅ t0

Alternatively, the limit is set via the normalization condition of the current scale factor to unity (a(t0) = 1):

x0 = asinh ( Ω Λ,0 Ωm,0 )

Let us substitute the experimental data of the Planck space telescope into the equation without rounding:

1.
The true ratio of baryons to the total gravitating matter: Ωb,0 Ωm,0 = 0.0493 0.3140 ≈ 0.1570.
2.
The upper limit of the integral: x0 = asinh (0.6860 0.3140 ) ≈ 1.1806.
3.
The value of the definite integral: ∫ 01.1806 dx sinh ⁡ 2∕3(x) ≈ 3.1025.
4.
The value of the cubed integral bracket: (3.1025)3 ≈ 29.8631.

We calculate the final mass coefficient:

MPH(ttoday) = 8 27 × 0.1570 × 29.8631 × M0 = 1.3892 ⋅ M0

0.2 Conclusion

The obtained result exposes a fundamental cosmological phenomenon. Substituting the current Planck density parameters into the classical Friedmann GR apparatus yields a baryonic mass coefficient of 1.39 ⋅ M0, which is strikingly close to the true weight of the conserving central quantum core of our model (1.00 ⋅ M0).

This coincidence is not a random mathematical curiosity or anthropic luck of the standard ΛCDM model. Within the framework of the developed trilogy, it has a strict physical justification: the current macroscopic epoch represents a unique phase moment of the system’s evolution.

In the deep past (as t → 0), the Universe fully obeyed the laws of quantum bounce cosmology, space discreteness, and strong vacuum tension, where the GR equations are inapplicable. In the distant future (as t →∞), the baryonic front will

completely disperse and go to the external informational screen, leaving a pure static de Sitter vacuum inside the cavity. It is precisely today that the expanding baryonic bubble inside the cavern is distributed macroscopically in the most quasi-homogeneous way. Our epoch is the only point on the timeline where the physical state of the inhomogeneous quantum-gravitational cavity has come maximally close to the description of the classical Friedmann FLRW metric. This temporary convergence of two fundamentally different physical paradigms caused the mathematical convergence of the GR calculation to the true quantum invariant of mass conservation M0.

Bibliography

[1]   Michele Re Fiorentin, and Stefano Re Fiorentin, Cosmological horizons, Am. J. Phys. 91 (8), 2023

Part V
Part 5. Unitary Informational-Cosmological Mass Balance Model

Abstract. An analytical model of the baryonic front kinematics is constructed, describing the mechanical parting of matter from the geometric center to an external stationary screen. Based on the integration of the visibility front trajectory, a dynamic equation is derived for the mass of matter located inside the particle horizon at an arbitrary moment in time. It is discovered that due to the mutual cancellation of dynamic scale factors, the resulting mass functional is expressed directly through the fundamental mass conservation invariant of the closed system and the structural ratio of the component density fractions. Numerical verification of the equation based on modern cosmological data revealed the mathematical convergence of the standard general relativity calculation to the true quantum weight of the central core. It is shown that the discovered coincidence is due to the uniqueness of the current macroscopic epoch, which represents the only phase moment of evolution in which the expanding material bubble is distributed most quasi-homogeneously, and the physical state of the quantum-gravitational cavity is maximally close to the description of a classical homogeneous metric.

0.1 Introduction and Core Postulate

The model considers the Universe as a finite, closed informational system (a "bag of bits"), the evolution of which is bounded by two fundamental scales: the ultraviolet limit (UV limit, the initial singularity where the entire mass is concentrated at the Planck density M0) and the infrared limit (IR limit, the cosmological event horizon L).

The dynamics of the system is described via a dimensionless stage of expansion x = R∕L, where R is the current radius of the Universe and L is its limiting causal size. The total energy budget of the system at any moment in time is strictly fixed and equals the initial mass M0. The gravitational interaction and evolution of the Universe are governed by a non-linear superposition of three interconnected components.

0.2 Energy Budget Components

0.2.1 Decay of Baryons (Modified GR)

In contrast to the classical Friedmann model, where the total mass of baryons inside a comoving volume is invariant, in the present model ordinary matter is considered as a finite quantum resource displaced by the expanding Dark Energy. The effective gravitating mass of baryons MB inside the causal bubble decreases proportionally to the residual three-dimensional volume of space not occupied by the vacuum horizon:

MB = M0 ⋅ (1 - x)3 		(1)

where the factor (1 - x)3 describes the "freezing out" of the matter degrees of freedom. As the Universe expands, the quantum modes of particles lose coherence and are irreversibly pumped into the entropy of the horizon.

0.2.2 Mass on the Horizon (Geometry of Dark Energy)

The energy of Dark Energy MU (the de Sitter vacuum background) is not a static constant, but grows linearly as the horizon expands, reflecting the holographic increase in the surface area (the "screen") of the system:

MU = M0 ⋅ x 		(2)

At x = 1, the Universe completely transitions into an asymptotic IR state (pure de Sitter vacuum), where the entire initial mass M0 is completely converted into the geometric energy of the event horizon.

0.2.3 Emergent Dark Matter (Quantum Bridge)

Dark Matter MD in this model does not consist of hypothetical heavy particles. It arises as a quantum tension (correlation) between the vacuum geometry and baryonic matter. Mathematically, it is derived via the geometric mean (Bhattacharyya coefficient) of the interacting subsystems with a fundamental thermodynamic factor of 1.5:

MD = 1.5 ⋅MB ⋅ MU = 1.5M0x(1 - x)3 		(3)

Dark Matter is a non-self-contained, emergent entity — the "glue" of the informational network. It reaches its peak in the middle of the Universe’s evolution, temporarily holding mass and compensating for the dimensionality difference between the volumetric decay of baryons (R3) and the linear growth of the horizon (R).

0.3 Global Equilibrium Verification (Unitarity)

The total energy budget of the Universe represents the sum of all three components:

Mtotal(x) = M0 ⋅ [(1 - x)3 + x + 1.5x(1 - x)3] ≈ M 0 		(4)


Figure 1: Mass change of the Universe.

An exact algebraic calculation shows high stability of this "informational harness" at the key stages of the Universe’s life:

1.
Beginning of the Universe (x → 0): Matter completely dominates.
M = 1.0 + 0 + 0 = 1.0M0(Ideal coincidence)
2.
"Golden Ratio" (x ≈ 0.4): The phase transition is in full swing.
M = 0.216 + 0.4 + 1.50.0864 ≈ 0.616 + 0.44 = 1.05M0(Balance ripple of only 5%)
3.
Geometric midpoint (x = 0.5): The point of strict phase balance.
M = 0.125 + 0.5 + 1.50.0625 = 0.625 + 0.375 = 1.0M0(Absolute hit into the constant)
4.
Late stage (x ≈ 0.8): The Universe is close to heat death.
M = 0.008 + 0.8 + 1.50.0064 = 0.808 + 0.12 = 0.92M0(Budget drawdown stays within 8%)
5.
End of the Universe (x = 1.0): Pure de Sitter era.
M = 0 + 1.0 + 0 = 1.0M0(Ideal coincidence)

0.4 Conclusion

The use of the GR-dependence of the residual volume together with the thermodynamic coefficient of 1.5 proves that the fluctuations of the total effective mass of the Universe do not exceed 10% throughout the entire expansion cycle (we consider this model as an approximation — the total mass must be constant). The model successfully resolves the divergence problem of Verlinde’s theory, naturally cutting off the infinite tails of Dark Matter at the scale where they completely dissolve into the cosmological event horizon.

Part VI
Part 6. Derivation of the Empirical Tully–Fisher Law from the Quantum-Informational Superposition of the Mean Field

Abstract. A quantum-informational method for describing the dynamics of local systems within the stationary cavity of the Universe is proposed. The approach is based on the mean-field wave function, which represents a superposition of baryonic and vacuum degrees of freedom on a local holographic screen. It is established that integrating the squared modulus of the total wave function over the sphere surface generates an interference term responsible for the emergence of hidden mass. This component is formed as a result of the cross-quantum intersection of matter and the background vacuum condensate. An exact relation for the fourth power dependence of the rotation velocity on the baryonic mass is derived analytically. The obtained results eliminate the need to introduce hypothetical hidden particles, proving the holographic nature of the observed dynamical anomalies on the periphery of systems.

0.1 Physical Formulation of the Local Problem in a Stationary Vacuum

Let us consider an isolated macroscopic system (a galaxy) within the global stationary cavity of the Universe. The boundary conditions and local force balance of the system are determined by the following parameters:

  • External holographic screen of the Universe: possesses a fixed total mass-energy M0 = 1061 in Planck units.

  • Global Dark Energy background: is given by a constant volumetric density Λ ≡ ρde = M0 V stat ≈ 10-122.

  • Localized baryonic mass: all visible matter of the galaxy is concentrated at its geometric center and has a mass Mb.

  • Smallness condition: the mass of the galaxy is negligibly small compared to the mass of the entire Universe (Mb ≪ M0).

  • Compensatory residue: all remaining space of the cavity is filled with the background Bose-condensate of Dark Energy and induced elastic correlations of Dark Matter distributed along the stationary Anti-PG coordinate grid.

The system is considered completely stationary, fixed within the spatial structure of the Gaur–Visser cavern, which eliminates time dynamics at galactic scales.

0.2 Quantum-Informational Superposition on a Local Screen

Let us surround the central baryonic mass Mb with a spherical holographic screen of radius r. Dark Energy as a manifestation of vacuum elasticity obeys a strict holographic principle, so its effective mass Mde(r) inside a local region is determined not by the three-dimensional volume, but by the surface tension of the vacuum condensate and is proportional to the area of this two-dimensional boundary:

Mde(r) = σ ⋅ 4πr2 		(1)

where σ is a constant coefficient of the surface mass density of the vacuum.

Since the total system is in a pure quantum state, according to the mean-field method, the distribution of information on the screen surface is described by a single macroscopic wave function Ψfull(θ,ϕ). It represents a weighted quantum superposition of baryonic and vacuum degrees of freedom uniformly distributed over the sphere surface:

Ψfull(θ,ϕ) = Mb ⋅Ψb(θ,ϕ) + Mde (r) ⋅Ψde(θ,ϕ) 		(2)

0.3 Calculation of Dark Matter Mass via the Interference Term

The total mass-energy M(r) inside a local holographic screen is found as the integral of the squared modulus of the total mean-field wave function over the entire two-dimensional sphere surface:

M(r) = ∫ SΨfull(θ,ϕ) ⋅Ψfull*(θ,ϕ)dS 		(3)

Expanding the square of the sum, we obtain:

M(r) = ∫ SMb|Ψb|2dS + ∫ SMde(r)|Ψde|2dS + ∫ S2Mb Mde (r) ⋅Re(ΨbΨde*)dS 		(4)

Since the wave functions are normalized to a unit area of the screen ( ∫ S|Ψ|2dS = 1), the first two integrals yield the pure masses of baryons and Dark Energy. The third cross term determines the mass of induced Dark Matter correlations arising due to quantum interference (crossing) between the baryonic matter and the vacuum DE condensate:

M(r) = Mb + Mde(r) + 2 ⋅ Ccorr ⋅Mb Mde (r) 		(5)

In this case, the correlation constant Ccorr is explicitly determined by the following surface integral:

Ccorr ≡∫ SRe(ΨbΨde*)dS 		(6)

The physical properties of the quantum superposition impose rigid theoretical boundaries on the value of the constant:

-1 ≤ Ccorr ≤ 1 		(7)

0.4 Global Invariants and Local Fluctuations

Let us take the radius of the local screen to the global limit of the Universe (r → R0). By virtue of time homogeneity according to Noether’s theorem, the total mass of the Universe in a stationary volume is a rigid invariant M0 = 1061. The global mass balance equation has the form:

M0 = Mde + Mb + 3 2Mde Mb 		(8)

Equating it to the general expression on the external holographic screen of the Universe of radius R0, where Mde(R0) ≡ Mde:

M(R0) = Mb + Mde(R0) + 2 ⋅⟨Ccorr⟩⋅Mb Mde (R0 ) 		(9)

We find the exact average value of the correlation constant for the Universe as a whole:

2 ⋅⟨Ccorr⟩ = 3 2⟹⟨Ccorr⟩ = 3 4 		(10)

For an individual stationary galaxy, which is a local spatial fluctuation, the correlation constant Ccorr has a fixed individual value determined by the geometry of field entanglement in a given cavern:

Ccorr = 3 4 + δC 		(11)

0.5 Analytical Expression of Mass via Milgrom’s Constant a0

In Planck units (G = c = ℏ = 1), the minimum acceleration limit a0 induced by the IR boundary of the stationary cavity is inversely proportional to the radius of the external horizon: a0 = 1 R0. Using the holographic Schwarzschild relation R0 = 2Mde, we express the Dark Energy mass of the entire Universe through Milgrom’s constant:

M0 ≈ Mde = 1 2a0 		(12)

Then the surface mass density of the vacuum σ on the two-dimensional screen takes the form:

σ = Mde 4πR02 = 1 2a0 4π ( 1 a0 ) 2 = a0 8π 		(13)

Substituting σ into the local DE mass equation, we find its distribution along the radius:

Mde(r) = σ ⋅ 4πr2 = 1 2a0r2 		(14)

Let us substitute the obtained values of Mde(r) and Ccorr into the general mass equation of the local galaxy cavity of radius r. Pulling the linear spatial coordinate r outside for the interference term, we obtain a strict analytical mass distribution in exact fractions and radicals:

M(r) = Mb + Mde(r) + 2 ⋅ (3 4 + δC) ⋅Mb a0 ⋅ r 		(15)

For an ideal average galactic cavern where the local quantum fluctuation is strictly zero (δC = 0), the numerical coefficient before the interference term reduces to the exact fraction 32 4 . The resulting mass equation takes the form:

M(r) = Mb + Mde(r) + 32 4 Mb a0 ⋅ r 		(16)

0.6 Calculation of the Gravitational Force and Derivation of the Tully–Fisher Law

To calculate the local gravitational force (acceleration a) on a circular orbit of a test body, we discard the contribution of the background Dark Energy Mde(r), since a homogeneous vacuum condensate inside a cavity does not create a directed gravitational gradient due to the spherical symmetry of the background. The gravitating part of the system remains the central baryonic core Mb and the emergently induced Dark Matter:

a = Mgrav(r) r2 = 1 r2 (Mb + 32 4 Mb a0 ⋅ r) = Mb r2 + 32 4 Mb a0 r 		(17)

On the far periphery of the galaxy (at large radii r), the Newtonian term rapidly decays as 1 r2, and the second, interference term of Dark Matter, which decays much slower as 1 r, becomes dominant:

a ≈32 4 Mb a0 r 		(18)

The obtained expression exactly reproduces the deep quasi-classical regime of Milgrom’s Modified Newtonian Dynamics (MOND), derived from first quantum-informational principles.

Since a test body (a star) moves in a circular orbit with a velocity v, its centripetal acceleration is kinematically given as a = v2 r . Let us substitute this dependence into the asymptotic force equation:

v2 r = 32 4 Mb a0 r 		(19)

The radial coordinate r on the left and right sides of the equation completely and strictly cancels out:

v2 = 32 4 Mb a0 		(20)

Squaring both sides of the equation, we obtain the final, absolutely exact analytical form of the empirical Tully–Fisher law:

v4 = 9 8Mba0 		(21)

0.7 Conclusion

In the standard cosmological model ΛCDM, the constancy of star velocities on the outskirts of galaxies and the dependence v4 ~ M b are considered a random coincidence requiring individual fitting of the density profiles of hypothetical dark matter particles for each specific system. Within the framework of the stationary Anti-PG model, the Tully–Fisher law is an inevitable consequence of the mean-field quantum interference. The exact numerical coefficient 9 8 before the product of the baryonic mass and the critical acceleration completely resolves the problem of galaxy rotation curves, proving that Dark Matter is not physical particles, but a holographic projection of the elastic response of the vacuum.

Part VII
Part 7. Quantum Complementarity of Horizons and Renormalization of the Verlinde Potential

Abstract. A method for describing quantum cosmology based on the separation of the Universe’s states into a stationary global background and the causal bubble of a local observer is proposed. Within the framework of the complementarity principle of holographic screens, the Universe is interpreted as a set of intersecting and mutually consistent local informational spaces. The nature of the joint development of cosmological horizons, which determine the dynamics of unfolding the total informational capacity of the system from an initial zero state, is investigated. An approach to the renormalization of the local emergent potential is developed, eliminating the problem of infinite divergence of hidden mass at large distances. The method is based on the dynamic balance between the elastic tension of the quantum network and the background acceleration of the vacuum. A spatial limit bounding the region of coherent interaction of informational degrees of freedom is analytically determined. The proposed modified dependence ensures a smooth decay of the hidden gravitational contribution at macroscales, forming a finite and physically stable cosmological structure.

0.1 Fundamental Analogy: Separation of Motions in Quantum Mechanics

In classical and quantum mechanics (QM), the two-body problem is standardly solved via a canonical partitioning of the Schrödinger equation into two independent parts:

1.
Center-of-mass (CM) motion: describes the dynamics of the system as a whole in an external space.
2.
Relative motion: describes the internal quantum dynamics of particles relative to this CM.

Analogously to this quantum separation, the "bag of bits" quantum cosmology performs a rigid splitting of the quantum mechanics of the entire Universe into two conjugated subsystems:

HUniverse = HGlobal_Background ⊗HCausal_Bubble 		(1)

  • Global background: Describes the evolution of the Universe as a whole (analogous to CM motion). At this scale, spacetime is flat, and the total energy of the system is fixed.

  • Causal bubble of the observer: Describes the local physics and geometry for a specific observer who is stationary relative to the cosmic microwave background (analogous to relative motion).

According to the complementarity principle of quantum mechanics, formulated by L. Susskind for holographic screens, every local observer stationary relative to the background possesses their own causal bubble and their own local quantum-informational density matrix. All these observers are absolutely equal. The global "hologram" of the Universe does not exist objectively as a single rigid 3D scene; it is woven from a multitude of complementary, intersecting, and mutually consistent local bubbles of observers.

0.2 Dynamics and Classification of Cosmological Horizons from Zero

In a non-stationary, expanding Universe (especially at the early stages of the quantum-informational network’s evolution), the local de Sitter horizon, the global cosmological event horizon, and the observed particle horizon represent three fundamentally different physical and mathematical entities, forming a triad of informational scales.

1.
De Sitter horizon (Current Hubble radius): Is determined by the instantaneous value of the Hubble parameter at a given moment of time t:

RdS(t) = c H(t) 		(2)

At the singular point of the Big Bang (t → 0), when the Universe is filled with ultra-dense baryonic matter, the parameter H(t) →∞. Consequently, the local Hubble horizon RdS starts from a strict zero. At the early stages, it acts as a dynamic marker of the current expansion phase ("weather" inside the bubble).

2.
Cosmological event horizon (Coordinate R of the model): Is an integral quantity that determines the absolute IR limit of causality "forward in time," and is calculated via an integral over the future time of the scale factor a(t):

RCE(t) = a(t) ∫ t∞ cdt′ a(t′) 		(3)

At the moment of the Big Bang (t = 0), due to the fact that a(0) = 0, this integral, multiplied by the zero scale factor, also vanishes to a strict zero. The informational capacity of the vacuum at this moment is Nbits ~ RCE2 = 0. The variable R on the mass balance graph of Part 10 is precisely the current event horizon, and the dimensionless radius x = R∕L is a measure of the unfolding of the full informational capacity of the bubble to its final value L = c∕H0 in the asymptotic de Sitter future (x = 1), where it and the Hubble radius finally coincide (RdS → RCE → L).

3.
Observed horizon (Particle horizon Rp): Determines the absolute boundary of causality "backward in time" — the maximum distance from which light has already managed to reach the observer from the moment of the Big Bang to the current moment t:

Rp(t) = a(t) ∫ 0t cdt′ a(t′) 		(4)

At the point t = 0, the particle horizon is equal to a strict zero, since the lifetime of the Universe is zero. As it expands, it grows faster than the Hubble radius (Rp ~ t in the matter era), outlining the physical volume of baryons that have entered into informational contact. In our model, the particle horizon determines the size of the "active zone" inside the complementary bubble, where baryons are still capable of coherently interacting with the network before Dark Energy displaces them beyond the boundaries of RCE.

0.3 Conceptual Crisis of the Original Verlinde Model

In the canonical formulation by Erik Verlinde (2016), the cosmological dynamics of unfolding horizons from zero was completely ignored, which led to non-physical consequences. The mathematical failure of his model is as follows:

  • Verlinde postulates the Hubble scale as a static constant of the entire Universe (L = c∕H0), neglecting its evolution. For him, this is simply an immutable size of the "box" in which information is locked.

  • In his basic equation of emergent DM acceleration around an isolated baryonic mass M:

    aDM(r) = a0 ⋅ gN = GMa0 r     		(5)

    the variable r is a local radial coordinate in the volume (the distance from the center of a specific galaxy), rather than the global size of the Universe. Extrapolating the local elastic vacuum response to large distances (r →∞) leads to a linear divergence of the effective dark mass (MDM ~ r), which eventually exceeds the mass of the entire observed Universe.

0.4 Derivation of the Verlinde Cutoff from First Principles via a0

To renormalize Verlinde without fitting parameters, we apply the principle of dynamic balance: the informational TM tail exists only as long as the acceleration of the local network tension (aDM) is able to withstand the acceleration of the vacuum background expansion (aΛ).

The acceleration of the background expansion at a distance r is determined by the critical acceleration of the Universe a0:

aΛ(r) = a02 c2 r 		(6)

Equating the "’signal" of the tension to the "noise" of the vacuum expansion at the breaking point (aDM = aΛ), we obtain the equation:

GMa0 rcut = a02 c2 rcut 		(7)

Squaring both sides and isolating rcut4, we find:

GMa0 rcut2 = a04 c4 rcut2⟹rcut4 = c4GM a03 		(8)

Extracting the fourth root, we obtain the true DM cutoff radius, expressed strictly through first principles and the vacuum viscosity parameter a0:

rcut = c ⋅GM a03 		(9)

0.5 Final Renormalized Acceleration Formula

The introduction of this radius into the model allows for a smooth "extinction" of the infinite Verlinde tail at the scale where the elasticity of the local network is torn apart by the vacuum expansion. The modified total acceleration takes a self-consistent form:

aeff(r) = GM r2 + GMa0 r ⋅ exp ⁡ (- r4 rcut4 ) 		(10)

0.6 Conclusion

Thanks to the QM complementarity principle, each observer is locked inside their own quantum bubble, where three types of horizons (RdS, RCE, Rp) synchronously unfold from a strict zero. Within this dynamic frame, emergent Dark Matter works as an elastic tension of vacuum qubits, but strictly up to the radius rcut derived from the acceleration balance. Beyond this scale, the vacuum expansion a0 completely nullifies the coherence of the "tail," turning the Verlinde potential into a finite, physically safe cosmological structure.

Part VIII
Part 8. Description of the Jordan–Brans–Dicke Theory and Conformal Frames in the Context of Generalized Geometric Non-linearity

Abstract. A theory of gravitation with a dynamic dimensional scalar field replacing fixed physical constants is considered. Based on the Legendre transformation, an exact mathematical connection is established between the generalized geometric non-linearity of the vacuum and the representation of the scalar-tensor interaction in which the masses of elementary particles remain constant. A method for the conformal transition to a description system with a linear gravitational sector and a constant gravitational coupling, accompanied by a scale change of the four-dimensional volume, is described. It is shown that in the resulting metric, particle masses acquire an exponential dependence on coordinates, while the potential of the original geometric non-linearity is completely absorbed by the potential of the canonical field. The dynamic spontaneous generation of the cosmological term in the form of a decaying profile of the quintessence field without an independent static vacuum tension is proven. It is established that the density of the emergent energy decreases exponentially with time, providing an automatic regularization of the vacuum spectrum.

In modern gravitational physics and quantum cosmology, the Jordan–Brans–Dicke (JBD) theory [1] serves as a fundamental basis for describing systems with scalar fields. Within this approach, one and the same physical reality can be represented in two mathematically equivalent but conceptually different frames (systems of description): the Jordan frame and the Einstein frame [2]. The transition between them is implemented via a local scale stretching of the spacetime grid — a conformal transformation of the metric tensor [3].

0.1 Jordan–Brans–Dicke Theory (Jordan Frame)

In the canonical Jordan frame of the Jordan–Brans–Dicke theory, the gravitational interaction is described without an a priori introduction of Newtonian constant gravity G and the cosmological constant Λ. Their role at the fundamental level is assumed by a dynamic dimensional scalar field Φ(x) [1,4].

Definition of basic parameters:

  • Scalar field Φ(x): A field having the dimensionality of mass squared ([M2]) in the natural system of units (where ℏ = c = 1). Physically, it represents the reciprocal of the effective gravitational constant at a given point in spacetime: Φ ~ 1∕Geff.

  • Parameter ω0: A dimensionless fundamental coupling constant of the Brans–Dicke theory ([M0]). It determines the relative contribution ("weight") of the kinetic energy of the scalar field in the overall energy balance of the system [1].

The pure action of the Jordan–Brans–Dicke theory in the Jordan frame is written in the following form:

SJBD = ∫ d4x-g [ΦR -ω0 Φ gμν∂ μΦ∂νΦ - 2U(Φ)] + Smatter[gμν] 		(1)

where R is the Ricci scalar curvature (dimensionality [M2]), U(Φ) is the self-interaction potential of the scalar field Φ (dimensionality [M4]), and Smatter[gμν] is the action of ordinary matter, which is minimally coupled exclusively to the metric gμν.

Physical meaning of the Jordan frame and emergent constants:
In the Jordan frame, the masses of all elementary particles of matter are strictly constant (m = const) [2]. Particles move along standard geodesic lines, and the energy-momentum tensor of matter satisfies the canonical conservation law (∇ ⁡μTμν = 0).

Since fixed constants of gravity and vacuum tension are absent in the action (1) itself, they are strictly determined below — as emergent quantities depending on the parameters of the formula and the vacuum expectation value of the field in the modern epoch (where the average value of Φ is Φ0 ≈const):

1.
The effective Newtonian gravitational constant Geff is expressed through the parameters of the equation as [1,4]:

Geff = 1 Φ0 (4 + 2ω0 3 + 2ω0 ) 		(2)

As ω0 →∞, the scalar field loses its dynamics, turning into a rigid constant, and the Jordan–Brans–Dicke theory transitions exactly into Einstein’s General Theory of Relativity (GR), where Geff = 1∕Φ0.

2.
The effective cosmological constant Λ is completely determined by the value of the potential U on the vacuum background of the field [2]:

Λeff = U(Φ0) Φ0 		(3)

0.2 Generalized Non-linearity f(R) and Its Connection to the Jordan Frame

To completely eliminate the arbitrariness of manually constructing potentials, we expand the narrow interpretation of non-linear gravity modifications, which is often limited to the specific Starobinsky inflationary model (R + αR2). In the general case of an arbitrary geometric non-linearity of the vacuum given by a function of the scalar curvature f(R), the Lagrangian in the original representation is formulated as a pure modified theory, initially free of scalar fields and Λ [5]:

SJ = ∫ d4x-g [ f(R) 16πG0 ] 		(4)

The mathematical connection of this non-linearity with the Jordan frame of the Jordan–Brans–Dicke theory is realized via the classical Legendre transformation method [5]. This method allows equivalently replacing the non-linear dependence on the higher derivatives of the metric by introducing an auxiliary scalar field.

We can rewrite the original action by introducing a new independent scalar field χ (which has the physical meaning of an auxiliary curvature) and linearizing the function f(R) in the vicinity of this point using a first-order Taylor expansion:

SJ = ∫ d4x-g [f(χ) + f′(χ)(R - χ) 16πG0 ] 		(5)

If we vary this action with respect to the field χ, we obtain the equation of motion for this variable:

f″(χ)(R - χ) = 0 		(6)

Since for any truly non-linear theory the second derivative of the function is non-zero (f″(R)≠0), this equation rigidly fixes the relation: χ = R. Substituting χ = R back, we return to the original non-linear form f(R). This proves the mathematical identity of the two Lagrangians.

Let us rearrange the terms in the linearized action to explicitly highlight the structure of the Jordan frame:

SJ = ∫ d4x-g [f′(χ)R - (χf′(χ) - f(χ)) 16πG0 ] 		(7)

Now we can make a direct change of variables, defining the canonical Jordan–Brans–Dicke scalar field Φ as the derivative (local slope) of the original geometric non-linearity [5]:

Φ ≡ f′(χ) 16πG0 = f′(R) 16πG0 		(8)

Since the field Φ is now directly and non-minimally multiplied by the scalar curvature ΦR, this action by definition becomes the action of the Jordan–Brans–Dicke theory in the Jordan frame:

SJ = ∫ d4x-g [ΦR - 2U(Φ)] + Smatter[g μν] 		(9)

In this case, the Brans–Dicke coupling parameter ω0 automatically turns out to be equal to zero (ω0 = 0), since explicit kinetic terms of the scalar fields (∂Φ)2 were absent in the original purely geometric action f(R) [5].

All the non-linearity of the original function f(R), which previously resided in the geometry, has now been completely transferred into the internal self-interaction potential of the field U(Φ). Comparing the Lagrangians, we obtain the exact formula for this potential [5]:

U(Φ) = 1 2 [R(Φ)Φ - f(R(Φ)) 16πG0 ] 		(10)

To calculate this potential for a specific theory, one needs to express the curvature as a function of the field (R = R(Φ) as an inverse function) and substitute the resulting expression into the potential formula. The cosmological constant Lambda does not need to be introduced at this stage — it is bound to emerge on its own during the next stage of conformal transformations.

0.3 Conformal Transition to the Einstein Frame

To linearize the gravitational sector and eliminate the non-minimal coupling ΦR, a transition to the Einstein frame is performed via a conformal transformation of the metric tensor [2,3]. The dimensionless scale factor Ω2(x) is chosen to be equal to the scalar field Φ itself, normalized to the vacuum value Φ0:

g~μν = Ω2g μν = Φ Φ0gμν 		(11)

-g~ = ( Φ Φ0 ) 2-g 		(12)

In four-dimensional spacetime, the exact differential-geometric relation connecting the scalar curvatures of the two frames has the form [3]:

R = Φ Φ0 (R~ + 3□~ (ln ⁡ Φ Φ0 ) -3 2g~μν∂ μ (ln ⁡ Φ Φ0 ) ∂ν (ln ⁡ Φ Φ0 )) 		(13)

where □~ is the canonical d’Alembertian wave operator constructed with respect to the Einstein metric g~μν.

0.4 Action in the Einstein Frame and Dynamic Generation of Lambda

Substituting the curvature coupling formula (13) into the original action and integrating the total derivative by parts brings the Lagrangian to the canonical linear Hilbert–Einstein form [2]:

SE = ∫ d4x-g~ ( R~ 16πG -1 2g~μν∂ μϕ∂νϕ - V (ϕ)) 		(14)

Physical meaning of the Einstein frame and difference from the Jordan frame:

1.
The gravitational constant G: Becomes a strictly constant world constant (expressed through Φ0 according to the equation G = G0 Φ0 ), fixing the geometric scale of curvature.
2.
Masses of matter particles run: Due to the scale distortion of the metric (11), the spacetime interval shrinks. The mass of any fermion or boson becomes a function of the new field, running depending on the coordinates [2]:

meff(ϕ) = m0 Φ(ϕ)∕Φ0 = m0 ⋅ exp ⁡ (- 4πG 3 + 2ω0ϕ) 		(15)
