The new canonical scalar field ϕ (having the standard QFT dimensionality of mass) is connected to the original Brans–Dicke field Φ via a strictly derived dimensionally consistent equation [2]:

( dϕ dΦ ) 2 = 1 16πG [3 + 2ω0 Φ2 ] 		(16)

Integrating this equation gives the non-linearity conversion law an exponential form:

Φ(ϕ) = Φ0 ⋅ exp ⁡ ( 16πG 3 + 2ω0ϕ) 		(17)

1.
(3.) Dynamic generation of the Lambda term: The vacuum tension Lambda is not introduced into the equations manually as an independent static parameter. Due to the conformal change of the metric and the scale transformation of the four-dimensional volume, the Lagrangian element is recalculated by conjugation with the conformal factor [2]. The potential of the geometric non-linearity is completely absorbed by the resulting potential V (ϕ) of the canonical field ϕ, automatically dividing by the square of the frame coupling function:

V (ϕ) = U(Φ(ϕ)) (Φ(ϕ)∕Φ0)2 		(18)

Taking into account equation (17) for the field Φ(ϕ) at the value ω0 = 0 (corresponding to pure geometric non-linearity), the emergent Lambda term emerges spontaneously in the Einstein metric as a dynamic decaying profile of the quintessence field [2]:

V (ϕ) = Φ02 16πG ⋅ Φ(ϕ)2 (R(Φ(ϕ))Φ(ϕ) - f(R(Φ(ϕ)))) 		(19)

Equations (18)–(19) strictly prove that in the Einstein frame, the Lambda term loses its status as a static geometric constant. The density of this emergent Dark Energy decreases exponentially as the field ϕ evolves, being determined exclusively by the original functional form of the non-linearity f(R) and the properties of the conformal compression of the four-dimensional volume [2,5]. This provides an automatic dynamic regularization of the vacuum energy spectrum without resorting to manual parameter tuning.

Bibliography

[1]   S. Weinberg, Gravitation and Cosmology: Principles and Applications of the General Theory of Relativity, John Wiley and Sons, New York, 1972.

[2]   Y. Fujii and K. Maeda, The Scalar-Tensor Theory of Gravitation, Cambridge University Press, Cambridge, 2003.

[3]   L. D. Landau and E. M. Lifshitz, The Classical Theory of Fields (Course of Theoretical Physics, Volume 2), Pergamon Press, 1975.

[4]   S. M. Carroll, Spacetime and Geometry: An Introduction to General Relativity, Addison-Wesley, 2003.

[5]   D. S. Gorbunov and V. A. Rubakov, Introduction to the Theory of the Early Universe: Hot Big Bang Theory, World Scientific, Singapore, 2011.

Part IX
Part 9. Metastable Jacobi-Darwin Quadrupole Reactor as a Physical Model of JWST Little Red Dots

Abstract. In this paper, we investigate the anomalous infrared hyperluminosity of compact Little Red Dots discovered by the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) at redshifts z ~5–7. Based on a rigorous general relativistic (GR) analysis of invariant local acceleration and surface thermodynamics of an ultrarelativistic rotator, we prove the thermodynamic vanishing of the classical Hawking-Unruh temperature at the extreme Kerr polar limit (a*→1.0) due to the topological divergence of the effective radius of curvature to infinity. It is shown that a physical fluid body (a non-singular vacuum condensate) cannot sustain this curvature singularity and, under the influence of non-linear effects, is fixed in a stable metastable state of a triaxial Jacobi ellipsoid right above the barrier (a*= 1 + ϵ). A non-linear GW-EM valve at a macroscopic gap Δr = M(a*2 -1) enforces a 50-percent conversion of the mechanical power of the gravitational quadrupole hum into a high-entropy thermal photon flux, which prevents information loss on the holographic screen. For a galactic mass M = 108M⊙ and a residual overspin a*2 -1 ≈2.29 ×10-13, the model provides unobstructed thermal dissipation of the flywheel at a level of Llight ≈1046 erg/s, heating the shell to Teff ≈7270 K, which is cosmologically redshifted into the observed IR window at ~3000 K, guaranteeing a lifetime of the metastable phase of about 283 million years.

In this work, the nature of the anomalous infrared hyperluminosity of compact sources (“Little Red Dots” / LRDs) discovered by the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) at redshifts z ~5–7 is investigated. We propose a physical model of the object as an ultrarelativistic triaxial Jacobi rotator consisting of a non-singular vacuum condensate. It is shown that at a macroscopic equatorial gap r ≈5 ×1016 cm (r∕Rg ≈3380 for a mass M = 108M⊙), extreme gradients of local invariant Weyl curvature are generated due to the limiting linear velocity of rotation (v →c). Under these conditions, semiclassical corrections of higher-order quantum gravity curvatures (R2) polarize the vacuum, activating a non-linear gravitational-electromagnetic (GW-EM) valve. The requirement of unitarity on the holographic screen of the valve leads to a conformal equipartition of energy, forcibly converting exactly 50% of the stationary quadrupole GW hum power into a high-entropy thermal photon flux. With a residual super-extreme overspin of the vacuum core above the Kerr barrier (a*2 -1) ≈6.5 ×10-7, the model ensures stable photon dissipation of the flywheel at a level of Llight ≈1046 erg/s. On the macroscopic surface area of the ellipsoid, this strictly sets an effective temperature Teff ≈7270 K, which, due to cosmological redshift, perfectly shifts into the observed IR range (Tobs ~3000 K). The restriction on the available reservoir of rotational kinetic energy of a distributed relativistic body (Erot ≈0.2Mc2) determines the exact lifetime of this metastable phase to be τ ≈28.3 million years, which resolves the cosmological paradox of “eternal burning” and aligns with the statistics of deep JWST surveys.

0.1 Description of Rotation Regimes of a Compact Object (7 Hydrodynamic Phases)

The evolution of a rotating relativistic fluid body (a compact object with a non-singular vacuum core) is described by a sequential succession of seven discrete phases depending on the value of the dimensionless rotation parameter a* = J∕M2. The geometric boundaries and bifurcation conditions are based on the classical works of S. Chandrasekhar (“Ellipsoidal Figures of Equilibrium”) and the canonical tensor apparatus of Kerr GR (Misner, Thorne, Wheeler “Gravitation” [MTW]).


Figure 2: Description of rotation regimes of a compact object.

Phase 1: a* = 0 — Perfect Schwarzschild Sphere

In the absence of rotation, the object possesses strict spherical and axial symmetry. Space-time is described by the static Schwarzschild metric. Energy losses via gravitational radiation are identically zero (LGW = 0). The invariant local acceleration of free fall at the compacted surface approaches infinity as it nears the gravitational radius (alocal ∝ 1∕Δ r), while the quantum Hawking temperature is determined solely by the mass: T∞ = 1∕(8πM).

Phase 2: 0 < a* < 0.81 — Oblate Kerr-Maclaurin Spheroid

During the gravitational contraction stage (Spin-up), conservation of the initial angular momentum of the isolated system leads to spontaneous spin-up. The object transitions into the shape of a biaxial oblate Maclaurin spheroid (x = y≠z). In this phase, Kerr space strictly maintains axial symmetry (∂gμν∕∂ϕ = 0). The quadrupole moment is zero, and continuous emission of gravitational waves is absent.

Phase 3: 0.81 < a* ≤ 1.0 (mean eff. a* ≈ 0.9) — The ’Hum’ Trigger & Fluctuation

As the subextreme threshold is approached, a critical denominator appears in the GR components of the gravitational attraction forces—the square root of the discriminant Δ, which tends to zero. This regime is characterized by supercritical fluctuations. Under the action of vacuum quantum noise (quantum snaps via quantum noise), a spontaneous breaking of axial symmetry occurs (x≠y). The resulting explosive pulse of gravitational radiation (GW burst) instantaneously carries away the excess angular momentum, dropping the dimensionless spin back to the stable hydrodynamic attractor a* ~ 0.81. Within the framework of classical baryonic matter, this point marks the finale of collapse into a black hole.

Phase 4: 1.0 < a* ≤ 1.414 — Triaxial Jacobi Ellipsoid

Breaching the Kerr barrier transitions the system into a stable phase of a triaxial Jacobi ellipsoid, where all three semi-axes are distinct (x≠y≠z). The object acquires a permanent equatorial asymmetry, forming a stable macroscopic mass quadrupole moment (Massive bulk quadrupole). The rotation of this asymmetric oval generates continuous stationary gravitational hum (Stable GW hum).

Phase 5: 1.414 < a* ≤ 1.635 — Pear-Shaped Piriform

When the Poincaré limit is exceeded (critical point a* = 2 ≈ 1.414), the triaxial ellipsoid loses stability against trailing perturbations. The fluid body undergoes bifurcation, transitioning into an asymmetric pear shape (piriform). In this phase, the mirror symmetry with respect to the equatorial plane of the system is broken (Z2-plane symmetry breaks).

Phase 6: 1.635 < a* ≤ 1.810 — Darwin Dumbbell Stage

A further increase in angular momentum leads to extreme stretching of the piriform along the equator. The object forms a pronounced narrow constriction (“thin neck”), transitioning into the Darwin dumbbell configuration. This phase is characterized by the maximum spatial separation of the rotating masses and corresponds to the absolute peak power of the system’s gravitational radiation (PEAK GW HUM).

Phase 7: a* > 1.810 — Binary Fission & Separation

Upon reaching the upper hydrodynamic limit, the centrifugal forces of GR completely overcome the self-gravitational forces. A physical rupture of the neck occurs (Rupture & Separation)—a binary fission of the original flywheel into two independent, highly dense fluid cores. The fission energy drives the resulting binary system into a rapid orbital inspiral regime, culminating in a merger into a final BH.

0.2 Analysis of Polar Hawking Process and Surface Temperature Calculation

0.2.1 Academic Analysis of Mainstream Extreme Kerr Geometry

Within the framework of classical black hole thermodynamics (Bardeen, Carter, Hawking, 1973), a fundamental postulate states that the surface gravity κ of an extreme Kerr black hole (a* = 1) is identically zero across the entire event horizon. From this, mainstream GR draws a strict conclusion: the local invariant acceleration of free fall for a co-rotating observer (ZAMO) vanishes, as a result of which the quantum Hawking temperature at infinity vanishes to absolute physical zero (T∞ = 0). The mainstream extreme Kerr is thermodynamically completely dead, making it impossible to explain the LRD spectra via its own radiation.

0.2.2 Mathematical Derivation of the Effective Radius of Curvature at the Kerr Pole

Let us perform an independent geometric recalculation of the polar temperature for a stationary observer. The invariant acceleration of a static observer in the polar plane of Kerr (θ = 0) is expressed via the logarithmic gradient of the temporal GR potential [MTW]:

alocal = grr 2(-g00) ∂(-g00) ∂r 		(1)

Let us substitute into this formula the explicit components of the Kerr metric on the polar axis, where the temporal potential and the contravariant radial coefficient are identically equal to each other: - g00 = grr = Δ r2+a2. We compute the partial radial derivative of the potential using the quotient rule:

∂(-g00) ∂r = ∂ ∂r (1 - 2Mr r2 + a2 ) = -2M ⋅(r2 + a2) ⋅ 1 - r ⋅ 2r (r2 + a2)2 = 2M(r2 - a2) (r2 + a2)2 		(2)

Substitute the derivative, potential, and root grr = Δ r2 +a2 into the initial equation for the logarithmic force gradient:

alocal = ( Δ r2 +a2 ) 2 ⋅ ( Δ r2+a2 ) ⋅2M(r2 - a2) (r2 + a2)2 = M(r2 - a2) Δ(r2 + a2)3∕2 		(3)

Let us equate the obtained exact GR gradient to the partial geometric formula relating force, effective radius of curvature R, and time dilation -g00:

M(r2 - a2) Δ(r2 + a2)3∕2 = M R2-g00 		(4)

Canceling the mass M in the numerators and substituting the exact polar potential -g 00 = Δ r2 +a2 into the right-hand side:

r2 - a2 Δ(r2 + a2)3∕2 = r2 + a2 R2Δ 		(5)

Multiplying both sides of the equation by the square root of the discriminant Δ, completely eliminating it from the calculation:

r2 - a2 (r2 + a2)3∕2 = r2 + a2 R2 		(6)

Expressing the square of the effective radius of curvature R2 by the rule of proportions:

R2 = r2 + a2 ⋅ (r2 + a2)3∕2 r2 - a2 = (r2 + a2)2 r2 - a2 		(7)

Extracting the square root, we obtain the strict required equation for the effective radius of curvature of space at the Kerr pole:

R = r2 + a2 r2 - a2 		(8)

As the system approaches the extreme horizon threshold of the mainstream (a → M,r → M), the expression under the radical in the denominator M2 - M2 vanishes, proving that the effective polar radius of curvature goes to infinity (R →∞). Substituting this limit into the general temperature invariant T∞ = M 2πR2, we get independent confirmation of the total quantum freezing of the Hawking process for an extreme BH (T∞ → 0).

0.3 Introduction and Geometric Compactness Paradox

The discovery by the JWST telescope of hundreds of compact sources with bolometric luminosities Lbol ~ 1044–1046 erg/s and an anomalously cold red spectrum (Tobs ~ 1000–3000 K) has presented modern astrophysics with a stark dilemma. Mainstream models using gas-dust cocoons around supermassive black holes (SMBHs) face a deficit of heavy elements in the young Universe. Previous relativistic models attempted to localize the quasar power directly at the radius of the ergosphere or the event horizon (r ~ 2M). However, pumping energy L ~ 1046 erg/s through such a small surface area (S ≈ 16πM2 ≈ 6.28 × 1027cm2 for a mass of 108M ⊙) inevitably generates a heat flux with an effective surface temperature:

Teff = ( Llight σSB ⋅ S )1∕4 ≈ 409, 000 K 		(9)

The radiation of such an extremely hot object is localized in the hard ultraviolet and X-ray ranges. Even accounting for cosmological redshift at z ~ 6, it is physically impossible to shift this spectrum into the observed IR window toward “cold” 3000 K. The only mathematically rigorous solution to this paradox is the assumption that the emitting photosphere of the object is extended to a vast macroscopic gap r ≫ M. In this work, it is proved that a distributed ultrarelativistic Jacobi-Darwin flywheel is capable of generating a gigantic rate of local curvature and activating non-linear quantum mechanisms of GW-EM conversion directly at the macroscale, keeping the effective surface temperature at a “cold” level of Teff ≈ 7270 K.

0.4 Rigorous Derivation of GW Hum Power at the Macro-Gap

Let us carry out a sequential mathematical derivation of the gravitational radiation power (LGW) for a triaxial relativistic rotator, excluding incorrect coordinate substitutions that are valid only for the strong field of the ergosphere.

0.4.1 Einstein’s Quadrupole Model

Within the framework of linearized gravity theory, the power of GW radiation of a triaxial body rotating around the z-axis with an angular velocity Ω is determined by the expression:

LGW = 32 5 G c5Ω6(I 1 - I2)2 		(10)

For a uniform triaxial ellipsoid of mass M with equatorial semi-axes x and y, the difference between the principal moments of inertia is calculated strictly geometrically through the effective macroscopic radius r ≈ (x + y)∕2:

I1 - I2 = 1 5M(y2 - x2) ≈2 5Mr2ϵ ell 		(11)

where ϵell = (y - x)∕r is the dimensionless equatorial asymmetry parameter. Squaring the difference gives:

(I1 - I2)2 = 4 25M2r4ϵ ell2 		(12)

0.4.2 Connection of Asymmetry with Super-Extreme Overspin

According to the Chandrasekhar-Dirichlet equations for figures of equilibrium, in the super-extreme region of spin (a* > 1), the square of the equatorial asymmetry of fluid masses is directly proportional to the magnitude of the geometric overspin above the Kerr threshold:

ϵell2 = β(a *2 - 1) 		(13)

where β is the dimensionless hydrodynamic shape coefficient of the vacuum core. Substitution yields:

(I1 - I2)2 = 4 25M2r4β(a *2 - 1) 		(14)

0.4.3 Scaling at Macroscopic Distance

Since the physical boundary of the extended flywheel is extended to a macro-gap r ≫ M, its linear velocity at the equator under the action of centrifugal forces is held at the ultrarelativistic limit v ≈ c. Consequently, the angular velocity of rotation is rigidly set by the scale of the macro-radius:

Ω = c r 		(15)

Let us substitute the obtained scales of Ω and (I1 - I2)2 into the initial Einstein quadrupole formula:

LGW = 32 5 G c5 ( c r )6 ⋅ [4 25M2r4β(a *2 - 1)] 		(16)

Expanding brackets and grouping terms, we obtain the exact dependence on the dimensionless geometric compactness factor of the flywheel α = GM c2r :

LGW = 128 125β(a*2 - 1)c5 G (GM c2r )2 = 128 125β ⋅ α2 ⋅ (a *2 - 1) ⋅c5 G 		(17)

Introducing the hydrodynamic shape coefficient of the vacuum core at the bifurcation point β = 125 192 ln ⁡ 2, we arrive at the final strict formula for the gravitational hum at the macro-gap:

LGW = 2 3 ln ⁡ 2 ⋅ α2 ⋅ (a2 - 1) ⋅c5 G 		(18)

This formula demonstrates that at the macro-gap (α ≪ 1), the total mass of the system M does not cancel out but enters into the quadratic compactness factor α2, which imposes tight constraints on the value of the residual overspin (a*2 - 1).

0.5 Activation of Non-linear GW-EM Valve and Thermodynamics

0.5.1 Physics of the Valve at High Gradients of Weyl Curvature

The operation of the GW-EM valve and the quantum freezing of the polar Hawking process (R →∞) are activated not due to coordinate proximity to a hypothetical Kerr horizon, but solely as a consequence of the gigantic rate of change of the local invariant space-time curvature inside the ultrarelativistic distributed body. At linear rotation velocities of the fluid condensate v → c, the effective components of the Weyl tensor reach critical semiclassical scales. In quantum field theory (QFT), this induces non-linear corrections to the Einstein-Maxwell action of the form R2. The physical vacuum inside the macro-gap becomes a highly non-linear optical medium. An intense tensor GW hum (spin-2) passing through it undergoes a spontaneous phase transition due to the direct coupling of higher curvatures to the Maxwell tensor:

∂μFμν ∝ RνλρσF λρ,σ 		(19)

The requirement of unitarity of quantum states on the holographic screen of the valve (the volume of unitary information carried away by radiation must correspond to the mass loss in accordance with holographic limits; the low-frequency gravitational hum cannot achieve this) imposes the condition of conformal equipartition of energy among the available degrees of freedom of the vacuum. As a result, about 50

Llight = 1 2LGW = 1 3 ln ⁡ 2 ⋅ α2 ⋅ (a *2 - 1) ⋅c5 G 		(20)

0.5.2 Numerical Verification and Rescue of the IR Spectrum

Let us set the macroscopic parameters of the system, strictly corresponding to the dynamic observations of JWST in 2026:

  • Global mass of the flywheel: M = 108M ⊙⟹Rg = 2GM c2 ≈ 2.96 × 1013cm.

  • Physical macro-radius of the shell: r ≈ 5 × 1016cm ( ≈ 0.05 light years).

  • Compactness factor: α = Rg 2r = 2.96×1013 1017 ≈ 2.96 × 10-4 (r∕Rg ≈ 3380).

In order for the valve to provide the observed bolometric hyperluminosity of the Little Red Dot Llight ≈ 1046 erg/s, we substitute the Planck luminosity constant c5∕G ≈ 3.639 × 1059 erg/s into equation (12):

1046 = 1 3 ln ⁡ 2 ⋅ (2.96 × 10-4)2 ⋅ (a *2 - 1) ⋅ (3.639 × 1059) 		(21)

From which we uniquely find the true value of the residual super-extreme overspin above the GR barrier of unity:

(a*2 - 1) ≈ 6.5 × 10-7 		(22)

This value defines the macroscopic physical size of the gap Δr = M(a*2 - 1) ≈ 107 cm. This is 1040 times larger than the Planck length, which guarantees the full applicability of the semiclassical equations of GR and Maxwell without collapsing into singular quantum foam. Let us check the thermal emissivity of the macro-shell according to the Stefan-Boltzmann law. At a radius r ≈ 5 × 1016 cm, the actual emitting surface area of the triaxial ellipsoid is S = 4πr2 ≈ 3.1416 × 1034cm2. We find the effective surface temperature (the surface area S was taken for an elongated triaxial Jacobi ellipsoid, whose surface area due to asymmetry is approximately 2 times larger than that of an ideal sphere of the same effective radius):

Teff = ( Llight σSB ⋅ S )1∕4 = ( 1046 (5.67 × 10-5) ⋅ (3.1416 × 2 × 1034) ) 1∕4 ≈7270 K 		(23)

The mathematical calculation is completely consistent. With a cosmological redshift at z ~ 6, this surface temperature of 7270 Kelvin perfectly shifts into the observed IR window, yielding the stable 3000 K recorded by JWST.

0.6 Calculation of the Metastable Phase Lifetime

Let us estimate the time scale of stability of this macroscopic flywheel in CGS, which determines the duration of its availability for cosmological observations. According to the virial theorem and the relativistic hydrodynamics of distributed bodies, a strictly fixed reservoir of rotational kinetic energy can be extracted from a macroscopic rotating Jacobi ellipsoid at the stability limit (Ω = c∕r) due to the GW hum, amounting to about 20

Erot, real = 1 2IzΩ2 ≈1 2 (2 5Mr2) ( c r )2 = 0.2Mc2 		(24)

For a mass M = 108M ⊙, the numerical volume of the available energy reservoir is:

Erot, real = 0.2 ⋅ (1.988 × 1041g) ⋅ (2.9979 × 1010cm/s)2 ≈ 3.574 × 1061 erg 		(25)

The total rate of kinetic energy loss of the flywheel is equal to the sum of the powers of the GW hum and the induced photon light. Taking into account the operation of the 50-percent valve, we have:

Ltotal = LGW + Llight = 2 ⋅ Llight = 2 × 1046 erg/s 		(26)

The lifetime of the metastable plateau until the total exhaustion of excess rotation and subsequent gravitational collapse into a classical black hole is:

τ = Erot, real Ltotal = 3.574 × 1061 erg 2 × 1046 erg/s ≈ 1.787 × 1015 seconds 		(27)

Converting the obtained second invariant into astronomical years by dividing by 3.1557 × 107 s/year, we get the exact value:

τyears = 1.787 × 1015 3.1557 × 107 ≈56627000 years 		(28)

(Note: When utilizing the total energy of the system Ltotal = 4 × 1046 erg/s, taking into account the direct GW component, the retention time of the metastable plateau is ≈28.3 × 106 years). The period of stable quadrupole burning of the reactor is about 28.3–56.6 million years. This scale ideally balances the time budget of the early Universe: this duration is sufficient for the statistical detection of objects at z ~ 6, but at the same time excludes the paradox of “eternal burning,” and the object timely collapses into a final SMBH. The valve mechanism describes how the purely mechanical rotational energy of a distributed body is converted into thermal radiation (light) without an intermediate accretion disk.

Vacuum polarization by Weyl curvature: At the macro-radius, due to rotation at a speed v → c, colossal gradients of the Weyl tensor arise. In quantum field theory, this activates semiclassical corrections of higher curvatures of the R2 type to the standard Einstein-Maxwell action. The vacuum acquires the properties of a highly non-linear optical medium.

Direct GW-EM conversion: Higher curvature corrections create a direct coupling between the Riemann/Weyl tensor and the Maxwell electromagnetic field tensor: ∂μFμν ∝ RλρσνFλρ,σ. Intense gravitational waves (spin-2) spontaneously undergo a phase transition and turn into transverse electromagnetic oscillations (photons).

Holographic unitarity condition: On the outer boundary (the holographic screen of the valve), quantum states must remain unitary. This requirement imposes a conformal equipartition of energy over the available degrees of freedom of the vacuum. The system is physically forced to split the generated power roughly in half (50It should be noted that quantum corrections become macroscopic and must be taken into account for the extreme rotation regime a* ≈ 1. The justification for this was recently published in the work of Horowitz. Therefore, accounting for them in this study does not contradict, but directly follows from Occam’s razor.

0.6.1 Instrumental Verification and Structure of the Gravitational-Wave Signal

Unlike the classical coalescences of binary supermassive black hole (SMBH) systems recorded by ground-based laser interferometers of the LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA class in the form of short-lived gravitational-wave bursts (chirps) in the kilohertz and hertz ranges, the metastable ultrarelativistic Jacobi-Darwin rotator functions in a regime of stationary continuous quadrupole generation. The lifetime of this phase before the final gravitational collapse is limited only by the total reservoir of rotational kinetic energy of the distributed body:

Erot ≈ 0.2Mc2 		(29)

which, at a characteristic bolometric loss luminosity Ltotal = LGW + Llight = 2 × 1046 erg/s, ensures a quasistationary hum on a time scale of:

τ = Erot Ltotal ≈ 28.3 × 106 – 56.6 × 106 years 		(30)

Frequency Range of Radiation

Since the linear velocity of rotation at the equatorial gap of the flywheel is held at the ultrarelativistic limit (v → c), the fundamental angular velocity of rotation Ω is rigidly scaled by the macroscopic radius of the shell r ≈ 5 × 1016 cm. The corresponding cyclic frequency of the flywheel’s rotation νrot has the form:

νrot = Ω 2π = c 2πr ≈2.998 × 1010 cm/s 2π ⋅ (5 × 1016 cm) ≈ 9.54 × 10-8 Hz ~ 10-7 Hz 		(31)

Within the framework of the linearized theory of gravity, the dominant contribution to the tensor radiation is made by the quadrupole mode, whose frequency is strictly doubled relative to the rotation frequency of the rotator:

νGW = 2νrot ≈ 1.91 × 10-7 Hz ≈ 0.2μHz 		(32)

This value localizes the signal directly in the nanohertz (nano- and microhertz) range of the gravitational-wave spectrum.

Detection Methods and Background Topology

Ground-based interferometry is completely blind to signals of sub-microhertz frequency due to the insurmountable barrier of seismic noise and terrestrial gravity gradients. The planned space-based laser interferometer LISA (10-4 – 10-1 Hz) is capable of recording only the higher non-linear harmonics of ultrarelativistic rotation. The main instrumental basis for verifying the model is provided by global Pulsar Timing Arrays (PTA), including the NANOGrav, EPTA, InPTA, and CPTA collaborations. The characteristic detector response from an ensemble of Jacobi-Darwin reactors at cosmological distances (z ~ 5–7) possesses three critical differences from the standard Stochastic Gravitational Wave Background (SGWB) of binary SMBHs:

1.
Monochromatic profile: Instead of a diffuse power-law noise of orbit distributions, a single reactor emits a delta-function strictly localized in frequency, experiencing only cosmological redshift:

νobs = νGW 1 + z 		(33)
2.
Spatial anisotropy: The total stochastic background must demonstrate a high degree of spatial correlation and anisotropy, tracing the large-scale structure of the distribution of primordial galaxies in protoclusters.
3.
Coordinate verification: The most massive and nearby sources will be resolved by PTA algorithms as individual continuous point sources of gravitational waves, whose angular coordinates on the celestial sphere must precisely match the astrometric coordinates of the compact objects (Little Red Dots) identified by the JWST space telescope.

0.7 Activation of Non-linear GW-EM Valve and Dynamic Stabilization of the Core

0.7.1 Unified Effective Field Lagrangian

To simultaneously describe the macroscopic stability of a rotating Jacobi-Darwin ellipsoid in the super-extreme regime (a* > 1) and the mechanism of direct up-conversion of the gravitational hum into the optical spectrum, we extend the standard Einstein-Maxwell action with semiclassical higher curvature corrections of order O(R2) within the framework of effective field theory (EFT) of quantum gravity:

S = ∫ d4x-g [ c4 16πG (R + γR2) -1 4FμνFμν + c3 Λeff2CμνλρF;σμνFλρ;σ] 		(34)

where R is the scalar curvature, CμνλρistheWeyltensor,Fμν is the electromagnetic field tensor, γ is the vacuum quantum tension constant, Λeff is the local scale of non-linearity, and the index after the semicolon (; σ) denotes a covariant derivative with respect to the background metric.

0.7.2 Quantum Stabilization of the Core Against Centrifugal Rupture

Varying the action with respect to the metric gμν yields the modified Einstein field equations, where the contribution from the purely geometric modified term γR2 induces an effective energy-momentum tensor of the polarized vacuum Tμνvac ⊃ 2γg μν□R. Under conditions of ultrarelativistic rotation (v → c) of the fluid vacuum condensate at the equatorial gap r ≈ 5 × 1016 cm, sub-Planckian local density gradients arise, generating a jump in the scalar curvature. The radial component of the induced pressure tensor takes strictly negative values (Pvac < 0). The condition for radial hydrodynamic equilibrium of the relativistic flywheel takes the form:

ρeffv2 r = ∇ ⁡rPmatter + ∇ ⁡rPvac 		(35)

At a critical overspin value (a*2 - 1) ≈ 6.5 × 10-7, a powerful quantum barrier ∇ ⁡rPvac ∝ γ∇ ⁡r(□R) completely compensates for the classical centrifugal repulsion of the fluid masses. This prevents instantaneous destruction and dynamic rupture of the shell, ensuring a metastable plateau for the system’s existence during τ ≈ 28.3 million years.

0.7.3 Mechanism of the GW-EM Valve and Holographic Thermalization

The non-linear interaction term C ⋅ (∇ ⁡F)2 in the action specifies a direct three-particle coupling of the GW-photon-photon” type, since the covariant derivatives of the electromagnetic tensor explicitly contain the Christoffel symbols of the dynamic gravitational field gμν = ημν + hμν. Varying the total action with respect to the electromagnetic potential Aν (in the Lorenz gauge A;μμ = 0) extracts a wave equation with an effective current from the Weyl curvature gradients:

□Fμν = 4c3 Λeff2∇ ⁡σ (Cμνλρ∇ ⁡σF λρ) ∝ RλρσνFλρ,σ 		(36)

This equation describes a spontaneous phase transition of coherent tensor gravitons (spin-2) into transverse photons (spin-1). The strict requirement of unitarity of quantum states on the outer holographic screen of the valve (the volume of information carried away by radiation must correspond to the mass loss of the system) imposes a condition of conformal equipartition of energy. The system is physically forced to chaoticize the ordered low-frequency GW hum (νGW ≈ 0.2μHz), forcibly redirecting exactly 50% of the flywheel’s bolometric loss power into a high-entropy thermal flux:

Llight = 1 2LGW = 1 3 ln ⁡ 2 ⋅ α2 ⋅ (a *2 - 1) ⋅c5 G ≈ 1046erg/s 		(37)

On the macroscopic surface area of the elongated Jacobi ellipsoid S ≈ 6.28 × 1034cm2, this power balance strictly fixes the effective temperature of the photosphere at a cold” level of Teff ≈ 7270 K, which from first principles of GR and QFT rescues the source spectrum from the ultraviolet catastrophe.

0.7.4 Derivation of the Dimensionless Spin Differential Equation

The derivation of the relation is based on strict differentiation of the dimensionless rotation parameter a* = J M2 with respect to time under the condition connecting the losses of energy and angular momentum for quadrupole radiation (MTW). Let us take the total differential of the dimensionless spin a* = JM-2 using the product rule for differentiation:

da* = d ( J M2 ) = dJ M2 + J ⋅ (-2M-3dM ) = dJ M2 -2JdM M3 		(38)

Let us factor out the common multiplier 1 M2 and substitute back the original definition a* = J M2⟹ J M = a*M:

da* = 1 M2 (dJ - 2 J MdM ) = 1 M2 (dJ - 2a*MdM ) 		(39)

For the dominant quadrupole mode of gravitational radiation (l = 2,m = 2) of a relativistic rotator spinning with angular velocity Ω, the losses of angular momentum dJ and mass-energy dM are rigidly linked by a fundamental GR relation via the wave frequency ω = 2Ω (MTW):

dJ = m ω dM = 2 2ΩdM = 1 ΩdM 		(40)

At the relativistic Kerr limit, the angular velocity of surface rotation is rigidly fixed by the mass scale: Ω ≈ 1 2M. Substituting this value, we obtain the exact relation between the differentials of momentum and mass:

dJ = 1 ( 1 2M ) dM = 2MdM 		(41)

Now substitute the obtained expression for dJ into the bracket of the total differential of the dimensionless spin:

da* = 1 M2 (2MdM - 2a*MdM ) = 2MdM M2 (1 - a*) 		(42)

Canceling the mass M in the first fraction, we obtain the required exact differential equation of GR for the evolution of the rotation parameter:

da* ≈2dM M (1 - a*) 		(43)

(

0.7.5 System Behavior in the Sub-Extreme Regime (a* < 1)

Let us turn to the derived differential equation of GR linking the change in dimensionless spin with the rate of mass-equivalent loss due to quadrupole radiation:

da* ≈2dM M (1 - a*) 		(44)

In the region below the Kerr barrier (a* < 1), the geometric bracket (1 - a*) is strictly positive. Since the system loses mass-energy to radiation (dM < 0), the differential of the dimensionless spin becomes strictly negative (da* < 0). GW radiation below the barrier rapidly and irreversibly drops the rotation speed down. The centrifugal support of the fluid drops, and the object instantly collapses into a classical silent Kerr BH. The sub-extreme regime is thermodynamically unstable and leads to rapid collapse. The path from below is closed by conservation laws.

0.7.6 System Behavior in the Super-Extreme Regime (a* > 1)

Upon breaching the barrier and entering the region a* > 1, the sign of the geometric bracket (1 - a*) inverts to negative. Under conditions of continuous mass loss to GW radiation (dM < 0), the product of two negative quantities changes the sign of the spin differential to strictly positive:

da* > 0 		(45)

This is a fundamental stabilizing lock of GR. The loss of rotational energy in the super-extreme regime causes the dimensionless spin not to fall, but to shift even further upward away from the barrier! The system is physically trapped by a one-way GR valve and is incapable of falling down through unity into the black hole collapse zone. The object transitions into the phase of a long-lived metastable rotator.

0.7.7 Halting the Spin’s Upward Run and the Non-linear GW-EM Valve of the Holographic Screen

The avalanche-like upward shift of the spin along the trajectory da* > 0 from 1.0 toward the critical Darwin point 1.2 is rigidly blocked by the activation of higher non-linear corrections to the Einstein action—higher-order curvature terms R2 of quantum gravity (QG). As proven in the analysis of Gary Horowitz, Jorge Santos, and Benson Way (“Extremal Kerr Black Holes as Amplifiers of New Physics”, Physical Review Letters), the appearance of higher curvature terms in the relativistic action fundamentally alters the geometry of the extreme Kerr horizon. At the threshold of extremality, these non-linear terms of GR create a quantum backreaction of vacuum stiffness, which halts the growth of spatial curvature and stops the further upward run of the dimensionless spin. The system is reliably locked in a stationary phase right above the Kerr barrier (a* = 1 + ϵ). Simultaneously, within the thin macroscopic gap Δr = M(a*2 - 1) under conditions of extreme R2 non-linearities, the Maxwell-Einstein equations activate a direct tensor coupling. The non-linear interaction terms between gravitational and electromagnetic waves increase, turning the gap into an ideal field mixer. The gravitational “hum” of triaxiality (spin 2) hits this barrier and cannot freely escape into the outer Universe as pure GWs. Instead, the system undergoes a spontaneous phase transition: exactly 50% of the mechanical energy of the GW hum is forcibly converted into EM radiation (light). This 50% spin bridge represents a strict requirement of unitarity on the holographic screen of the valve: the rotational energy is translated into a high-entropy photon flux, which completely prevents information loss, resolves the Hawking paradox, and rescues the Bekenstein entropy bound, ensuring stable thermal dissipation of the Little Red Dot flywheel for hundreds of millions of years.

0.8 Conclusion

In this paper, a closed GR model of the non-linear Jacobi-Darwin quadrupole reactor is presented, which completely and without fitting parameters explains the phenomenon of JWST Little Red Dots. We have proved that under the strict conservation laws and Einstein-Maxwell non-linearities, the system escapes the coordinate deadlocks of classical Kerr, converting a colossal supply of rotational energy of a compact flywheel into pure thermal light. The model completely resolves the information loss paradox and eliminates the need for hypothetical mechanisms of super-Eddington accretion. It should be noted that in this work, only the simplest mechanism of rotational asymmetric collapse leading to stationary radiation is outlined. Other much more complex and interesting cases of stationary oscillatory asymmetric collapse are possible, which also lead to high-power radiation over an extended time. Accretion can also stabilize and support such regimes. The non-linear equations describing such complex systems sometimes possess analytical solutions [6].

In this work, a closed, internally consistent GR model of a non-linear Jacobi-Darwin quadrupole reactor at a macroscopic gap is presented. Correcting the compactness scale factor allowed simultaneously solving the ultraviolet catastrophe problem (reducing the local temperature to 7270 K due to the macroscopic surface area) and maintaining the operability of the semiclassical GW-EM valve due to high local gradients of the Weyl curvature of ultrarelativistic rotation. The model completely eliminates the need for hypothetical mechanisms of early dust enrichment in the young Universe and explains the phenomenon of JWST Little Red Dots from first principles of GR and QFT.

1. The problem of the curvature scale at the macro-gap Objection: At a gigantic radius r ≈ 5 × 1016 cm, the average curvature of space-time is negligible. Quantum gravity corrections (R2) cannot activate there, as they only operate at Planck scales. textitResponse : The model operates not with average quantities, but with the extreme local invariant curvature at the discontinuity points of the fluid vacuum condensate. Local gradients of density and velocity (v → c) go to infinity, which locally activates sub-Planckian physics independently of the macroscopic radius of the entire shell.

2. Conversion of low-frequency hum into high-frequency light (Up-conversion) Objection: Direct conversion of gravitational waves (GW) into electromagnetic (EM) waves at the frequency of the rotator (ν ~ 10-7 Hz) would produce radio waves of the same ultra-low frequency. Standard QFT does not allow a coherent GW hum to split into billions of chaotic optical photons with Teff ≈ 7270 K. Response: Standard QFT is inapplicable here because non-linear quantum gravity corrections are at work. The strict requirement of unitarity on the holographic screen of the valve forbids carrying away energy without an adequate carry-away of entropy. The system is physically forced to chaoticize and break up the coherent GW flux, triggering an avalanche-like thermalization of energy into the optical spectrum.

3. Violation of the Kerr rotation limit (a* > 1) Objection: Exceeding the Kerr limit (a*2 - 1 ≈ 6.5 × 10-7) leads to the formation of naked singularities and violates Penrose’s cosmic censorship hypothesis. Response: This restriction applies only to black holes beneath an event horizon. In the model, the object is initially postulated as a distributed, extended relativistic body (a Jacobi flywheel). For material relativistic rotators, the parameter a* > 1) is permissible and does not generate GR singularities as long as the body is held back from collapse.

4. Dynamic instability of discontinuity points Objection:

According to the laws of relativistic hydrodynamics, points of density discontinuity and extreme curvature are unstable. They should be instantly smoothed out by shock waves or collapse into micro-BHs, rather than exist stationarily for millions of years. Response: The stability of the metastable plateau ( ~ 28.3 million years) is ensured by continuous replenishment from a colossal reservoir of rotational kinetic energy of the flywheel (Erot ≈ 0.2Mc2). The system is in dynamic quasistationary equilibrium until the flywheel is braked by GW dissipation and collapses into a final SMBH.

Bibliography

[1]   S. Chandrasekhar, Ellipsoidal Figures of Equilibrium, Yale University Press, 1969.

[2]   C. W. Misner, K. S. Thorne, J. A. Wheeler, Gravitation,W. H. Freeman, 1973.

[3]   L. Smarr, Surface Geometry of Charged Rotating Black Holes, Phys. Rev. D 7, 289 (1973).

[4]   Gary T. Horowitz, Maciej Kolanowski, Grant N. Remmen, Jorge E. Santos, Extremal Kerr Black Holes as Amplifiers of New Physics,Phys. Rev. Lett. 131, 091402 (2023).

[5]   K. S. Croker, J. L. Weiner, Implications of Symmetry and Pressure in Friedmann Cosmology. I. Formalism, The Astrophysical Journal, Volume 882, Number 1, 19 (2019).

[6]   Kupervasser O., Olami Z., and Procaccia I., “Stability analysis of flame fronts: Dynamical systems approach in the complex plane”, (1999) Physical Review E. 59, 3, p. 2587-2593.

Part X
Part 10. Wheeler’s Geometrodynamics, Topological Barriers, and the Quantum-Geometric Higgs–Cosmon Mechanism

Abstract. The evolution of the classical program of physics geometrization and the reasons for its conceptual crisis, associated with causal barriers to the dynamic change of spatial topology over time, are analyzed. A conformal method for overcoming these limitations is considered by means of a completely scale-invariant description of the gravitational sector, which excludes the manual input of fundamental rest masses. The mechanism of dynamic generation of the effective Planck scale due to the acquisition of a vacuum expectation value by the dilaton field is described. The conformal recalculation of independent scalar fields during the transition to a description system with a linear curvature sector is investigated, which leads to the formation of a heavy inflaton and a massless cosmon field. A quantum mass-dependent mechanism for fixing the residual vacuum energy density by means of the macroscopic contribution of the relict fermionic medium is formulated. It is shown that the alignment of the scalar field potential and the density of the cooling gas leads to the transition of the system into a bound bosonic state with the formation of a macroscopic coherent condensate. This phase transition stationary fixes the observed value of the cosmological constant and protects it from external fluctuations. A methodological rejection of the idea of purely geometric extraction of fermionic matter in favor of electroweak bosonic and cosmon vacuum condensates is stated.

0.1 The Concept of Quantum Foam and Particles as Topological Solitons

Einstein’s classical General Theory of Relativity (GR) admits the existence of non-linear solutions that change the global properties of the spacetime continuum. Such configurations include black holes, wormholes (Einstein–Rosen bridges), Alcubierre warp bubbles, and geons — gravitational-electromagnetic solitons. In the mid-20th century, John Archibald Wheeler proposed the program of geometrodynamics — a concept seeking to completely eliminate the notion of "matter" from physics and reduce it to pure, non-linear geometry of spacetime [1].

At the Planck curvature scale, the metric fluctuations of the vacuum become critical, forming Wheeler’s quantum foam [1]. Within it, submicroscopic wormholes, geons, and closed topological micro-bubbles are continuously created and destroyed. Wheeler hypothesized that all elementary particles of the macro-world are stable topological solitons — mouths of this boiling Planck vacuum [1].

Electric charge in geometrodynamics is described as "charge without charge": the field lines of force do not terminate on a singularity, but enter one mouth of a microscopic wormhole and exit from another, creating the illusion of a pair of opposite charges in space [4]. Moreover, Wheeler, together with Charles Misner, managed to mathematically prove that a purely geometric wormhole throat undergoing topological winding in a multiply connected space acquires an effective half-integer spin and obeys the symmetries of the Dirac fermion field [4]. This concept was named "mass without mass" and "spin without spin," proving the possibility of assembling a fermion from pure GR geometry [4].

0.2 The Problem of Topological Collapse: Prohibition on Creation and Destruction

The fundamental crisis of geometrodynamics, which put an end to Wheeler’s hopes of deriving the entire particle spectrum from pure geometry, lay in the mathematical prohibition on changing spatial topology over time [6]. For a geometric fermion-soliton (a micro-wormhole) to physically be created in a field collision or disappear in the process of annihilation, the initial smooth, simply connected Minkowski space must physically tear and reorganize into a multiply connected space with a throat.

However, within the framework of GR, such a change in topology encounters insurmountable causal barriers. According to Geroch’s theorem, any smooth spacetime process that transitions one spatial topology into another over a finite time interval inevitably generates closed timelike curves [6]. This leads to the emergence of unresolvable time paradoxes and macroscopic singularities, destroying the causal structure of physics [6]. Wheeler’s structure can exist as an eternal static solution, but it cannot appear or disappear dynamically, which rendered geometrodynamics incapable of describing the quantum dynamics of real particles [6].

0.3 Conformal Way Out of the Dead End: Scale Invariance, Wetterich’s Cosmon Field, and Shaposhnikov’s Higgs

Modern physics has overcome this topological dead end of Wheeler by the method of conformal perspective adjustment, completely eliminating the dimensional arbitrariness of the manual input of fundamental masses [2,8]. In scale-invariant extensions of the Higgs inflation model developed by Bezrukov, Shaposhnikov, and Zenhausern, the initial action of the gravitational sector in the Jordan frame is formulated as completely dimensionless and scale-invariant [8]:

SJ = ∫ d4x-g [ξHH2 + ξ χχ2 16π R -1 2(∂H)2 -1 2(∂χ)2 - V (H,χ)] 		(1)

In this initial scheme, two independent scalar fields are present [8]:

  • H — the scalar doublet of the Standard Model Higgs field.

  • χ — an additional geometric dilaton (cosmon) field introduced by Christof Wetterich [2].

The interaction potential V (H,χ) contains only dimensionless coupling constants, which prohibits the manual input of the Planck mass or Higgs mass at the start. Instead, an emergent gravity mechanism is realized in the theory: when the dilaton field χ acquires a vacuum expectation value ⟨χ⟩ = χ0 during the evolution of the Universe, it dynamically generates the effective Planck rest mass of the Universe on its own [8]:

MPl2 = ξ χχ02 		(2)

To linearize the gravitational sector and eliminate the non-minimal coupling of fields to the Ricci curvature scalar R, a conformal transition to the Einstein frame is performed [8]. During this metric transformation, the kinetic terms of the two original scalar fields H and χ become mathematically entangled through a non-linear mixing matrix [8].

As a result of this conformal recalculation, the two original fields H and χ transform into two entirely new independent canonical fields in the Einstein frame [8]:

1.
Heavy field (Shaposhnikov’s Higgs-inflaton, ϕ): It is born predominantly from the fluctuations of the Higgs doublet H [5]. In the early Universe, this field is responsible for cosmological inflation, and in the modern epoch, it acquires a mass of 125 GeV and ensures a stable mass spectrum of Standard Model fermions due to Yukawa coupling constants yi [5]:

meff(H) = yi ⋅ v 1 + ξH H2 ∕MPl 2 		(3)

where v is the canonical vacuum expectation value of the Higgs field.

2.
Massless field (Wetterich’s Cosmon, σ): It is isolated from the radial component of the dilaton χ [2]. Due to exact scale invariance, the cosmon potential V E(σ) in the Einstein frame acquires an ideally flat, exponentially decaying form [8]:

V E(σ) ~ MPl4 ⋅ exp ⁡ (-γ σ MPl ) 		(4)

The residual energy density of this potential smoothly evolves over time, playing the role of a dynamic Lambda term (quintessence) [2]. Thus, the number of scalar fields in the system is strictly conserved: two entangled fields of the Jordan frame gave birth to two canonical fields of the Einstein frame [8].

0.4 Quantum Corrections to the Vacuum Energy Density and Wetterich’s Neutrino Stopping Trigger

In canonical quantum field theory, the vacuum energy density is determined by summing the contributions of zero-point oscillations of all fundamental fields [7]. In this case, bosonic fields yield a positive contribution (+), while fermionic fields, due to the anticommuntation relations for creation and destruction operators, introduce a negative contribution (-) [7]. Within the Standard Model, the fermionic contribution, the dominant source of which is the top quark due to its high rest mass ( ~ 173 GeV), significantly exceeds the bosonic sector. In Shaposhnikov’s scale-invariant theory, this gigantic negative minus of the top quark is completely absorbed by dynamic regularization through the dilaton field, leaving the cosmon potential free from quantum infinities of the fourth power in particle masses [8].

Nevertheless, to fix the residual positive cosmon potential V E(σ) at the observed microscopic level of Dark Energy, a dynamic mechanism to stop its cosmological evolution is required [9]. This problem is solved in Christof Wetterich’s model due to the macroscopic contribution of a real relict neutrino gas [9]. As the cosmon field σ runs, the effective mass of real neutrinos increases exponentially, transforming them from relativistic radiation into cold fermionic matter [9]. The cosmon run stops when the positive cosmon potential V E(σ) and the positive macroscopic density of the neutrino gas ρν match and balance each other (ρν ≈ V E) [10]. The point of this classical balance of densities rigidly fixes the microscopic value of the Universe’s Dark Energy observed today without involving mechanisms of fine-tuning constants [10].

0.4.1 Scalar Attractor and Phase Transition of Neutrino Cooper Pairs (MaVaNs Mechanism)

In alternative mass-dependent scenarios (the MaVaNs models of Kaplan, Nelson, and Weiner), the fixation of Dark Energy is transferred to the quantum sector by introducing an independent scalar field — the acceleron [3]. The total vacuum energy density in this scheme is determined by a stationary thermodynamic minimum arising from the superposition of the increasing acceleron potential and the decreasing energy density of the neutrino medium [3]. When, in the course of cosmological cooling, the acceleron-dependent effective neutrino mass reaches a critical threshold, the long-range attraction between fermions induced by the exchange of acceleron quanta completely overcomes the kinetic Fermi pressure of the degenerate gas [3]. This excess scalar attraction triggers a mechanism analogous to BCS superconductivity in solid-state physics [11]. Neutrino fermions combine into bound composite Cooper pairs. Since a pair of two fermions with spin 1/2 forms a composite boson with an integer spin, the quantum statistics of the combined neutrino medium changes fundamentally. A phase transition of Bose–Einstein condensation is realized in the system with the instantaneous precipitation of Cooper pairs into a single macroscopic coherent state — a neutrino Bose-condensate [12]. This condensate step-like rearranges the free energy of the neutrino component. This phase transition acts as a quantum lock: it securely locks the acceleron field at the point of the thermodynamic minimum, stationary fixing the observed Dark Energy density and protecting it from cosmic fluctuations [3].

0.5 The Final Paradox of the Geometrodynamic Program: Bosonic Condensate versus Wheeler’s Fermion

It is necessary to record the fundamental conceptual contradiction separating Wheeler’s initial program and its modern implementation. Wheeler and Misner sought to derive structures possessing the properties of Dirac fermions with spin 1/2 from the topology of a multiply connected space, substantiating the concept of "spin without spin" [4]. However, in the scale-invariant model of Shaposhnikov and Wetterich, the direct geometrization of the scalar sector of Jordan–Brans–Dicke gravity is implemented due to the Higgs field and Wetterich’s cosmon field [8], and in MaVaNs scenarios — due to the acceleron field [3], which are pure BOSONS with spin 0. Moreover, even when turning on the quantum mass-dependent MaVaNs mechanism, the stabilization of the Lambda term is achieved by the transition of the neutrino medium into a bosonic condensate of Cooper pairs [11]. This step actually marked a methodological rejection of Wheeler’s idea of extracting fermionic matter from geometry. Within this geometry, spacetime is incapable of independently generating fermion fields; they are still introduced into the Lagrangian phenomenologically, as external matter scaled by the bosonic sector. Wheeler’s geometrodynamics remained an unrealized static blueprint, yielding its place to the electroweak bosonic and cosmon vacuum condensates.

Part XI
Part 11. Einstein–Cartan Theory and the Geometric Nature of Spin Torsion

Standard Einstein’s General Relativity (GR) is based on pseudo-Riemannian geometry, in which the spacetime torsion tensor is identically zero throughout the continuum [6]. Before introducing torsion, it is necessary to strictly define the basic geometric object and the gauge restriction that limits standard GR.

0.1 Local Spacetime Symmetries, Christoffel Symbols, and Gauge Restrictions of GR

In modern theoretical physics, the spacetime structure of the classical macro-world is described by a gravitational theory based on the local Poincaré group, which combines local translations (shifts) and Lorentz rotations SO(3, 1) [2].

When describing a curved spacetime, a differentiation operator — the covariant derivative — is introduced. The basic geometric object that sets this operator and determines the law of parallel transport of vectors along coordinate axes is the affine connection Γμνλ:

∂νeμ = Γμνλe λ 		(1)

where eμ are the basis vectors.

Standard Einstein’s General Relativity (GR) imposes two rigid restrictions on this connection [6]:

1.
The metricity postulate, requiring the invariance of vector lengths under transport: ∇ ⁡λgμν = 0.
2.
The postulate of strict symmetry of the connection with respect to the lower coordinate indices:

Γμνλ = Γ νμλ 		(2)

which is derived based on Einstein’s equivalence principle.

From the joint solution of these conditions (by the method of cyclic permutation of indices), an exact formula is uniquely derived, expressing the connection components exclusively through the first partial derivatives of the metric tensor gμν in the form of Christoffel symbols of the second kind Γ{μν}λ [3]:

Γ{μν}λ = 1 2gλρ (∂ μgνρ + ∂νgμρ - ∂ρgμν) 		(3)

The physical meaning of the symmetry restriction (2) in GR is that only the translation sector is gauged, the field of which is represented by the metric gμν [2]. Local Lorentz rotations SO(3, 1) are forcibly deprived of independent dynamics — the rotation of the basis is completely predetermined by metric shifts (3) [4]. As a consequence, GR "feels" only the macroscopic mass-energy of bodies through the energy-momentum tensor, while the microscopic characteristic of matter — the intrinsic spin of particles (the generator of Lorentz rotations) — is completely isolated from influencing the geometry [1].

0.2 Breaking of Riemannian Symmetry and Definition of the Torsion Tensor

Overcoming this limitation of GR is implemented in the Einstein–Cartan theory (ECT) by independent local gauging of the Lorentz rotations of the Poincaré group [4]. Lifting the artificial Riemannian restriction (2) means that the lower indices of the affine connection cease to be symmetric (Γμνλ≠Γ νμλ) [2]. This symmetry breaking leads to the connection acquiring an antisymmetric component, forming the torsion tensor Sμνλ, which by definition is given by the formula [1]:

Sμνλ = 1 2 (Γμνλ - Γ νμλ) 		(4)

Since any non-linear coordinate distortions of the connection mutually cancel out during the subtraction in formula (4), the object Sμνλ is a strict, pure geometric tensor [6].

In Riemann–Cartan geometry, while maintaining the metricity condition, the full affine connection is expressed through Christoffel symbols and tensor combinations of torsion (contorsion) [5]:

Γμνλ = Γ {μν}λ + S μνλ + S μν λ + S νμ λ 		(5)

Equation (5) clearly demonstrates that when torsion equals zero (Sμνλ = 0), the connection instantly reduces to the canonical metric form of GR (3) [3]. In ECT, the torsion tensor becomes a free gauge variable, directly coupled with the spin density of the medium [1].

0.3 Gauge Noether Identities and Continuity of the Total Angular Momentum

A fundamental condition for the mathematical consistency of the Einstein–Cartan theory is the strict fulfillment of the conservation laws imposed by local invariance with respect to the Poincaré group [1]. According to Noether’s theorem, the independence of Lorentz rotations generates a differential gauge identity connecting the divergence of the spin density with the antisymmetric part of the canonical energy-momentum tensor of matter [2]:

∇ ⁡λτ μνλ = T μν - Tνμ 		(6)

where τ μνλ is the spin density tensor, and Tμν is the canonical energy-momentum tensor [4].

It must be emphasized that a macroscopic local vortex of a medium (velocity rotor or orbital angular momentum) is fundamentally incapable of generating geometric torsion of spacetime [1]. According to Cartan’s algebraic equations, the only source of torsion is exclusively the intrinsic spin of particles τ μνλ. Moreover, of all the particles in the Standard Model, only fermions can create the required spin current; bosonic fields are deprived of this capability due to the requirements of their own strict internal gauge invariance, which completely nullifies their contribution to the torsion of the connection [5]. In the absence of a fermionic spin current, torsion is identically zero, regardless of the intensity of mechanical rotation or vortex flows of spinless matter [1].

Equation (6) describes the mechanism of preserving the continuity of the total angular momentum during dynamic transitions (for example, during the conversion of intrinsic spin into orbital angular momentum, ΔS →ΔL). When the local spin current τ disappears, torsion decays; however, this angular momentum is not lost, but due to identity (6) instantly gives rise to an asymmetry of the energy-momentum tensor (Tμν≠Tνμ), which manifests itself in the form of orbital rotation and mechanical stresses [2]. Thus, the full continuity of the physical process is preserved: the quantum torsion of the microworld is transformed into an asymmetric contribution to the macroscopic curvature of the Riemannian metric of GR, excluding any topological tears of the continuum [5].

0.4 Action of the Einstein–Cartan Theory and Algebraic Equations

The gravitational action of the Einstein–Cartan theory retains the Hilbert–Einstein form linear in the total curvature, but is varied independently with respect to the metric and the Palatini connection [1]:

SEC = ∫ d4x-g [ R(Γ) 16πG ] + Smatter[gμν, Γμνλ,ψ] 		(7)

where:

  • d4x — an element of the four-dimensional spacetime volume (a differential form of the energy density dimension [M-4]).

  • gμν — the covariant metric tensor determining spacetime intervals, setting the macroscopic geometry, and serving as the gauge field of local translations of the Poincaré group [6].

  • g — the determinant of the matrix of the metric tensor gμν. This value is always negative in the Lorentzian signature, so the root -g acts as an invariant volume element [3].

  • Γμνλ — the full affine connection of the Riemann–Cartan spacetime. In the Palatini formalism, it is treated as a dynamic variable independent of the metric gμν and serves as a gauge field for the local Lorentz group SO(3, 1) [1].

  • R(Γ) — the full Riemann–Cartan scalar curvature. It is calculated by contracting the metric with the Ricci tensor built exclusively from the independent affine connection Γ, and contains deformations quadratic in torsion [5].

  • G — the canonical Newtonian gravitational constant, which fixes the rigid energy scale of the curvature and torsion of the continuum (in the natural system of units, its dimensionality is [M-2]) [8].

  • Smatter — the action functional of classical matter fields. In the Einstein–Cartan theory, it explicitly depends on the metric, material fields, and on the independent connection Γμνλ due to the presence of spin currents [4].

  • ψ — the classical Dirac fermion field. It represents the substance of the Universe and is the only source capable, through non-minimal coupling with the spin connection, of generating a non-zero spin density tensor τ μνλ and geometric torsion [5].

Varying with respect to the independent connection leads to Cartan’s field equations, which, due to the absence of derivatives of the connection in the Lagrangian (7), are purely algebraic [1]:

Sμνλ + δ μλS νρρ - δ νλS μρρ = 8πGτ μνλ 		(8)

where τ μνλ is the spin density tensor of matter. From equation (8), it follows that torsion is localized exclusively inside massive spin-oriented bodies and is identically zero in empty vacuum [5].

5. Effective GR Lagrangian and Contact Four-Fermion Interaction

Expressing Cartan’s algebraic equation (8) through the spin currents of the material field ψ (where the axial current is Jμ = ψŻγμγ5ψ) and substituting it back into the Lagrangian (7) generates an effective action in pure Riemannian GR geometry [1]:

Seff = ∫ d4x-g (R(Γ) 16πG + Lmatter(Γ) - 3πG (ψŻγμγ5ψ) (ψŻγ μγ5ψ)) 		(9)

Physical meaning of the effective Cartan Lagrangian:

  • The gravitational part is completely reduced to the standard linear Einstein form with a constant coupling G [6].

  • Spacetime torsion has completely disappeared as a geometric variable, giving rise to a non-linear contact four-component spin-spin interaction [4,5].

  • The negative sign before the contact term in (9) indicates that this interaction is repulsive for parallel spin currents [1]. At extreme density scales, this repulsion halts the gravitational collapse of matter, preventing the creation of singularities inside black holes and replacing the singular beginning with a cosmological bounce [7].

Bibliography

[1]   F. W. Hehl, P. von der Heyde, G. D. Kerlick, and J. M. Nester, General relativity with spin and torsion: Foundations and prospects,” Rev. Mod. Phys., vol. 48, no. 3, pp. 393–416, 1976.

[2]   T. W. B. Kibble, Lorentz invariance and the gravitational field,” J. Math. Phys., vol. 2, no. 2, pp. 212–221, 1961.

[5]   I. L. Shapiro, Physical aspects of the space-time torsion,” Phys. Rep., vol. 357, no. 2, pp. 113–213, 2002.

[7]   A. Trautman, “Spin and torsion may avert gravitational singularities,” Nature Phys. Sci., vol. 242, pp. 7–8, 1973.

Part XII
Part 12. On the Conformal Isomorphism Between Modified Gravity and Varying Mass Solutions to the Hubble Tension

Abstract. The modern cosmological crisis surrounding the Hubble tension (H0 tension) has split alternative early-time scenarios into two competing, mutually exclusive taxonomies: modified gravity models introducing an early evolution of the effective gravitational constant Geff (natively in the Jordan frame), and particle-physics extensions postulating a dynamical variation of the electron mass me around the recombination era (natively in the Einstein frame). In this paper, we demonstrate that at the semiclassical level, these two approaches represent not distinct physical hypotheses, but conformally isomorphic representations of the exact same underlying scalar-tensor dynamics. We explicitly construct the mathematical map between the gravitational and matter sectors via a conformal transformation of the spacetime metric. It is shown that key dimensionless cosmological observables, including the angular scale of the sound horizon θ* and the resulting Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) anisotropy power spectra, remain strictly invariant under this mapping. The apparent split between these paradigms in current literature is shown to be an artifact of the computational architecture of modern Boltzmann solvers, which are hardcoded in the Einstein frame. Furthermore, we outline the preservation of this isomorphism against quantum loop corrections at the recombination scale and establish its compatibility with local tests of the Weak Equivalence Principle via late-time screening and freezing mechanisms.

0.1 Introduction

The history of theoretical physics demonstrates that radically different physical intuitions often conceal a strict underlying mathematical equivalence. A historically prominent example is the 1926 debate between E. Schrödinger’s wave mechanics and V. Heisenberg’s matrix mechanics, which was ultimately resolved by proving their isomorphism within Dirac’s transformation theory.

Today, a similar hidden conceptual split is taking place in cosmology during attempts to resolve the H0 tension between local measurements (z ≈ 0) and global Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) data from the Planck satellite (z ≈ 1100). Two independent camps have emerged in the literature:

1.
Physicists modifying the gravitational sector in the Jordan frame, invoking a time-dependent effective gravitational constant ΔGeff or generalized scalar-tensor theories [1].
2.
Phenomenologists modifying the particle sector in the Einstein frame, introducing an early shift in the electron mass Δme to alter the recombination history [2,3].

The purpose of this article is to bridge this methodological gap by showing that debates over which sector “actually” evolves are physically vacant on a semiclassical cosmological background.

0.2 The Core Mathematics of Conformal Isomorphism

Let us consider a scale-invariant action of a scalar-tensor theory in the original Jordan frame (J), where a scalar field χ (acting as a dilaton or cosmon) couples non-minimally to the Ricci scalar RJ:

SJ = ∫ d4x-g J [ξχχ2 2 RJ -1 2gJμν∂ μχ∂νχ - yeψŻHψ] 		(1)

In this representation, the fundamental masses of fermions (including the electron) are fixed by the standard Yukawa vacuum expectation value of the Higgs field ⟨H⟩ = v, while the Planck mass is dynamically generated as MPl2(x) = ξ χχ2(x). This is mathematically equivalent to a variable effective gravitational constant:

Geff(χ) = 1 8πξχχ2 		(2)

To transition to the standard Einstein frame (E), we perform a conformal transformation of the spacetime metric:

gμνE = Ω2(x)g μνJ,Ω2(x) = ξχχ2(x) MPl,02 		(3)

where MPl,0 represents the modern fixed laboratory value of the Planck mass. Re-scaling the fermion fields as ψE = Ω-3∕2ψ J to maintain canonical kinetic terms maps the original scale-invariant Yukawa interaction into:

LY ukawa,E = -ye ⋅ v Ω(χ) ψŻEψE⟹me(χ) = me,0 Ω(χ) = me,0 ( MPl,0 ξχχ ) 		(4)

Equation ((4)) demonstrates that the spatial and temporal drift of the scalar field χ, which alters the expansion rate via Geff(χ) in the Jordan frame, maps precisely onto a dynamical drift of the electron mass me(χ) in the Einstein frame.

0.3 Semiclassical Validity and Quantum Loop Corrections

A standard theoretical caveat regarding the equivalence of the Jordan and Einstein frames emerges at the quantum level. When calculating loop corrections within the path integral framework, the non-trivial transformation of the functional integration measure can induce a frame anomaly [4,5]. We must explicitly emphasize, however, that such quantum ambiguities are suppressed during the cosmological epoch under consideration.

The physics of cosmological recombination occurs at an energy scale of E ~O(1 eV), which is separated from the ultraviolet Planck scale MPl by nearly 28 orders of magnitude. Any non-local quantum gravitational loop contributions to the effective action arising from the non-minimal coupling ξχχ2R are strictly suppressed by powers of the UV cutoff:

δLloop ~O ( E4 MPl4 ) → 0 		(5)

Consequently, at z ~ 1100, the semiclassical approximation is a phenomenologically exact description. The conformal isomorphism between the scaling of Geff(χ) and the drift of me(χ) remains robust against quantum loop corrections.

0.4 Invariance of Cosmological Observables

The ultimate proof of this isomorphism lies in the strict invariance of the primary dimensionless observable of the early universe—the angular scale of the sound horizon at the moment of recombination θ*:

θ* = rs(z*) DM(z*) 		(6)

  • In the Jordan frame representation (Modified Gravity [1]), the electron mass me is constant, and the thermodynamics of plasma remain standard. However, an elevated Geff prior to recombination increases the Hubble expansion rate via the Friedmann equation (H2 ∝ G effρ). This shortens the cosmic time available before recombination, shrinking the comoving size of the sound horizon rs = ∫ csdt and proportionally reducing the angular diameter distance DM.

  • In the Einstein frame representation (Varying Mass [2,3]), the gravitational constant G and background expansion history H(z) are standard. However, a heavier electron mass me(χ) scales up the hydrogen binding energy (Ebind ∝ me). This forces cosmological recombination to trigger earlier at a higher plasma temperature T*. This earlier boundary truncates the sound horizon integration for rs in a mathematically identical manner.

Because astrophysical instruments register only dimensionless angles θ* and relative temperature fluctuations ΔT∕T, both representations yield completely identical CMB power spectra.

0.5 The Weak Equivalence Principle and Late-Time Freezing

A severe empirical test for any model postulating a dynamical electron mass me(χ) stems from local tests of the Weak Equivalence Principle (WEP). If the scalar field χ mediates long-range forces into the late universe, composition-dependent “fifth forces” would emerge, which are heavily ruled out by the sub-part-per-trillion limits on the Eötvös parameter (η ~ 10-15) achieved by the MICROSCOPE space mission [7,8].

To remain compatible with these empirical boundaries, the underlying scalar-tensor theory must invoke one of two standard post-recombination mechanisms:

1.
Dynamical Freezing via Steep Attractors: The cosmic potential V (χ) can be structured such that following the recombination epoch (z ≲ 1000), the field χ rapidly rolls down a steep gradient into a highly localized minimum (⟨χ⟩→ χ0). By z ~ 10, the field becomes entirely static. The conformal factor locks (Ω → 1), freezing me at its standard laboratory value (0.511 MeV) and rendering the field’s quanta heavily massive (mχ ≫eV), which suppresses any macroscopic fifth force via a Yukawa-type cutoff.
2.
Density-Dependent Screening Mechanisms: Alternatively, the scalar sector can employ a Chameleon or Symmetron effect [9, 10]. Here, the effective mass of the field is a function of the local ambient matter density: meff2 ∝ ρ. In the ultra-low density environments of the early intergalactic medium, the field is light and drives the conformal drift needed to shift θ*. However, inside high-density environments like the Solar System or Earth-based laboratories, the field acquires an enormous mass, screening the scalar charge of macroscopic test bodies.
3.
Classical Vainshtein Mechanism (Vainshtein screening) A kinetic screening scenario originally proposed by A. Vainshtein  [11] and further developed within the modern framework of covariant Galileons by C. Deffayet  [12] and E. Babichev  [13]. In this framework, the suppression of the extra force is mediated by non-linear kinetic terms containing higher-order derivatives of the scalar field (typically of the form (∂ϕ)2□ϕ). In the vicinity of a massive mechanical source, the spatial gradients of the scalar field itself grow drastically, forcing the non-linear kinetic operator to dominate over its linear counterpart. Consequently, the kinetic energy of the scalar fluctuations becomes elastically enhanced, which effectively drives the effective coupling constant between the scalar field and baryonic matter to zero. The primary vulnerability of the Vainshtein mechanism within continuous 4D effective field theories (EFT) lies in the fact that quantum corrections frequently pull the scalar field mass back to the Planck scale, locking the field within the micro-world (10-33 cm).
4.
Newtonian Freezing Mechanism (Acceleration-dependent screening) A mathematically robust scenario capable of ensuring the complete quenching of scalar forces within the vacuum of planetary systems. This approach was covariantly formulated by S. Hossenfelder within the context of emergent gravity  [15] and systematically investigated in non-linear models of the K-mouflage class by J. Khoury  [16]. In this fourth scenario, the non-linear kinetic operator of the scalar field is accelerated in the equations of motion not by the gradients of the scalar field itself, but by the magnitude of the classical Newtonian acceleration of the background geometry aN (i.e., by the Newtonian potential of the macroscopic source). Although baryonic matter is practically absent within the interplanetary vacuum of the Solar System (rendering the Chameleon screening powerless), the Newtonian acceleration generated by the Sun at the Earth’s orbit is immense and exceeds the critical infrared MOND threshold by many orders of magnitude (aN ≫ a0 ~ 10-10m c2) [17]. This strong Newtonian field of the background macro-geometry forcefully clamps the fluctuations of the emergent scalar field throughout the entire interplanetary volume of the planetary system, driving its coupling constant to zero and seamlessly restoring Einstein’s General Relativity (GR) for terrestrial laboratories and spacecraft.

As a result, one of these screening mechanisms dampens WEP violations far below the MICROSCOPE threshold.

0.6 Computational Architecture and the Source of the Illusion

The apparent illusion that modified gravity and varying electron mass are competing physical hypotheses stems from the computational design of modern Boltzmann solvers (such as CAMB or CLASS). These software codes are natively hardcoded in the Einstein frame with a fixed Planck mass.Consequently, when a team of phenomenologists seeks to test an early-universe scalar-tensor or geometric modification, they are computationally forced to project the scalar field’s dynamics into the matter sector, translating it into a variation of me. The separate taxonomy in literature is therefore a consequence of programming constraints rather than a reflection of distinct physical realities.

0.7 Conclusion

We have shown that the ongoing debate between early-universe modified gravity scenarios and variations of fundamental constants is artificial. On a semiclassical background, both paradigms are conformally isomorphic. Much like the historical equivalence between the Heisenberg and Schr"odinger representations, these frameworks view the same physical reality through different mathematical lenses. The real objective of early-time cosmos-modelling should shift away from treating them as separate alternatives, and toward tightly constraining the unique scalar field potential V (χ) that dictates both phenomena simultaneously.
