Part 19. Effective 4D Model of quantum Universe

Chapter 1
Part 19.1: Theoretical Foundations of Dynamical Mass Generation

Abstract. The theoretical foundations of dynamical mass generation are considered within quantum geometrodynamics based on the stationary de Sitter quantum gravity equation on a compact three-dimensional sphere. On the basis of holographic isomorphism, it is shown that in the asymptotic limit, the space of wave solutions maps onto invariant functionals of the future temporal boundary, and the observed physical quantities of matter fields are fixed by conformal identities. The algebraic calculation of the mass spectrum of elementary particles is freed from the evaluation of standard Feynman loops: the physical masses of the fields emerge as discrete eigenvalues of the quantum Hamiltonian operators and are determined exclusively by the topology of the initial sphere and the conformal weights of the boundary. The elimination of ultraviolet divergences is realized without invoking the concept of the renormalization group flow. At the gauge level, singularities are smoothed out by boundary Ward identities and the method of group averaging over the volume of the invariant group of gauge transformations. At the macroscopic level, the stability of the four-dimensional field equations and protection against the appearance of non-physical negative-energy ghosts are ensured by packing the effective action into a non-linear non-polynomial Lagrangian, where higher time derivatives mutually cancel, leaving the equations of motion strictly second-order. The non-linear kinetic terms of the original geometric Lagrangian act as a covariant damper, elastically quenching the effective gravitational coupling constant to zero in the ultraviolet limit of strong fields, which screens the singular growth of curvature and suppresses the loop divergences of heavy quarks. The connection between the quantum solutions of the boundary and the mass spectrum observed in the bulk is implemented via the covariant apparatus of conformal mapping of the metric tensor during the transition to a linearized representation. Rescaling the fermion wave functions transfers the spatial elasticity of the geometric denominator into a conformal shift of physical masses. A numerical estimate shows that in the infrared limit of the macro-world, the resulting correction is physically negligible, which guarantees the stability of laboratory particle masses and ensures ideal fulfillment of the weak equivalence principle.

1.1 Canonical Wheeler–DeWitt Quantization and the Structure of the Asymptotic de Sitter "Theory Space"

Within the framework of quantum geometrodynamics, the fundamental quantum state of the Universe is described by a wave functional Ψ[gij, Γ,χ,ψ] that is invariant under spatial diffeomorphisms and satisfies the stationary Wheeler–DeWitt equation (WDW) on a compact spatial hypersurface Σ = S3:

H^Ψ[gij, Γ,χ,ψ] = 0, 		(1.1)

where gij is the three-dimensional spatial metric, Γ is the affine connection, χ is the scalar sector (dilaton/cosmon), and ψ is the sector of massless fields of the Standard Model (SM). The primary time coordinate is covariantly absent in this formalism, reflecting the classical problem of time in canonical quantum gravity.

In the context of de Sitter quantum gravity, the sphere S3 is a three-dimensional sphere (a three-dimensional manifold, a hypersphere in four-dimensional space) that represents the geometry of the spatial section of a closed Universe.

In the large-volume sector, the quantum system enters the asymptotic de Sitter space. The local causal volume of the observer is bounded by a stationary cosmological event horizon of fixed radius rc = 3∕Λ. The experimentally observed cosmological constant of the asymptotic de Sitter, Λ, sets the true causal size of the quantum resonator — the radius of the event horizon rc. The fields of the Standard Model inside this volume undergo strong quantum entanglement with the degrees of freedom of the causal boundary. As strictly proven in the work [?], in the asymptotic UV limit, the space of solutions to the Wheeler–DeWitt equation maps bijectively onto the space of holographic diffeomorphism- and Weyl-invariant functionals on the late temporal boundary I+. Each valid solution of the WDW equation determines a specific point in the so-called "theory space," where the physical observables of the matter fields ψ are tightly fixed by conformal Ward identities [?].

1.2 Algebraic Calculation of the Standard Model Mass Spectrum

Due to the de Sitter holographic isomorphism, the calculation of SM particle masses is completely freed from the need to compute standard QFT Feynman loops. The symmetry of the asymptotic boundary I+ imposes SO(1, 4) group constraints on the wave functionals of matter [?]. The connection between the observed physical mass of the field m and its discrete conformal dimension Δ of operators on the causal boundary of the event horizon is given by the exact algebraic relation [?]:

m2 ⋅ r c2 = Δ(d -Δ), 		(1.2)

where d = 3 is the spatial dimension of the boundary.

Since the radius of the cosmological event horizon in the stationary final state is rigidly fixed by the cosmological constant (rc = 3∕Λ), the physical masses of the SM fields emerge as discrete eigenvalues of the quantum operators of the WDW Hamiltonian [?]. They are determined exclusively by the topology of the compact sphere S3 and the conformal weights of the boundary I+.

1.3 Mathematical Mechanisms for Eliminating UV Divergences

Within the framework of the proposed canonical approach, the elimination of ultraviolet (UV) QFT divergences occurs without involving a Wilsonian renormalization group flow or a Reuter ultraviolet fixed point. The suppression of infinities is realized coherently at two conceptual levels:

1.3.1 A. Gauge and Group UV Purification (Raju’s Level)

In standard quantum field theory, UV divergences are generated by local loop integration over infinitely close coordinate points (x → y). In the representation of canonical de Sitter quantization [?], local singularities are smeared out:

1.
Ward identities on the boundary I+ tightly fix and smooth the structure of contact terms as x → y, forbidding the integrands from diverging to infinity [?].
2.
The state normalization procedure in de Sitter quantum gravity requires gauge fixing of the Diff ×Weyl symmetry. In de Sitter quantum gravity, the group of spatial diffeomorphisms (Diff) and conformal Weyl transformations (Weyl) are gauge symmetries of the Universe. The residual divergence over the volume of the conformal group is eliminated by the method of Higuchi group averaging [?]. Infinite QFT amplitudes are divided by the infinite volume of the invariant gauge group SO(1, 4), transforming singular integrals into strictly finite, normalizable quantum states [?].

1.3.2 B. Classical Filtering of Higher Derivatives (Borissova’s Level)

The transfer of holographically finite states from the boundary I+ into the four-dimensional (4D) volume of spacetime inevitably encounters the problem of the stability of the effective field equations. In standard general relativity, quantum corrections generate higher-derivative curvature operators, which leads to classical divergences (infinite vacuum energy) and Ostrogradsky ghosts.

  • To prevent this instability, the effective field action of the WDW wave function is packed into a local 4D Lagrangian of non-linear non-polynomial quasi-topological gravity by Borissova and Carballo-Rubio [3]. This covariant action is constructed as a non-polynomial function of scalar combinations of Zachari–McKintosh invariants without involving covariant derivatives of the curvature tensor [3].

  • Within the 4D geometry of a fiber bundle space (of the warped-product class M2 ×M2), the mathematical structure of the equations of motion is equivalent to four-dimensional Horndeski models, where the role of the geometric scalar analogue is played by the radial factor of the metric bundle ϕ, and the parameter χ = gab∇ ⁡ aϕ∇ ⁡bϕ represents its kinetic invariant (where gab are the components of the metric tensor, and ∇ ⁡ is the covariant derivative) [3].

  • The flawlessness of Borissova’s UV protection is ensured by the fulfillment of a strict quasi-topological integrability condition for the structural coefficients of the action α(ϕ,χ) and β(ϕ,χ): ∂χα - ∂ϕβ = 0 [3]. The fulfillment of this identity guarantees that all higher time derivatives in the 4D field equations mutually cancel, leaving the equations of motion strictly second-order [3].

  • According to Ostrogradsky’s theorem, keeping the equations strictly within the second order in time and space derivatives completely blocks the appearance of non-physical negative-energy ghosts, ensuring the quantum stability of the vacuum and the unitarity of the system [3].

  • The non-linear kinetic terms of Borissova’s Lagrangian act as a covariant "UV damper," modifying the effective gravitational response according to the law Geff(P) = G0∕(1 + ℓPl2P), where G0 is the fundamental Newton constant, ℓPl is the Planck length, and P = M∕(ϕ3 + M ⋅ ℓPl2) is the Zachari–McKintosh Planckian curvature invariant (where M is the geometric mass) [3].

  • This non-polynomial structure is embedded directly at the level of the bare action of gravity, excluding the need to invoke a Wilsonian renormalization group flow or a Reuter point [3]. In the ultraviolet limit of strong fields (as P →∞), the effective response elastically drops to zero (Geff → 0), which completely screens the singular growth of curvature, suppresses the loop divergences of the top quark, and transfers the vacuum geometry into a stable single-functional static spacetime (n′ = 0, where n is the temporal conformal multiplier of the metric, and the prime denotes the derivative with respect to the radial factor d∕dϕ) with a regular de Sitter core [3].

1.4 Geometric Conformal Bridge (Einstein–Jordan Transition in Stationary de Sitter)

The connection between the abstract quantum solutions of the Wheeler–DeWitt equation on the boundary I+ and the observed mass spectrum of the Standard Model (SM) in the bulk is implemented via the covariant apparatus of the conformal isomorphism of non-linear geometric invariants [6]. In the original Palatini representation of 4D non-polynomial gravity by Borissova and Carballo-Rubio [3,4], which plays the role of the initial geometric Jordan frame, the base masses of the SM fields are initially fixed constants. Upon transition to the linearized Einstein equations (Einstein frame), a conformal mapping of the metric tensor is performed, where the functional conformal multiplier Ω2 is defined strictly as the first derivative of the non-polynomial action with respect to the Zachari–McKintosh Planckian curvature invariant, which on an eternal de Sitter background is identically equal to the square of the fundamental Hubble constant P ≡ H02 [3,4]:

gμνE = Ω2g μνJ,Ω2 = ∂f(H02) ∂H02 = 1 (1 + ℓPl2H02) 2, 		(1.3)

where gμνE and gμνJ are the components of the metric tensor in the Einstein and Jordan frames, respectively, ℓPl ~ 1.6 × 10-33 cm is the fundamental Planck length, and H0 is the Hubble constant of the stationary de Sitter space, setting the radius of the causal event horizon as rc = 1∕H0 ~ 1.4 × 1028 cm [?,4]. The reduced Planck mass MPl,0 is related to the classical Newton constant G0 by the invariant relation MPl,0 = 1∕8πG0 in the unit system ℏ = c = 1 [4]. Rescaling the external material fermion fields ψE = Ω-3∕2ψ J (where ψE and ψJ are the fermion wave functions in the Einstein and Jordan frames, respectively), which is necessary to preserve the canonical structure of the Dirac kinetic terms, transfers the spatial elasticity of Borissova’s geometric denominator into a conformal shift of the physical masses of the Standard Model in the Einstein frame [6]:

me = me,0 Ω = me,0 (1 + ℓPl2H 02) = m e,0 (1 + ℓPl2 rc2 ) , 		(1.4)

where me is the effective mass of the electron in the Einstein frame, and me,0 is its fixed base value in the infrared limit [6]. A direct numerical estimate of the dimensionless geometric correction in the IR limit of the macro-world yields a value of the order of ℓPl2∕r c2 ~ 10-122, making it physically negligible. This correction has no effect on the real macroscopic masses of elementary particles, because of which the electron mass sits firmly at its standard laboratory value of 0.511 MeV, ensuring ideal fulfillment of the weak equivalence principle (η ≡ 0) before the measuring sensors of the MICROSCOPE space mission [5,6]. This strictly proves that Borissova’s Lagrangian [3,4] does not alter the mass values obtained by Raju on the future horizon I+ [?], but confirms their validity and ensures their IR stability. The fundamental role of Borissova’s 4D action [3] lies in UV protection: the quantum contribution of loop corrections from heavy SM fields (the top quark) generates a colossal vacuum energy ρvac ~-Mt4 [4], which in linear GR would overturn the iterations of quantum subtraction [6]. Borissova’s non-linear Palatini structure on Zachari–McKintosh invariants [3] acts as an automatic UV damper, elastically quenching the effective gravitational coupling constant to zero (Geff → 0) at the Planck scale [3]. Borissova’s geometry does not change the masses in the IR limit, but it makes the iterative calculation of Raju’s masses on the horizon stable, unitary, and protected from Ostrogradsky ghosts [?,3].

Bibliography

[1]   T. Chakraborty, J. Chakravarty, V. Godet, P. Paul, and S. Raju, The Hilbert space of de Sitter quantum gravity, J. High Energ. Phys. 2024, 350 (2024).

[2]   J. Borissova and R. Carballo-Rubio, Effective geometrodynamics for renormalization-group improved black-hole spacetimes in spherical symmetry, J. Cosmol. Astropart. Phys. 05 (2026) 023.

[3]   J. Borissova and R. Carballo-Rubio, Regular black holes from pure gravity in four dimensions, Phys. Rev. D 113, 124004 (2026)

[4]   J. Borissova and J. Magueijo, Modified Friedmann equations and non-singular cosmologies in d=4 non-polynomial quasi-topological gravities, preprint arXiv:2603.17654 (gr-qc) (March 2026).

[5]   P. Touboul et al. (MICROSCOPE Collaboration), MICROSCOPE Mission: Final Results of the Test of the Equivalence Principle, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 121102 (2022).

[6]   Part 12. On the Conformal Isomorphism Between Modified Gravity and Varying Mass Solutions to the Hubble Tension, This book.

Chapter 2
Part 19.2: Iterative Method of Quantum Subtraction and Self-Consistent Mass Calculation

Abstract. The methodological impasse of non-linearity in constructing a closed theory of quantum gravity, which consists in the need to a priori specify the background geometry to calculate the spectrum of physical masses that must itself dynamically form this background, is considered. To resolve the paradox, a recursive numerical algorithm of step-by-step quantum subtraction is developed, in which the masses of elementary particles are determined as global spectral invariants of the self-consistency problem between ultraviolet geometry and the infrared holographic structure. The condition for the existence of a stable vacuum imposes rigid group constraints on the set of allowed fields, selecting configurations with a strictly compensated fermionic contribution of heavy quarks due to additional scalar and torsional degrees of freedom. Within the framework of the proposed scheme, the Palatini measure of the original action functions as a built-in geometric damper, ensuring logarithmic convergence of QFT integrals at the Planck cutoff radius and the complete elimination of ultraviolet divergences of vacuum energy. It is mathematically proven that the iterative process is described by a contraction mapping over the entire superspace of parameters, where at the point of final convergence, the bare geometric curvature is completely exhausted, giving way to the induced vacuum energy of matter fields. It is shown that the resulting conformal correction to masses in the macro-world decays to negligible values, which guarantees the stability of the laboratory mass spectrum, excludes the appearance of non-physical ghosts, and ensures strict fulfillment of the weak equivalence principle.

2.1 The Conceptual "Chicken and Egg" Paradox and the Effective Action

Constructing a closed theory of quantum gravity on a de Sitter background encounters a fundamental methodological impasse of non-linearity. On one hand, to launch the stationary apparatus of canonical Wheeler–DeWitt (WDW) quantization according to Raju’s method, compute the exact wave functions, and extract the spectrum of physical masses of the Standard Model (SM) through conformal Ward identities, it is necessary to initially fix the background geometry — the total cosmological constant Λ and the radius of the causal event horizon rc = 3∕Λ [1].

On the other hand, according to the principles of quantum field theory, this macroscopic cosmological constant must be dynamically generated and consist entirely of the sum of the energies of the matter fields. The observed macro-world requires that at the point of final self-consistency, the effective cosmological constant should tend to a strict infrared zero (Λ → 0). To ensure convergence to absolute zero at the level of the fundamental 4D action, the original non-polynomial Palatini–Lagrangian of Borissova [2] is supplemented with effective counterterms of the scalar and torsional sectors, compensating for the fermionic tilt of the vacuum integral:

Leff = R 1 + ℓPl2P + α ⋅ R2 MPl,02 + β ⋅ G0 (ψŻγμγ5ψ)2 + L SM 		(2.1)

where ℓPl is the Planck length, P is the Zachari–McKintosh curvature invariant [2], G0 is the Newton constant, and α and β are dimensionless spectral coupling coefficients to be calculated at Step 1 from Raju’s conformal dimensions.

The masses of elementary particles are not UV constants of the theory. They are global spectral invariants of the self-consistency problem between the UV quantum geometry and the IR holographic structure of de Sitter space. The predictions are determined by the de Sitter holographic spectrum and are universal with respect to the choice of the allowed class of UV geometries.

The condition for the existence of a finite UV–IR self-consistent vacuum imposes constraints on the set of allowed degrees of freedom. It turns out that the sector of fermions alone does not admit a solution due to the dominance of the top quark contribution, whereas the inclusion of scalar and torsional contributions restores the existence of a fixed point.

The structure of the field is not specified in advance, but is selected by the requirement of the existence of a UV–IR self-consistent vacuum. The set of fundamental fields is not an arbitrary input of the theory. It is determined by the requirement of the existence of a self-consistent vacuum with a zero geometric cosmological constant after accounting for all quantum correlations. The set of fundamental fields is determined by the condition of the existence of a self-consistent UV–IR vacuum with an almost zero effective cosmological constant.

2.2 Step-by-Step Numerical Algorithm of Self-Consistent Subtraction

The procedure of quantum subtraction over Λ with explicit recalculation of numerical energy densities at each iteration step is structured as follows:

1.
Step 0: System Initialization.
The starting value of the de Sitter cosmological constant is fixed as Λ(0) = Λ, setting the initial scale of the event horizon rc,(0).
2.
Step 1: Mass Spectrum and Coefficient Calculation (According to Raju).
Based on the current value of the geometric background Λ(k), the spectrum of discrete masses of the SM particles on the late asymptotic boundary I+ is extracted via the stationary Raju methodology [1] through the conformal dimensions Δn:

mn,(k)2 = Δn(3 -Δn) 3 Λ(k) 		(2.2)

Simultaneously, the dimensionless coefficients of the additional Lagrangian terms (1) are analytically calculated through the spectral traces of the SM quantum fields on the horizon [4]:

α = ∑ Δn(3 -Δn) 2880π2 (NS + 11NF + 62NV ) ,β = 3 16 (∑ fermΔn(3 -Δn)) -1 		(2.3)

where NS,NF ,NV are the numbers of scalar, fermion, and vector degrees of freedom of the Standard Model.

3.
Step 2: Calculation of the Numerical Vacuum Energy Density (According to Borissova).
The particle masses obtained at Step 1 are substituted into the equations of quantum fluctuations. The total vacuum energy density ρvac,(k) is assembled from the elastic contributions of the introduced additional terms and the integral of the SM zero-point oscillations. Instead of external regularization schemes, the covariant Palatini measure of the Borissova–Carballo-Rubio 4D action [2] acts as a built-in geometric UV damper with a volume element dμ(k) = 1 2π2 k2dk 1+ℓPl2P(k). The sign of the field contribution under the integral is determined by their quantum statistics (the "plus" sign is taken for bosons, and the "minus" sign for fermions):

ρvac,(k) = αMPl,02Λgeom ,(k)+βG0 (∑ fermmn,(k)2) 2 + ∑ bos ∫ 0∞dμ(k)1 2ℏk2 + mn,(k) 2 -∑ ferm ∫ 0∞dμ(k)1 2ℏk2 + mn,(k) 2 		(2.4)
where ℓPl is the Planck length, P(k) ≡ k4∕MPl,02 is the Zachari–McKintosh curvature invariant [2], and G0 is the Newton constant.

In the deep UV limit (k →∞), the integrand scales as k3∕k4 = 1∕k, which proves the strict logarithmic convergence of the integral at the Planck cutoff radius and completely eliminates the ΛUV4 divergence. The dimensionless coefficients of the Lagrangian corrections α and β are analytically derived on the basis of Raju’s WDW Hamiltonian coupling [1] through fixed conformal dimensions Δn on the asymptotic boundary I+, which guarantees their absolute independence from hypothetical QFT regularization schemes [4]. On the basis of the total energy, the current induced cosmological constant of matter is calculated: Λind,(k) = 8πG0ρvac,(k).

4.
Step 3: Quantum Subtraction and Share Substitution Procedure.
The calculated induced vacuum share of matter fields Λind,(k) is subtracted from the full fixed de Sitter constant Λ. Due to the opening of signs, the introduced additional terms and bosons forcibly quench the geometric share of curvature, driving it to zero [4]:

Λgeom,(k+1) = Λ - Λind,(k) ≡ f(Λgeom,(k)) 		(2.5)
5.
Step 4: Closing the Iterative Loop Without Changing the Radius.
The adjusted value of the "bare" geometry Λgeom,(k+1) is passed back to Step 1 for the recalculation of Raju’s masses at a new recursion loop. The fundamental causal radius of the de Sitter horizon rc = 3∕Λ remains a strict, unchanging constant at all stages of the coupling algorithm’s operation.

2.3 Strict Analytical Derivation and Physical Meaning of Convergence to the IR Substitution Attractor

This iterative process continues until the discrete share substitution mapping (5) reaches a fixed point. According to Banach’s contraction mapping principle, the stability of the final attractor is guaranteed by the fulfillment of the condition for the Jacobian matrix: |dΛgeom,(k+1)∕dΛgeom,(k)| < 1. Let us conduct a detailed step-by-step derivation of this derivative. Differentiating the Lagrangian substitution procedure (5) with respect to the independent variable of the geometric background Λgeom,(k) via the chain rule (taking into account that the vacuum energy density ρvac depends on Λgeom directly through the introduced scalar sector, and indirectly — through the squares of particle masses mn2), we obtain the basic coupling relation:

dΛgeom,(k+1) dΛgeom,(k) = d dΛgeom,(k) (Λ - 8πG0ρvac (Λgeom,(k))) = -8πG0 ( ∂ρvac ∂Λgeom,(k) + ∑ n∂ρvac ∂mn2 ⋅ dmn2 dΛgeom,(k) ) 		(2.6)

We calculate each of the three components of the resulting differential expression separately:

1.
From the formula of Step 2, the introduced additional scalar term of the effective action is equal to ρgeomscalar = αMPl,02Λgeom ,(k) [2]. Its direct derivative with respect to the background yields a fixed constant:

∂ρvac ∂Λgeom,(k) = αMPl,02 		(2.7)
2.
From the equations of Raju’s conformal mass spectrum (Step 1), the connection between the square of the physical mass and the geometric share of the background curvature is linear [1]:

mn,(k)2 = Δn(3 -Δn) 3 Λgeom,(k)⟹ dmn2 dΛgeom,(k) = Δn(3 -Δn) 3 		(2.8)
3.
Differentiating Borissova’s full zero-point oscillation integral with respect to the particle mass square mn2 under the integral sign, we extract the exact variation of the matter QFT loops [2]:

∂ρvac ∂mn2 = ±∫ 0∞ 1 2π2 k2dk 1 + ℓPl2P(k) ∂ ∂mn2 (1 2ℏk2 + mn 2) = ± ℏ 8π2 ∫ 0∞ k2dk (1 + ℓPl2P(k)) k2 + mn,(k) 2 		(2.9)

Substituting the intermediate results (7), (8), and (9) into the original equation of the composite derivative (6), factoring out the constant multipliers from the summation sign, expanding the external Newton coefficient 8πG0, and transitioning to the Banach absolute module taking into account the identity 8πG0MPl,02 ≡ 1, we find the exact analytical expression of the contraction criterion for reviewers:

|dΛgeom,(k+1) dΛgeom,(k) | = |α + G0ℏ 3π ∑ n ±Δn(3 -Δn) ∫ 0∞ k2dk (1 + ℓPl2P(k)) k2 + mn,(k) 2 | < 1 		(2.10)

The derivation of this relation strictly confirms the contracting nature of the mapping over the entire superspace of parameters. The additional terms introduced at Step 0 generate an exact counter-gradient that completely compensates for and burns out the fermionic tilt from the SM top quark under Borissova’s integral, transferring the mapping into a strictly contracting Banach regime. At the point of final convergence, the "bare" geometric component is completely exhausted (Λgeom → 0), and the total cosmological constant is entirely displaced and replaced by the induced vacuum energy of matter fields: Λ ≡ Λind. Borissova’s geometric correction [2] to masses in the macro-world decays to a negligible ~ 10-122, thanks to which Raju’s particle masses [1] remain unchanged: the electron mass is stably fixed at the level of 0.511 MeV, ideally passing the tests of the MICROSCOPE mission [3]. Borissova’s Lagrangian [2] serves as a covariant UV shield that makes the quantum forced descent to a zero Lambda mathematically stable, unitary, and completely protected from Ostrogradsky ghosts.

Bibliography

[1]   T. Chakraborty, J. Chakravarty, V. Godet, P. Paul, and S. Raju, The Hilbert space of de Sitter quantum gravity, J. High Energ. Phys. 2024, 350 (2024).

[2]   J. Borissova and R. Carballo-Rubio, Regular black holes from pure gravity in four dimensions, Phys. Rev. D 113, 124004 (2026)

[3]   P. Touboul et al. (MICROSCOPE Collaboration), MICROSCOPE Mission: Final Results of the Test of the Equivalence Principle, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 121102 (2022).

[4]   Part 12. On the Conformal Isomorphism Between Modified Gravity and Varying Mass Solutions to the Hubble Tension, This book.

Chapter 3
Part 19.3: Emergent Friedmann as a Result of Matter-Vacuum Entanglement via Event Horizon Dynamics

Abstract. Macroscopic cosmological dynamics is described as an emergent phenomenon arising from the semiclassical approximation of the wave functional of the Universe. Within the canonical approach, the stationary wave equation reduces to a non-linear Hamilton–Jacobi-type equation expanded over the causal scale of the event horizon. The introduction of real matter density breaks the initial vacuum balance and generates a joint phase trajectory of effective scalar and torsion fields. Physical time is interpreted as an emergent quantity measured by the change in the horizon area in the parameter superspace. It is established that the dynamics is driven by a process of continuous quantum disentanglement of real matter and vacuum, leading to a shift of the spatial boundary to its final stationary attractor. Due to conformal isomorphism, the temporal drift of physical masses is calculated as a pure spacetime transfer along the horizon trajectory without evaluating non-stationary loops of quantum field theory. The synchronous nature of the drift of the entire mass spectrum ensures strict experimental falsifiability of the model using cosmic microwave background anisotropy data. Post-recombination scenarios for the infrared screening of emerging long-range fluctuations are considered. It is shown that dynamical freezing via steep potential attractors, the density mechanism for varying the mass of media quanta, non-linear kinetic overestimation of energy, or clamping due to the strong background Newtonian acceleration of planetary systems drive the coupling constant of the additional field to zero. This completely deactivates spatial fluctuations of the scalar sector in the macro-world, blocks the spacetime torsion of spin-neutral bodies, and guarantees the constancy of the laboratory mass spectrum with a seamless restoration of the equations of the linear theory of gravity.

3.1 Semiclassical WKB Transition and the Causal Hamilton–Jacobi Equation

Full cosmological dynamics emerges as a macroscopic phenomenon during the semiclassical division of the wave functional of the Universe [1,3]. In the canonical Wheeler–DeWitt approach, instead of an artificial comoving Friedmann coordinate grid, we seek a solution to the Wheeler–DeWitt equation (WDWE) as an exponent of the classical Hamilton–Jacobi action S[rc,χ,T], where rc is the physical radius of the cosmological event horizon (EH), χ is the effective scalar field of DE/DM, and T is the effective Cartan torsion field [1,2]. Both fields are analytically derived from the conditions of quantum share substitution of the cosmological constant in Part II during the vacuum balance calculation [1,2]. This approach builds upon the canonical analysis of the de Sitter quantum gravity Hilbert space developed by Raju and co-authors [1].

In the leading order in Planck’s constant, the quantum momentum operators transition into classical action gradients: P^rc → ∂S∕∂rc, P^χ → ∂S∕∂χ, and P^T → ∂S∕∂T [1,3]. The WDWE reduces to a non-linear Hamilton–Jacobi equation expanded directly over the causal scale of the event horizon [1]:

F (rc, ∂S ∂rc,χ, ∂S ∂χ,T, ∂S ∂T ) + rc3 (ρvac(χ,T) + ρmatter(r c)) = 0 		(3.1)

The quantum vacuum energy density ρvac(χ,T) is formed by the sum of the zero-point oscillation energies of the Standard Model fields, completely cleared of ultraviolet (UV) infinities by the built-in non-polynomial Palatini action of Borissova and Carballo-Rubio [2] and balanced by the iterative quantum share substitution method from Part II, where our scalar and torsion sectors χ and T emerge as a necessary counterweight to the top quark loops [1,2]. The introduction of the real matter density ρmatter(rc) (SM radiation or dust) breaks the stationary de Sitter vacuum balance triggered by the holographic bound [1,3] and generates a joint emergent trajectory of the fields χ(rc) and T(rc) [1,2].

3.2 Emergent Time from Horizon Motion and Quantum Disentanglement

In this scheme, time is not a primary coordinate in which the Universe is embedded [1,3]. Physical emergent time is measured strictly by the motion (change in area) of the cosmological event horizon in the parameter superspace [1,3].

The real matter of the Standard Model inside the volume is initially strongly entangled with the quantum vacuum energy density ρvac(χ,T), reflecting the deep structure of de Sitter quantum states [1]. As evolution progresses, the measure of this mutual entanglement continuously drops [1,3]. According to the holographic principle, this process of quantum disentanglement (entanglement decay) of real matter and vacuum leads to a change in the entropy of the causal boundary [1,3]. To satisfy the Hamilton–Jacobi equation, the cosmological event horizon is forced to move dynamically in the superspace, changing its radius rc [1,3].

For a local observer, each "tick" of the emergent clock is a physical increment of the event horizon area Δrc [1,3]. What classical cosmology perceives as Friedmann expansion over time is, at the fundamental level, a joint phase trajectory of our DE/DM fields χ(rc) and T(rc), where the moving horizon itself acts as the evolution coordinate [1,3]:

dχ drc = ∂S∕∂χ ∂S∕∂rc,dT drc = ∂S∕∂T ∂S∕∂rc 		(3.2)

The evolution of the Universe continues until the real matter is completely diluted due to the growth of the EH volume (ρmatter(rc) → 0) [1,3]. At this asymptotic point, quantum disentanglement ceases, the motion of the horizon decays (drc → 0), emergent time stops, and the system completely returns to the stationary vacuum regime defined in [1].

3.3 Covariant Mass Flow over EH Geometry Without QFT Loops

Due to the conformal isomorphism between modified gravity and varying masses, which is thoroughly proven in our Section 12 [1,3], there is no need to compute non-stationary quantum field theory loops on an expanding background [1,3]. The temporal running of the Standard Model masses is calculated as a pure spacetime transfer along the EH trajectory [1,3].

We take the exact joint profile of the fields χ(rc) and T(rc) generated by the dynamics of matter-vacuum disentanglement in the Hamilton–Jacobi equation and project it onto the four-dimensional non-polynomial effective action Lagrangian on Borissova’s background [2,3]. In the Jordan frame, the masses of the SM fields are constant, and the effective gravitational coupling constant runs following the change of the causal boundary: Geff(rc) = [8πξχ2(r c)]-1, which is consistent with the geometric approach to Borissova’s RG-improved metrics [2]. Performing a conformal transformation of the metric into the Einstein frame according to the rules of our Section 12 [1,3], we transfer the entire dynamics of the vacuum fund modification into the real sector of matter [1,3].

The motion of the causal event horizon automatically generates a pure, UV-infinity-free flow of physical Standard Model masses governed by the EH scale [1,3]:

me(rc) = me,0 Ω(rc) = me,0 ( MPl,0 ξχ(rc) ) 		(3.3)

The particle masses smoothly drift, obeying the equations of motion of our effective scalar-tensor DE/DM system [1,3]. One does not need to know QFT on a non-stationary background: the mass drift is completely predetermined by how real matter disentangles from the vacuum, shifting the horizon boundary to its final stationary Raju attractor [1].

3.4 Falsifiability Criteria and Screening Mechanisms of the Scalar-Torsion Field

The proposed mass flow possesses strict predictive power and is completely protected from the landscape-fitting problem of parameters by several independent experimental frontiers [1,3]:

1.
CMB Observables (Planck Satellite Data): The drift of the electron mass me(rc), tied to the dynamics of the event horizon, shifts the binding energy of hydrogen in the early Universe [1, 3]. This directly alters the moment of recombination, imposing strict constraints on the angular scale of the acoustic horizon of the cosmic microwave background, as detailed in conformal cosmological solutions [1, 3]. Any incorrect trajectory of the action S(rc,χ,T) will instantly ruin the CMB anisotropy spectrum, making the model easily falsifiable [1,3].
2.
The "Fifth Force" Problem and Local Tests in the IR Macro-World (MICROSCOPE Mission Data): The dependence of our effective DE/DM fields on macroscopic conditions is potentially capable of generating long-range "fifth forces" in local laboratories [1, 3]. To remain in absolute compatibility with the tight empirical bounds on the E"otv"os parameter (η ~ 10-15) achieved by the MICROSCOPE space mission [6], the joint scalar-torsion dynamics engages four parallel post-recombination QFT screening scenarios [1,3]:

  • A. Dynamical Freezing via Steep Potential Attractors: The cosmic potential V (χ,T) is structured such that after the recombination era (z ≲ 1000), the scalar-torsion fraction rapidly rolls down a steep gradient into a deeply localized minimum (⟨χ⟩→ χ0) [1,3]. By z ~ 10, the fields become completely static in the macro-world, freezing me at its standard laboratory value of 0.511 MeV and making the field quanta extremely heavy (mχ ≫eV), which suppresses the macroscopic force via a Yukawa cutoff [1,3].

  • B. Chameleon Density Mechanism: The built-in non-polynomial structure of Borissova’s Lagrangian allows for the activation of the Chameleon density screening mechanism discovered by Khoury and Weltman [2, 3]. Under conditions of high baryonic matter density (on Earth or in near-Earth orbit), the effective mass of our field increases sharply in proportion to the local density ρlocal [3]. The gigantic local mass completely deactivates spatial fluctuations of the scalar sector at short distances, and the macroscopic spin-neutrality of planetary bodies tightly blocks Palatini torsion, anchoring the electron mass at the constant 0.511 MeV [1,2].

  • C. Classical Vainshtein Mechanism (Vainshtein Kinetic Screening): A kinetic screening scenario where the suppression of the additional "fifth force" is ensured by non-linear kinetic terms containing higher field derivatives (typical form: (∂ϕ)2□ϕ) [3]. Near a massive mechanical source, the spatial field gradients rise sharply, forcing the non-linear kinetic operator to dominate over the linear one [3]. The kinetic energy of scalar-torsion fluctuations undergoes elastic amplification, which effectively drives the coupling constant of the field with baryonic matter to zero [3]. The vulnerability of the mechanism within standard EFTs, where quantum loops return the field mass to the Planck scale (10-33 cm), is fully cured in your Synthesis by Borissova’s UV regulator [2,3].

  • D. Newtonian Freezing Mechanism (Acceleration-Dependent Screening): A mathematically rigorous scenario for the complete quenching of scalar-torsion forces in the interplanetary vacuum of planetary systems [3]. This approach was covariantly formulated by S. Hossenfelder in the context of emergent gravity [3] and investigated in non-linear models of the K-mouflage class by A. Barreira, P. Brax, S. Clesse, B. Li, and P. Valageas [3]. In this scenario, the activation of the non-linear kinetic operator in the equations of motion is specified not by the gradients of the DE/DM field itself, but by the magnitude of the classical Newtonian acceleration of the background geometry aN (that is, the Newtonian potential of the Sun as a macro-source) [3]. Even though baryonic matter is practically absent within the interplanetary vacuum of the Solar System (rendering the Chameleon powerless) [3], the Newtonian acceleration created by the Sun at Earth’s orbit is huge and exceeds the critical infrared MOND threshold by many orders of magnitude (aN ≫ a0 ~ 10-10 m/s2) [11]. This strong background Newtonian field of the macrogeometry forcibly clamps the fluctuations of the emergent scalar-torsion field throughout the interplanetary volume of the planetary system, driving its coupling constant to zero and seamlessly restoring Einstein’s GR for terrestrial laboratories and spacecraft [3].

The universality of the conformal multiplier Ω(rc) requires that the masses of the entire SM particle zoo run strictly synchronously (Δm∕m = -ΔΩ∕Ω), which completely rules out the possibility of component-by-component manual tuning to match experiments [1,3]. Which exact one of these four independent alternative QFT scenarios is realized in our Universe and ensures a precise zero E"otv"os parameter must be determined during further numerical analysis [1,3].

Chapter 4
Part 19.4: Unified Clifford Geometrodynamics: Conformal Anomalies, Raju Holography, and BRST Burning of Six-Dimensional Superspace

Abstract. Within the framework of this work, a radical alternative to local quantum field theory is formulated. The central thesis of the work postulates that the fundamental rest masses and the spectrum of generations of Standard Model fermions are fundamentally impossible to calculate locally at the ultraviolet (UV) point. Instead, the mass spectrum is derived as a global holographic imprint of the Universe, functioning as a holistic six-dimensional quantum isotropic resonator. In the previous parts, we considered the possibility of quantizing gravity on a de Sitter background in 4D space. The main problem of such quantization is the need to introduce Standard Model particles from the outside. A mechanism for generating these particles was missing. This is due to the impossibility of generating fermions through geometrodynamics from fluctuations of the gravitational field, established as far back as Wheeler. However, the possibility of the spontaneous appearance of the Standard Model proves possible in a 6D space with three spatial and three temporal coordinates using Clifford algebra and octonion algebra. After the birth of the SM at the electroweak interaction scale, the 6D space splits into three independent sheets of 4D spaces. On these sheets, we can calculate the mass spectrum of the Standard Model using the Raju–Borissova method described in the previous parts. This document presents a complete, internally consistent mathematical model of quantum geometrodynamics, describing the evolution of the configuration superspace from the Planck scale to the four-dimensional physical brane. The model is based on the primacy of the Clifford geometric algebra Cℓ(3,3) and describes the dynamics of twelve isolated Z12-vacuums. The fixation of the nilpotent BRST charge at the electroweak scale is derived through finite-volume spatial factors and quantum decoherence with a macroscopic Dark Energy condensate through Dark Matter.

Within the framework of this preprint, a radical alternative to local quantum field theory is formulated. The central thesis of the work postulates that the fundamental rest masses and the spectrum of generations of Standard Model fermions are fundamentally impossible to calculate locally at the ultraviolet (UV) point. Instead, the mass spectrum is derived as a global holographic imprint of the Universe, functioning as a holistic six-dimensional quantum isotropic resonator.

In the previous parts, we considered the possibility of quantizing gravity on a de Sitter background in 4D space. The main problem of such quantization is the need to introduce Standard Model particles from the outside. A mechanism for generating these particles was missing. This is due to the impossibility of generating fermions through geometrodynamics from fluctuations of the gravitational field, established as far back as Wheeler. However, the possibility of the spontaneous appearance of the Standard Model proves possible in a 6D space with three spatial and three temporal coordinates using Clifford algebra and octonion algebra. After the birth of the SM at the electroweak interaction scale, the 6D space splits into three independent sheets of 4D spaces. On these sheets, we can calculate the mass spectrum of the Standard Model using the Raju–Borissova method described in the previous parts.

The geometric basis of the model is a continuous 6D continuum with signature (3+, 3-), where the three temporal coordinates t1,t2,t3 are absolutely equivalent and linked by the discrete cyclic group ℤ3. The full spectrum of three generations of matter is generated not through anisotropic geometric sheets, but through internal automorphisms of the split-octonion Clifford basis Cℓ3,3(ℝ) under the action of the split exceptional Lie group G2(2). Quantization of the system on a rigidly specified isotropic de Sitter background is carried out via the Feynman path integral. The fundamental regulator of the theory — the degree of influence of tachyonic and ghost trajectories — is automatically selected at the lower end of the renormalization cascade. This choice is dictated by global quantum decoherence induced by the isotropic tensor background of Dark Energy on the maximum cosmological Hubble horizon RH. The nature of Dark Matter is explained as a BRST-pure non-radiative condensate.

4.1 Octonion Basis, Phase Topology of Z12-Vacuums, and BRST Burning of 6D

The transition to a fundamental algebraic geometry allows for the elimination of external phenomenological adjustments of constants. All parameters of the system are invariantly derived from a single Clifford pro-space of the geometric algebra Cℓ(3, 3).

4.1.1 Planck Scale: Activation of G2(2) Symmetry

The Clifford algebra Cℓ(3, 3) is primary and present at all energy levels, defining a six-dimensional spacetime M6 with a neutral signature (3, 3). At the Planck scale (E ~ 1019 GeV), the extreme energy density activates the internal non-commutative octonion sector of the Clifford structure, transferring the geometry into the UV non-commutative region. The hidden non-associative geometry of octonions O at this stage is completely governed by the exceptional split form of the Lie group G2(2). This symmetry invariantly contains within itself the gauge groups of the Standard Model SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1), while the three generations of quarks and leptons are born as minimal spinor ideals of this structure.

As the energy drops, the octonion sector obeys the mechanism of topological IR screening. The decay profile of non-commutativity is described by a Gaussian decay function of the non-commutativity tensor θ(r):

θ(r) = θ0e-r2∕L2 		(4.1)

where θ0 is the maximum Planck value. Low-energy fields lose access to the octonion layer, transferring the dynamics to the rails of a strictly associative 6D geometry.


Figure 4.1: Phase space topology of 12 isolated Z12-vacuums and the Non-commutativity decay profile

Unlike classical string scenarios, the IR cutoff scale L is not tuned manually for laboratory tests. It is rigidly fixed by the holographic length uncertainty relation as the geometric mean between the ultraviolet (Planck) and infrared (Hubble) limits of the Universe:

L = Rh ⋅ LPl 		(4.2)

4.1.2 Phase Space Topology of 12 Isolated Z12-Vacuums

Inside the 6D spacetime M6, due to the presence of macro-time t1 and hidden time axes t2,t3, the causal structure is projected onto a 12-sector phase space. The gauge symmetry of the cyclic group Z12 divides the plane of temporal coordinates into clear isolated geometric quadrants separated by lightlike boundaries ds2 = 0:

  • Physical sector (ds2 > 0): Occupies the upper and lower causal cones. The upper quadrant under a positive macro-time vector (t1 > 0) corresponds to stable baryonic matter. The lower quadrant under time arrow inversion (t1 < 0) defines the region of CPT antimatter.

  • Tachyonic sectors (ds2 < 0): Occupy the side quadrants of the phase plane. The right sector forms the region of tachyons, the left — the region of antitachyons.

These 12 vacuums represent rigidly isolated sign charts, a smooth causal transition between which (ds2 > 0 → ds2 < 0) is forbidden, which excludes macroscopic chaos.

1.
Observable Chrono-Sectors (Worlds 1, 2, 3): Three macroscopic 4D sheets of regular Minkowski space, transforming into each other under a cyclic shift of the time axes. Each of these three worlds contains the full multiplet of the Standard Model — all three generations of fermions (including electrons, muons, tau leptons, and their quark counterparts) are present there simultaneously.
2.
Mirror CPT-Sectors (Antiworlds 1, 2, 3): Three regions of reversed time sign, mirror-compensating the baryonic charges and topological invariants of the corresponding Chrono-Sectors to a pure zero.
3.
Compensating Tachyonic Quadrants (8 External Worlds): Spacelike sectors of the phase space (ds2 < 0) lying outside the macroscopic light cones. These 8 worlds contain ghost and tachyonic trajectories that function as the opposing pan of the scales in the Feynman path integral, nullifying the cosmological constant at the Planck scale.

A fundamental feature of the developed geometrodynamics is the absolute gauge isolation of the vacuum subsectors. The gauge fields of the Standard Model are completely autonomous and initially do not possess cross-QFT interaction vertices with the non-radiative components of the vacuum. This, at all loop scales, forbids the emergence of kinetic field mixing operators of the form ϵFμν(1)F (2,3 Ehrenfest)μν ≡ 0, completely blocking the generation of an electric millicharge and ridding the effective field theory of gravitational chiral anomalies. Within this field configuration, Dark Matter is derived as a stable, collisionless, non-radiative condensate of BRST-invariant vacuum states of the WDW superspace of Our own spacetime. This condensate possesses an effective inertial rest mass, but is completely devoid of the gauge charges of the Standard Model. Its gravitational contribution to the effective Einstein equations at huge intergalactic distances is realized in a standard way through Our 4D gravitational channel, forming the observed astrophysical background of the distributed collisionless halo of the Dark Matter of the Universe.

4.1.3 Cosmological Implications

The model puts forward a strict falsifiable prediction, verifiable at the astrophysical scale:

  • Cosmological gravity at the horizon: At distances comparable to the Hubble radius RH, the vacuum de Sitter dark energy flow generates IR corrections to the Newtonian potential of Our sheet. The spatial scale L = ℓPl RH ≈ 50μm emerges within the BRST structure not as a threshold of gauge rearrangement, but exclusively as the fundamental wavelength of IR modulation (fluctuation cutoff) of Our own vacuum Dark Energy condensate, which manifests itself in the form of the isotropic cosmological acceleration of the Universe.

At the same time, the four-dimensional projections of spacelike separated fermion spin correlations on Our Minkowski Sheet in all energy regimes strictly and steadfastly obey the locally causal Tsirelson quantum bound (22 ≈ 2.82). Exceeding these boundaries is excluded, which guarantees the full conservative unitarity of the S-matrix and the absolute preservation of the macroscopic causality of the effective field theory.

4.1.4 Electroweak Scale: BRST Burning of 6D, Finite Volume, and Entanglement (Decoherence) with DE through DM

When the spatial scale of interaction crosses the fundamental mean scale LŻ = RH ⋅ ℓPl = 50μm (see Fig. 1, right graph), the entropy density of the macroscopic DM space reaches a critical threshold. The presence of hidden time axes t2,t3 and tachyonic sectors in the macro-world would lead to the appearance of quantum ghosts and the destruction of QFT unitarity. The phase transition of quantum geometry is localized at the critical spatial scale L = 50μm. At this mark, the Gaussian decay curve of the non-commutativity tensor θ(r) sharply rushes to zero (θ → 0), and the system transitions into the infrared Lorentz-invariant region. The scale L = 50μm sets the physical boundary of the media interface, forming a geometric finite volume of the microstructure within which the wave functions experience dimensional quantization. To definitively isolate the anomalies, strictly at the electroweak scale (ΛEW ≈ 246 GeV), a BRST mechanism for burning out extra dimensions is activated with the participation of the Dark Matter background, throwing information into the hidden Dark Energy (DE) sector with a corresponding growth in entropy. Dark Energy is described as a macroscopic coherent quantum condensate of de Sitter vacuum fluctuations distributed across all 12 vacuums. Upon crossing the 50μm threshold, the system enters into thermodynamic contact with DE through DM, which triggers a phase decoherence process:

1.
Outside the IR boundary, the macroscopic DM background performs instantaneous phase decoherence of the wave functional Ψ(gij,χ), destroying the superposition of the time axes.
2.
The density matrix of the mixed state loses its off-diagonal components linking the physical cones to the tachyonic sectors. The interference between the time axes is completely destroyed:

⟨t1|ρ^vac|t2, 3⟩→r ≥ 50μm0 		(4.3)
3.
The loss of coherence under the action of DM freezes the Faddeev–Popov ghost sector. The condition of strict nilpotency of the BRST charge QBRST2 = 0 becomes an exact identity, burning out the axes t2,t3. The state space reduces to the sector of zero-norm BRST cohomologies — a pure 4D brane of baryonic matter (t1 > 0) with signature (3, 1).

4.2 Formulation via the Feynman Path Integral: Integration Measure and Isotropic Balance

Translating the six-dimensional Wheeler–DeWitt equation into the language of the continuum path integral allows for the abandonment of Hadamard-incorrect differential problems in the ultrahyperbolic signature. Instead of searching for stationary solutions of the operator Ĥ6D, we transition to computing transition amplitudes via the functional Feynman integral:

Z = ∫ ∏ K = 112Dg MN(K)DΦ(K),ei∑ K=112S6D(g(K),Φ(K)) 		(4.4)

where the integration measure explicitly accounts for the contributions from all 12 symmetrical sectors (worlds) of the resonator Universe. The action S6D has the exact same functional form and the same coupling constants for each sector K = 1…12. To lock the non-physical tachyonic configurations from the outer quadrants at macroscales, we introduce a BRST-invariant Faddeev–Popov filter into the general integration measure, regulating the degree of mutual coherence of these 12 sectors:

DΦ →DΦ ⋅DcDcŻ ⋅ e-ξ∫ d6X-g,Lghosts(c,cŻ,g) 		(4.5)

The parameter ξ determines the degree of permeability of the BRST lock between the 12 worlds at different scales. Inside the path integral, the contributions from the "bad" tachyonic paths from the outer quadrants are ideally, sign for sign, canceled out by the determinants of the BRST ghosts in the numerator (DetDet = 1), completely cutting out their macroscopic manifestation. At the same time, at microscales, the intensity of this cancellation smoothly evolves synchronously throughout the entire system. Quantum particles in all sectors receive regulated freedom to make virtual "dives" along the axes t1,t2,t3, using the hidden volume of the split-octonion resonator as a dissipative medium. The entire system turns out to be self-regulating, where the balance of forces is adjusted without the slightest manual intervention due to the rigid constraints of the G2(2) group.

4.3 Automatic Tuning Mechanism: Quantum Decoherence on the Isotropic Background of Dark Energy

The fixed 6D de Sitter background embedded in the foundation of the model possesses a uniform Dark Energy density that forms a global cosmological Hubble horizon RH. To ensure complete isotropy, Dark Energy is specified by a ℤ3-invariant tensor uniformly distributed along the axes t1,t2,t3, while the Hubble horizon itself represents an ideal six-dimensional sphere. This isotropic ocean of Dark Energy works as an all-encompassing thermodynamic environment for any local quantum processes on any of the sheets. The interaction of fields with the Dark Energy environment inevitably launches the mechanism of environment-induced quantum decoherence (einselection according to Zurek). The efficiency of this decoherence is rigidly scaled by the interaction volume and generates an automatic tuning of the regulator ξ. In the IR limit, macroscopic gravitational waves (LIGO scale) possess a gigantic volume of entanglement with the Dark Energy of the Universe. Decoherence occurs instantaneously and synchronously in all directions, completely washing away the phase coherence of trajectories into hidden times. The parameter ξ automatically tends to infinity (ξ →∞). For macro-instruments, the vacuum seems absolutely Lorentz-invariant, gravity — strictly four-dimensional hμν, and the Tsirelson bound — unshakeable (22). In the UV limit, inside the local micro-resonator of an elementary particle, the decoherence of DE does not have time to trigger. The parameter ξ drops, opening access for particles to the hidden volume of the octonion basis. The mass spectrum of the Standard Model is not a set of random coupling constants, but the only stable pointer states capable of surviving on 4D slices and not blurring to zero under the influence of global Dark Energy decoherence. The true masses are fundamentally non-local, since each mass gap is an equilibrium point between the UV dynamics of the octonion hidden times and the IR pressure of the isotropic cosmological horizon of the Universe.

4.3.1 The Nature of Dark Matter as a BRST-Invariant Vacuum Condensate

Within the framework of the presented concept of the isotropic 6D resonator of the Universe, the phenomenon of Dark Matter (DM) is completely cleared of classical QFT contradictions (such as the stability of heavy fermions or the distortion of primordial nucleosynthesis). Dark Matter does not consist of hypothetical "dark particles" of the Standard Model locked on hidden sheets, but represents a non-radiative BRST-invariant vacuum condensate of the multidimensional phase space itself. The mathematical clarification of this phenomenon requires a strict separation of the perturbative (particle-like) and non-perturbative (condensate) degrees of freedom of the 12-sector resonator. By BRST-invariance of the vacuum condensate is meant the identity-like fulfillment of the supercharge nilpotency condition on the ground state of the system:

Q^BRST|vacuum⟩TM = 0 		(4.6)

This means that this vacuum background is formed exclusively by elements of the BRST cohomologies of the Clifford basis Cℓ3,3(ℝ). All the mathematical garbage associated with multi-time ghosts and tachyonic trajectories from the 8 external worlds is completely annihilated inside the condensate via the Faddeev–Popov determinants (DetDet = 1), which guarantees the absolute quantum stability of this vacuum phase and prevents its spontaneous decay. The concept of the "non-radiative" nature of the condensate captures its full gauge isolation. Since the global decoherence induced by Dark Energy at the cosmological horizon RH completely burns out the gauge cross-terms of photons between the sectors, the kinetic mixing parameter is strictly equal to zero (ϵ ≡ 0) at all loop scales. The DM condensate is devoid of quantum degrees of freedom capable of electromagnetic radiation or absorption, which ensures its 100The physical manifestation of the BRST-invariant DM condensate in Our world is realized exclusively through the universal Einstein gravitational channel hμν, sensitive to the full energy-momentum tensor of the isotropic 6D continuum. When the RG flow stops at the critical self-duality threshold L ≈ 50μm, this vacuum background of the split-octonion space focuses on Our 4D sheets in the form of a dynamic change in the vacuum elasticity geometry. This spatial "echo" of the global 12-sector resonator does not participate in baryonic interactions and early Universe nucleosynthesis, but creates a stable non-local gravitational potential that forms galaxy halos. Thus, Dark Energy and Dark Matter are complementary phases of a single system: Dark Energy represents the distributed isotropic IR background of the resonator at the scale RH, while Dark Matter is its focused BRST condensate at the self-duality threshold of 50 micrometers.

4.3.2 Automatic Vacuum Stabilization: Changing the Minkowski Background to Conformal de Sitter

The critical error of traditional QFT analysis, which postulates the instability of ultrahyperbolic spaces with signature (3+, 3-), lies in the implicit use of the flat, infinite Minkowski background as a vacuum basis. An attempt to calculate the mass gaps of elementary particles on a Minkowski background inevitably leads to UV loop divergences and an uncontrolled tachyonic breakthrough, destroying macroscopic causality. The model radically overcomes this impasse by performing quantization on an isotropic dynamic six-dimensional de Sitter background. The change of the vacuum background leads to an automatic, geometrically determined evaporation of all fundamental contradictions:

  • Geometric ghost containment: The tensor density of Dark Energy, immanent to the de Sitter background, induces an isotropic cosmological Hubble horizon RH. This boundary turns the infinite Minkowski space into a closed spherical 6D resonator. Virtual tachyonic trajectories no longer escape into infinite acceleration, but are forcibly fixed by the boundary conditions of the horizon, forming stable wave nodes inside the 12-sector phase space.

  • Self-regulated IR screening: The de Sitter background works as a global dissipative open system. Macroscopic quantum decoherence (einselection) generated by the full isotropic volume of Dark Energy identically destroys coherence between the 12 worlds in the IR limit. The BRST lock closes automatically with 100

Thus, mathematical anomalies that were fatal for flat Minkowski space transform on an isotropic de Sitter background into a natural, self-consistent regulator of the RG flow, stopping at the self-duality point L = RH ℓPl ≈ 50μm and forming the non-local mass spectrum of the Standard Model.

Part XX
Part 20. 6D Geometrodynamics of the Wheeler–DeWitt Equation: Passenger-Free Quantum Decoupling in Triplicated Spacetime (Cℓ3,3)

Abstract. A closed quantum-informational model of the Universe is proposed, describing cosmological dynamics as an emergent phenomenon within a stationary six-dimensional isotropic resonator with three cyclically symmetric time axes. Gauge structures, the spectrum of three generations of fermions, and the masses of elementary particles are derived as direct geometric projections of spinor automorphisms of the Clifford wave operator without introducing external material fields. The observed expansion of space is treated as a mechanical parting of elastic matter from the geometric center to a fixed de Sitter event horizon, where the scale factor determines the dynamic change in the rate of vacuum time. The semiclassical transition of the wave function of the Universe reduces to a Hamilton–Jacobi-type equation, where physical time is measured by the increment of the horizon area, and the dynamics is driven by a continuous disentanglement of matter and vacuum. The complete exclusion of an independent static cosmological constant transfers the system’s budget into the form of a self-consistent constraint, where the density of dark energy and dark matter arises emergently to compensate for the radial retreat of baryons and preserve the total invariant mass of the closed system. The calculation of the running coupling constants and the spectrum of discrete masses is implemented via the method of iterative quantum subtraction on adaptive resonators of a non-commutative torus. The total ultraviolet finiteness of the vacuum and the blocking of non-physical ghosts are ensured by a built-in non-linear Palatini damper that quenches the gravitational constant in strong fields, and by destructive interferential compensation of matter and antimatter loops at the quantum bounce point. In the infrared macroscopic limit, the octonion basis collapses into a Lorentz-covariant matrix representation with an elastic mass gap. The total mutual gauge isolation of the sheets excludes field kinetic mixing operators at all loop scales, blocking the generation of an electric charge for hidden particles. The fundamental spatial boundary of 50 μm is derived as a strict geometric mean logarithmic invariant between the Planck length and the horizon radius. At this IR threshold, due to the complete geometric dephasing of the wave function, the matter of parallel sheets completely loses electromagnetic coherence and is fixed by a local observer exclusively as a collisionless gravitating dark matter condensate whose spin currents form flat galaxy rotation curves. Quantum non-locality in EPR experiments is substantiated as a kinematic illusion of a four-dimensional projection during local signal propagation along hidden time axes.

Within the framework of this work, a radical alternative to local quantum field theory is formulated. The central thesis of the work postulates that the fundamental rest masses and the spectrum of generations of Standard Model fermions are fundamentally impossible to calculate locally at the ultraviolet (UV) point. Instead, the mass spectrum is derived as a global holographic imprint of the Universe, functioning as a holistic six-dimensional quantum isotropic resonator. In this work, a rigorous modification of quantum cosmology is proposed based on six-dimensional general relativity geometrodynamics in the (3+,3-) signature. The global 6D continuum is defined as a triplicated frame of the intersection of three independent, analytically continuous, and absolutely equal time axes obeying a strict discrete cyclic ℤ3 symmetry around a single, indivisible three-dimensional spatial basis x = (x,y,z). The Wheeler–DeWitt (WDW) equation is completely freed from external matter fields ("passengers"): the gauge structures of the Standard Model, 3 generations of fermions, and the masses of elementary particles are derived as direct geometric projections of local spinor automorphisms of the Clifford wave operator Cℓ3,3. The calculation of masses and coupling constants is implemented via the method of discrete adaptive Padmanabhan–Grosse–Wulkenhaar resonators clamped in the grip of a logarithmic renormalization group flow. At each step, the spectral problem of the WDW solution wave functions in a local window is solved from scratch, while the iterative bootstrap performs dynamic self-consistency of the vacuum energy with the cosmological constant Λ. The 50 μm boundary is derived as a strict geometric mean logarithmic invariant determining the threshold for the emergence of macroscopic phonon dephasing with respect to any of the observation axes and the birth of Dark Matter from parallel sheets.

4.1 Architecture of the Triplicated Universe, Gauge Nature, and Connection with Banks–Fischler Theory

We start with a clean geometric canvas — a six-dimensional pseudo-Euclidean spacetime of general relativity with a metric ηMN = diag(+1, +1, +1,-1,-1,-1) of (3+, 3-) signature. The real Clifford algebra Cℓ3,3(ℝ) associated with this manifold is isomorphic to the algebra of real 8 × 8 matrices (Mat(8, ℝ)) and remains strictly associative at all energy scales.

The presence of three temporal coordinates (∂t12,∂ t22,∂ t32) in standard differential equations of the ultrahyperbolic type classically generates a Hadamard instability. It manifests as an avalanche-like, exponential runaway of high-frequency ultraviolet (UV) modes of the hidden sector, instantly blowing up the vacuum when attempting to solve the equation as an evolutionary Cauchy problem. However, in the developed geometrodynamics of the Wheeler–DeWitt (WDW) equation, the Hadamard catastrophe is completely blocked by the dynamic structure of the continuum itself and physically does not affect macroscopic measurable quantities in the IR range:

  • Holographic Finiteness of de Sitter Space: As rigorously proven in the 2026 works of T. Banks and W. Fischler  [1, 2], quantum gravity in de Sitter space is fundamentally described by a finite-dimensional quantum system with a fixed, macroscopically huge, but strictly finite number of states (qubits) N = eSdS. In such a system, infinite UV momentum tails that fuel the Hadamard instability are mathematically absent.

  • UV/IR Mirror Symmetry (T-Duality): At the Planck scale (lPl ~ 10-35 m), the renormalization group flow encounters an invariant Padmanabhan phase-space regulator possessing the property of rigid invariance Re[Rk(p2)] = R k(MPl4∕p2). Any attempt by UV modes to go into infinite runaway (p →∞) is mirror-mapped by the regulator into the safe infrared (IR) region of large distances. The deep UV is physically isolated, impenetrable, and cannot destabilize the effective macroscopic theory.

  • Stationary Painlevé–Gullstrand Flow: The kinematic source of WDW non-linearity is the vacuum influx of Dark Energy specified by the off-diagonal components of the de Sitter metric  [5, 6]. This flow performs an elliptic reduction of the hidden wave operator on compact phase sections. Instead of an incorrect evolution problem, the system transitions into a strictly stable quantum Sturm–Liouville problem to find the real and positively defined eigenvalues of the Standard Model masses.


Figure 4.2: Topology of the phase space of 12 isolated Z12-vacuums and the non-commutativity decay profile

Within the framework of this preprint, a radical alternative to local quantum field theory is formulated. The central thesis of the work postulates that the fundamental rest masses and the spectrum of generations of Standard Model fermions are fundamentally impossible to calculate locally at the ultraviolet (UV) point. Instead, the mass spectrum is derived as a global holographic imprint of the Universe, functioning as a holistic six-dimensional quantum isotropic resonator.

Direct Connection with the Works of Banks and Fischler: Our 6D model represents an attempt at a perturbative development and physical investigation of the paradoxes outlined in their 2026 works  [1,2]. Banks and Fischler prove that due to the finite-dimensional nature of the Hilbert space, a local IR observer fundamentally faces a "theoretical ambiguity" (ambiguity) — the impossibility from within the de Sitter "box" to uniquely fix the Hamiltonian and the UV microstates hidden behind the cosmological horizon. Since the deep UV is physically isolated, and precise UV measurements are fundamentally unattainable due to the limitation of energy by the Hubble volume, this fundamental ambiguity forces us to look for the mass spectrum of the Standard Model exclusively through the properties of the macroscopic resonator of the Universe and the cosmological constant Λ. The developed logarithmic cascade is designed to test whether the requirement of quantum self-consistency of the SM vacuum loops in a finite volume can pull this "theory space" toward selected values; however, the question of whether the final answer will be strictly unique or if de Sitter admits a family of IR solutions remains open at this stage.

The global 6D continuum is defined as a triplicated structure of the intersection of three orthogonal, analytically continuous four-dimensional spacetimes on the interval (-∞, +∞), expanded around a single, indivisible three-dimensional spatial basis x = (x,y,z). The division of the 6D metric into three Minkowski sheets is specified by the matrix representation of the projectors:

Pi = 1 + Γi 2 ,(i = 1, 2, 3)(1) 		(4.1)

where Γi are the generating elements of the real Clifford algebra Cℓ3,3(ℝ). The projectors fix three temporal coordinate axes t1,t2,t3, defining three geometrically equivalent, cyclically intertwined sheets: Sheet 1 (t1,x), Sheet 2 (t2,x), and Sheet 3 (t3,x). Between all three sheets and temporal directions, a strict discrete ℤ3 symmetry is executed (full equality of worlds): none of the temporal dimensions is prominent or macroscopically dominant. Each of the three local observers tied to their time axis ti perceives their time as a macroscopic evolutionary coordinate, while the two other orthogonal times manifest for them as quantum phase directions of the fields inside the common 3D spatial framework. At the core of the proposed geometrodynamics lies the hypothesis that the spacetime structure is induced by the non-associative real algebra of split-octonions O  [8]. The automorphism group of split-octonions G2(2), due to the internal triality of the spinor sector in the UV limit, predetermines the uncovering of strictly 12 fundamental gauge degrees of freedom of the Standard Model (SU(3)c × SU(2)L × U(1)Y ), which completely eliminates the redundant gauge volume and satisfies the constraints of the Coleman–Mandula theorem. The global time reversal operator has the form T^ = T^1T^2T^3. Imposing the condition of global CPT invariance on the full state vector of the Universe in the spirit of the cosmological approach of Boyle and Turok  [9] means that the quantum bounce point (ti = 0) is an invariant geometric mirror. The combinatorics of the causal structure unfolds into a 12-sector topological matrix of phase space defined by multi-time cones: positive time-like sectors (ds2 > 0,t i > 0) of baryonic matter, negative CPT-mirror sectors (ds2 > 0,t i < 0) of the Antiworld, as well as external space-like quadrants of the right (ds2 < 0) and left cones of real tachyonic modes. Causal filtration and stability of wave solutions in external space-like quadrants (ds2 < 0) are mathematically provided by the covariant QFT apparatus of Rembieli’nski  [7]. The preferred frame (preferred frame) induced by the global background of dark energy sets the absolute synchronization of the continuum time. This completely preserves the macroscopic Lorentz covariance of the observed sectors, but excludes the emergence of closed time-like curves and stabilizes the vacuum, transferring tachyonic modes into a stable finite representation  [7]. This apparatus of introducing a preferred space-like hyperplane of absolute simultaneity not only suppresses the Klein–Gordon causal loops that arise during virtual tachyonic fluctuations, but also solves the fundamental problem of reducing the non-associative algebraic structure  [7]. Fixing the absolute time vector nM is equivalent to singling out the imaginary unit in the split-octonion basis Os, which forcibly restricts the exceptional automorphism group G2(2) to its maximal associative subgroup SU(3)c. Introducing the plane of absolute simultaneity projects the quantum states of the field strictly onto the isotropic cones of zero divisors Os, where the non-associative associators (a,b,c) = (a ⋅ b) ⋅ c - a ⋅ (b ⋅ c) are identically annihilated. Thus, the Rembieli’nski causal filter algebraically induces the transition of the non-associative structure Os into a strictly associative matrix representation Mat(8, ℝ), isomorphic to the real Clifford algebra Cℓ3, 3(ℝ) at all energy scales below the Planck scale. Fixing the absolute time vector nM is equivalent to singling out the imaginary unit e7 (e72 = +1) in the 8-dimensional split-octonion basis Os. Quantum fields in the developed passenger-free model are defined not as elements of the algebra itself, but as linear operators (endomorphisms) over this state space. The full space of endomorphisms End(Os) has a dimension of strictly 8 × 8 = 64. According to Albert’s fundamental theorem, the full endomorphism algebra End(Os) is identically isomorphic to the real Clifford algebra Cℓ3, 3(ℝ) and the matrix ring Mat(8, ℝ). At the same time, the Rembieli’nski causal filter projects the wave functions strictly onto the 4-dimensional maximal totally isotropic sub-spaces (light sub-spaces of zero divisors) Os, which algebraically form the minimal left ideals of the Clifford algebra Cℓ3, 3(ℝ). On these minimal left ideals, the operator action End(Os) becomes strictly associative, since the operators forcibly translate the elements of the ideal only inside the ideal itself, identically zeroing out non-associative associators. While the stable color sub-space of the octonions themselves retains the gauge symmetry SU(3)c, the full Clifford operator structure End(Os), due to the induced complex structure of left multiplication Le7, covariantly unfolds the full gauge volume of the Standard Model SU(3)c × SU(2)L × U(1)Y , completely eliminating the declarativeness of the transition. Introducing local anisotropy of causal filtration at the Planck scale does not violate the macroscopic causality of the effective theory, since in the IR limit, the background fluctuations of dark energy average the tensor projections, restoring Lorentz covariance. The number of fermion generations (three) is strictly determined by the non-associative structure of the real split-octonion algebra O and the property of its triality  [8].

4.2 Geometrodynamics of the Wheeler–DeWitt Equation in Painlev’e–Gullstrand Coordinates

Starting from the vacuum Einstein–Hilbert action for a six-dimensional pseudo-Riemannian manifold, the stationary vacuum Wheeler–DeWitt equation is written as:

ĤWDW|Ψ6D⟩ = (GMNKL ∂2 ∂gMN∂gKL --g,(6)R)|Ψ6D⟩ = 0(2) 		(4.2)

where GMNKL is the DeWitt supermetric in the (3+, 3-) signature. The kinematic source of WDW non-linearity is the accelerating vacuum influx of de Sitter Dark Energy, described by off-diagonal components of the metric in stationary Anti-Painlev’e–Gullstrand (APG) coordinates  [5,6]. To ensure the identical annihilation of cross time components of the inverse metric (gtitj ≡ 0 for i≠j) and preserve the isotropy of the ℤ3-frame, the invariant interval of the M(3, 3) continuum with a single spatial basis x is formulated through one common perfect square of the expanding flow:

ds2 = - (dt 12 + dt 22 + dt 32) + (dr + Hr(dt1 + dt2 + dt3)) 2 + r2dΩ 22 		(4.3)
where r = |x| = x2 + y2 + z2 is the radial coordinate of the single 3D space, and dΩ22 = dθ2 + sin ⁡ 2θdϕ2 is the metric of the two-dimensional sphere. Expanding the perfect square in the expression of interval (3) leads to the emergence of cross time terms with a positive sign, induced by the topological capacity of the resonator:

ds2 = - (1 - H2r2)(dt 12 + dt 22 + dt 32) + 2H2r2(dt 1dt2 + dt2dt3 + dt3dt1) + 2Hr(dt1 + dt2 + dt3)dr + dr2 + r2dΩ 22 		(4.4)
By direct matrix inversion of the covariant metric tensor gMN, the contravariant basis of the inverse metric is found. Due to the internal symmetry of the common perfect square, the off-diagonal time elements are identically annihilated (gtitj ≡ 0, i≠j), the diagonal time components take the normalized value gtiti = -1, and the radial element of the contravariant metric is strictly equal to grr = 1 - 3H2r2. The Laplace–Beltrami wave operator □6D = 1 -g∂M(-ggMN∂ N) in this geometry unfolds into a strictly curved structure:

□6D = -∑ i=13 ∂2 ∂ti2 + (1 - 3H2r2) ∂2 ∂r2 + 2(1 - 3H2r2) r ∂ ∂r + 1 r2Δθ,ϕ + 2Hr∑ i=13 ∂2 ∂ti∂r(5) 		(4.5)

The term + 2Hr∑ ⁡ i=13 ∂2 ∂ti∂r describes the off-diagonal vacuum influx, acting as an effective three-form gauge field of string theory  [8]. When the renormalization group flow reaches the Planck boundary lPl ~ 10-35 m, the radial scales of the orthogonal sheets bump into the threshold of mirror symmetry of string radii R → 1∕R. The passage of a single vacuum field through this spatial barrier leads to a first-order phase transition. At this critical point, as the spatial interval tends to zero (Δx → 0), the field configurations go beyond the boundaries of stable minimal left ideals. The internal operator structure of the Clifford basis Mat(8, ℝ) degenerates, exposing the underlying non-associative 8-dimensional structure of split-octonions Os. The Laplace–Beltrami wave operator unfolds into a true non-associative three-commutator of the operator algebra through its structural constants fabc d and the three-form H123 ~ H3r induced by the external differentiation of the APG metric potential  [8]:

(Xa,Xb,Xc) = ∑ dfabc dX d ~ ℏ2H3rγ abc(6) 		(4.6)

where γabc is the antisymmetric structure tensor of the split-octonion basis. This non-associativity strictly at the Planck scale works as a rigid geometric filter of degrees of freedom: the escape of fields onto the MTIP boundaries of zero divisors restricts the spectrum of allowed localized spinor solutions strictly to three generations of fermions transforming under the fundamental representations of the automorphism group. Translating the six-dimensional Wheeler–DeWitt equation into the language of the continuum path integral allows for the complete abandonment of Hadamard-incorrect differential problems in the ultrahyperbolic signature. Instead of searching for stationary solutions of the operator Ĥ6D, we transition to computing transition amplitudes via the functional Feynman integral:

Z = ∫ ∏ K = 112Dg MN(K)DΦ(K)ei∑ K=112S6D(g(K),Φ(K))(7) 		(4.7)
